## DESIGN DECISIONS (54th SCT6-cycle additions — 2026-06-12T05-19)

**FI-SCT6-54-A [FEEL — CRITICAL — 14th CONSECUTIVE] CW near-zero in shardcaller-only formation:**
14th consecutive SCT6 cycle with 1 or fewer CW charges fired (this run: 1 of 9). Root cause: MQ-12 hold rule means SC-mid and SC-low almost always see their target ally (SC-high) has already acted. TR/CW bonus-action conflict fires nearly every round for any SC that gets pressed in melee (all 3 in a no-melee-ally formation). These two rules together make CW structurally dead for 2 of 3 SCs in a shardcaller-only slice. **Recommendation (either):** (a) MQ-12 look-forward exception: SC-mid may CW SC-high if SC-high has not yet used a bonus action this round — reduces wasted charges; (b) 3-SC stagger variant: SC-mid CWs SC-low (same-round benefit since SC-low acts after SC-mid) and SC-low holds per MQ-12 — partially surfaces CW. Neither changes balance; the effect (advantage on 1 attack) is already weak vs the party's save-or-damage kit.

**FI-SCT6-54-B [FEEL — DESIGN] Solo-SC endgame: all social mechanics disabled:**
From R4 onward (SC-C alone), CW has no valid ally target and PTV has no triggering ally hits. Solo SC becomes pure 2-throw ranged DPS + TR repositioning — flat but mechanically correct. Optional solo rule for consideration: "if the last surviving shardcaller, CW may target itself (advantage on own next attack roll, reflecting the voice marking its own intent)." Cost: trivial. Benefit: one interesting resource-spending decision per endgame. Flag for human; do not auto-implement.

**MQ-SCT6-54-A [MECH QUESTION — 14th CARRY] PTV ally-definition, solo-SC state:**
"When an ally within 30 ft hits" — definitively ambiguous when solo. Simulation applied PTV from SC's own hits (SIM-SCT6-54-A: R4 Barrage, Sabriel incorrectly on disadv). Proposed ruling text for the .md: "When ANY OTHER shardcaller within 30 ft lands a hit, this SC's voice marks the target's weakness — that target has disadvantage on its next saving throw before end of its next turn. A solo surviving shardcaller cannot self-trigger PTV." This closes the ambiguity and fixes the simulation.

**MQ-SCT6-54-B [MECH QUESTION — 14th CARRY from MQ-SCT6-52-A] Barrage stagger reassignment:**
Designating SC-low as the R1 Barrage SC works per stagger rules, but SC-low is the most likely first-kill target when Marwen opens with Magic Missile or Fireball (priority: lowest-HP or visible-threat). If SC-low dies before acting, R1 Barrage is wasted. Prior SCT6 runs (52, 53) confirm this pattern. **Recommend testing:** reassign R1 Barrage to SC-high (highest init). SC-high acts first, Barrage fires before PCs have moved optimally, and SC-high is less likely to be focus-killed first. Downside: SC-high CW is then wasted (can't CW the Barrage SC). Given CW is near-zero anyway (FI-SCT6-54-A), this tradeoff is acceptable.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (51st SR5-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T20-18)

**FI-SR5-51-A [FEEL — CRITICAL — 11th CONSECUTIVE] Retire solo-rager-rush slice:**
11th consecutive SR5 cycle. Taunt correctly skipped 16× this run (all per the FI-SR5-46-A tactics annotation). The tactic note is functioning. But the slice has now been confirmed for 11 cycles straight as providing zero Taunt test coverage — Taunt is structurally moot in a same-type formation where all targets are ragers. **The FI-SR5-50-A CRITICAL retire flag has not been acted on. This is now 11 consecutive cycles of diminishing returns from SR5.** Recommendation: replace slice #5 in `thrulm-rotation.json` with a mixed-formation slice, e.g. "rager-shardcaller-duo" (1× derro-rager + 2× derro-shardcaller): exercises Taunt vs shardcaller (non-rager targets), Call Weakness pairing with Taunt, and a rager/shardcaller coordination scenario that has never been tested in a dedicated SR5-class slice. The existing tank-wall slice (rager + shardcaller) covers this but only a 1:1 ratio; the new slice would bias it shardcaller-heavy to stress Call Weakness stack behavior.

**FI-SR5-51-B [FEEL — VARIANCE] Berserk R1 all-miss (0/9 attacks hit):**
All 3 ragers used Berserk in R1 (correct: 2+ PCs in reach after charge). Combined 9 attack rolls, 0 hits vs AC 18/15/19. Berserk base to-hit +4 gives ~30–47% per swing vs the party; a 0/9 run has probability ~0.5% (binomial tail). This is bad luck with seed 494780, not a balance issue. The fight still resolved as expected (TPK R7). No action needed.

**MQ-SR5-51-A [MECH QUESTION — recurring] Local RNG mock, 114th consecutive external block:**
The `roll_combat_action` module requires either quantumnumbers API or random.org to be reachable. Both have been blocked in this cloud sandbox for 114 consecutive fires. The mock (pre-populating `_dr._number_cache` with seeded `random.Random`) works correctly but adds 15 lines of setup boilerplate to each playtest fire. **Recommendation for the human:** add a `USE_LOCAL_RNG=1` env var path in `dnd_roller.py` that seeds `random.Random(int(os.environ.get('RNG_SEED', time.time())))` as the third-tier fallback instead of raising `RuntimeError`. This would make the module sandbox-safe without touching the quantum/random.org paths.

**MQ-SR5-51-B [MECH QUESTION — structural] Berserk DB 3-line cap, 1-PC late-game scenario:**
When fewer than 3 PCs are alive, the DB Berserk output still shows 3 attack lines. The DM/sim must discard excess lines. In R6 this run, only Sabriel remained alive but Rager-C had Berserk recharged — correctly fell back to Multiattack per 2-enemy check. If the check had not fired, a single-target Berserk would have generated 2 wasted output lines. Acceptable for now. Track if this causes real-table confusion in future BEL3 or final-confrontation slices where ragers coexist with beholder.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (50th FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T19-26)

**FI-FC50-A [FEEL — STRUCTURAL CARRY] Beholder 0-damage — 50th FC cycle:**
Beholder took 0 damage this run. Sabriel's longsword hit STD-A (15 dmg), Bazgar missed STD-A twice, no PC targeted the beholder. Root cause is unchanged from prior cycles: Fireball forces attention to the thrall cluster, STDs are closer melee targets than the hovering beholder, and the beholder's multiattack eliminates Marwen before she can rotate onto the beholder. The party's realistic boss-damage window is zero when Marwen dies R1 and Bazgar's greataxe is halved by resistance. **This is the 50th cycle; carries from FI-FC48-F/FI-FC49-B. Not a balance bug (the fight is meant to overwhelm), but the boss-feels-untouchable read is a dramatic concern. DM note candidate: explicitly position the beholder in melee range of Bazgar from initiative start (not 60+ ft away behind derro), so Bazgar has a valid melee target before engaging STDs.**

**FI-FC50-C [FEEL — DESIGN VARIANCE] DD whiff (Sabriel nat-20 save) burns 3 LA:**
Sabriel rolled nat-20 (+3 CHA = 23 vs DC 16) on DD. Beholder spent 3 LA for zero effect. After the whiff, beholder had 0 LA for R1 remainder — but VS fired R2 and ended the fight anyway, so the whiff did not change the outcome. The variance (65% fail / 35% save) is acceptable. When DD fires and lands it strips smites; when it misses, the beholder loses its LA budget. Both outcomes feel real and high-stakes. **No change recommended. The variance is the point.**

**FI-FC50-D [FEEL] Marwen-first Fireball → Maw death trade:**
Marwen (init 22) fired Fireball before beholder, then beholder's Maw (autocrit 28pierce) killed her immediately. This "Fireball → die" trade is the most common Marwen R1 trajectory across FC cycles. Fireball is worth the trade (kills 2 thralls, chips 4 NPCs), but DM should narrate Marwen staying at 150+ ft to avoid Tentacle Lash reach — in practice the sim places her in range automatically. Encounter terrain note: back wall is 150 ft from altar; Marwen should START at the threshold, not advancing to melee range.

**MQ-FC50-A [MECH QUESTION] DD redirect to Marwen after Sabriel saves:**
DD text allows targeting "one creature within 30 feet that has spell slots, divine favor, or clerical powers." Marwen has L2 slots remaining after Fireball. After a Sabriel save, the beholder could redirect DD to Marwen in R2+ — stripping Shatter/Misty Step slots. Recommend adding a tactics note: "If Sabriel saves DD in R1, redirect DD to Marwen (spell slots) in R2 if she has L2+ remaining." Low priority — VS tends to end the fight before R2 DD fires.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (51st FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-12T03-19)

**FI-FC51-A [FEEL — DESIGN DECISION] Marwen structural pre-emption by beholder in final-confrontation:**
Across 51 FC cycles, the dominant death pathway for Marwen is beholder-grapple + Maw-autocrit *before she acts* in R1. This fires whenever beholder initiative > Marwen initiative. Beholder rolls d20+3; Marwen rolls d20+2. Probability of beholder pre-empting Marwen: ≈ 53% (roughly when beholder rolls higher, which with +1 net advantage is slightly above 50%). In this run, beholder rolled 16, Marwen rolled 13. Marwen never cast a single spell in the entire fight. This is the intended feel (beholder hunts casters first) but has a significant gameplay problem: Marwen's primary contribution (Fireball, 8d6 AoE) often never deploys against the full-confrontation minion swarm. **Three options for human decision:** (a) Accept — beholder as caster-killer is thematically correct; (b) Reduce beholder Dex from +3 to +1 to shift pre-emption probability to ≈40%; (c) Give Marwen a defensive reaction (e.g., Counterspell-as-free-action) that lets her interrupt before the Maw hits. Do not auto-fix.

**FI-FC51-C [FEEL — RETIRE CANDIDATE] Thrall-derro zero-pressure confirmed 51st consecutive FC cycle:**
4 TDs delivered ~3 hits and ~9 total damage across 3 rounds vs party max HP 125. At +3 to hit vs AC 15–19, individual TDs are 15–35% hit rate each attack. Across 51 FC runs, TDs have never delivered more than ~15% of total party damage. They function as target saturation and LoH/action-economy drain, not damage dealers. **STRONG RETIRE CANDIDATE:** Replace 2 of 4 TDs with a new mechanic that uses their Compelled Movement ability — e.g., beholder directs them into the path of AoE spells as living shields (force the party to harm them or be blocked). Alternatively reduce to 2 TDs total and use the freed design space for something more distinct. Do not auto-fix.

**MQ-FC51-A [MECH QUESTION — MATERIAL] DD save rate vs level-5 paladin: Aura of Protection does NOT apply:**
Aura of Protection (PHB Paladin, Level 6) is not available to Sabriel at level 5. Prior sims have incorrectly applied +Cha modifier twice (Cha save + Aura) to DD saves, making DD appear ~45% success rate. Corrected (no Aura): d20 + Cha mod vs DC 16. If Sabriel has Cha +2: needs 14+ = 40% save = **60% DD success rate.** If Cha +3: needs 13+ = 40% save = **60% DD success rate.** DD is considerably more reliable than previously tracked. In this run, the simmed save of 18 (d20=12 + incorrectly applied +6) should have been 15 (d20=12 + correct +3) → DD succeeds, Sabriel loses L2 slot, beholder +4 THP. **Recommendation:** confirm Sabriel's Cha score in `world/party/the-compass-edge/combat-roster.yml`; correct future sim DD save formula to `d20 + Cha_mod` (no Aura for level 5); update prior cycle DD stats accordingly.

**MQ-FC51-B [MECH QUESTION] Disintegration Ray target priority: Marwen (AC 15) optimal over Sabriel (AC 19):**
DR has +6 to hit. Hit rates: vs Marwen AC 15 → 65%; vs Sabriel AC 19 → 35%; vs Bazgar AC 18 → 40%. This run, DR was aimed at Sabriel (standing, conscious) per existing guidance ("prefer conscious standing targets") and missed (roll 9). The existing DR targeting note (FIX-EV7-30-A) says "avoid downed targets" but does not rank by AC. **Recommendation:** Add one line to DR targeting guidance in beholder-thrulm.md: "Prefer lower-AC targets for DR — Marwen (AC 15, 65% hit) is the priority target over Sabriel (AC 19, 35%) or Bazgar (AC 18, 40%). Only target Sabriel or Bazgar if Marwen is down."

## DESIGN DECISIONS (10th BEL3-cycle additions — 2026-06-12T02-26)

**FI-BEL3-10-A [FEEL — POSITIVE] PARTY VICTORY R3 — first clean BEL3 win in 10 cycles:**
The party defeated the beholder in 3 rounds. This is the first time the party has won slice #3 across 10 playtests. Key factors: (1) Fireball hit beholder for full damage (failed Dex save, nat 2 = 8 vs DC14); (2) Sabriel's double smite both landed R1 (22,22 vs AC17, total 40 dmg radiant); (3) beholder's R3 multiattack went cold (raw d20 rolls 7, 2, 1 — all three attacks miss vs AC17/18/19); (4) Bazgar landed 2 hits from 25 HP to close out (CRIT 21 + hit 12). Final burst totals R1: 40 (smites) + 23 (Bazgar crit) + 26 (Fireball) = 89 gross, 85 net after DD THP → beholder at 25/110 after R1. This establishes the "variance floor" for this slice: ~12-15% probability (both smites hit + Fireball full + beholder cold R3). Not a balance problem — the beholder is CR13 and this required multiple high-rolls aligning — but good to have a confirmed win in the record. Recommendation: no balance change. Note as "party CAN win this slice under favorable variance."

**FI-BEL3-10-B [FEEL] DD fires post-Sabriel but smites already spent — temporal ordering note:**
Drain Divinity fired correctly post-Sabriel per R1 protocol, stripping her final smite slot (CHA save 9 < DC16). However, Sabriel had already expended 2 smites attacking the beholder earlier in the same turn. DD stripped the *remaining* slot, not all slots. This is RAW-correct (DD fires after Sabriel's turn ends), but the tactical value was diminished: stripped 1 remaining slot rather than 3. DD is most impactful when Sabriel has not yet acted (beholder needs lower initiative than Sabriel to hold all LAs). Recommend adding to the beholder DD tactics note: "DD post-Sabriel-turn strips only unconsumed slots — Sabriel may have already expended smites on her turn. Still valuable to deplete R2+ smites. Maximum value when Sabriel has not yet acted (requires beholder lower initiative than Sabriel)."

**FI-BEL3-10-C [FEEL] Manifest Thralls lair with single surviving TD vs AC18+ — switch to UG:**
In R2 and R3, lair used Manifest Thralls with only TD-C alive (9 HP, +3 to hit). Both reaction attacks missed Bazgar (AC18): rolls 9+3=12 and 13+3=16. The existing note says "switch to UG if thralls land 0 hits on AC18+ targets" — but this check is post-facto (you can't know before the attack whether it will hit). Recommend a pre-emptive rule: **"If ≤1 thrall is alive AND primary standing melee targets are AC18+, prefer Unstable Ground over Manifest Thralls."** TD-C at +3 has only ~10-15% hit rate vs AC18/19; UG (DC16 DEX save) has ~40-50% pressure rate vs a fighter with +2 DEX. The UG trade is clearly better. Add this threshold to the Manifest Thralls DM note in beholder-thrulm.md.

**MQ-BEL3-10-A [MECH QUESTION — carry — 120th consecutive] VoidFeeding DB gap:**
Same as MQ-BEL3-9-A (9th consecutive BEL3 carry). DB rolls don't include +1 VF bonus. Not fight-decisive this run (TL2 already missed by 11; VF couldn't save the cold R3 multiattack), but carry pending human decision.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (9th BEL3-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T18-28)

**FI-BEL3-9-A [FEEL — POSITIVE] First non-Maw kill in BEL3 history:**
T1 thrall dagger (to-hit 22 → 6 piercing) dealt the finishing blow to Sabriel in R3. This is the first BEL3 cycle across 9 runs where any PC was not killed by Maw-autocrit. The beholder's Maw missed Sabriel at AC19 (to-hit 16 vs DC19; base roll ~9 + 6 bonus + 1 VF = 16), and the thrall's concentrated dagger attack finished her after double TL hits brought her to 3 HP. **Positive signal: the Maw's all-dominance pattern is breaking under natural variance. The thrall delivering the final blow reads dramatically well — the void-creature grapples and batters, the thrall finishes. This is the intended feel of the escort slice.** No action needed.

**FI-BEL3-9-B [FEEL — POSITIVE] Drain Divinity fired for 2nd time in BEL3:**
DD fired in R1 (Sabriel Cha save 12 < DC16, FAIL; L2 slot stripped). Root cause: beholder rolled init 8 (below Bazgar's 20). Bazgar's LA consumed only 1pt on a legendary tentacle lash that missed; the pool was still 2pts when Sabriel acted. Sabriel's end-of-turn LA with 3pts remaining → DD fired. **Confirms FI-BEL3-7-B analysis: DD fires when beholder has low initiative AND Bazgar doesn't consume the full LA pool.** Cumulative DD rate: 2/9 cycles (22%), slightly above theoretical 16%. Carry. No action needed.

**MQ-BEL3-9-A [MECH QUESTION — URGENT — carry from MQ-BEL3-8-A, 9th consecutive cycle] Void Feeding +1 to-hit/+1 dmg absent from DB spec — fight-decisive this run:**
TL1 vs Sabriel AC19 in R3 was to-hit 18+1(VF)=19 → hit. Without VF it's 18 < 19 → miss. If TL1 misses, Sabriel takes only T3 thrall hit (5 pierce) in R3 and the beholder's Maw also misses — Sabriel survives R3 at 20 HP, ungrappled. Fight extends to R4. VF was fight-ending. **9th consecutive cycle where VF materially affected the fight outcome. Human decision urgently needed:** (a) Add +1 to all beholder `to_hit_bonus` and `damage_modifier` values in DB (permanent until confirmed out-of-shrine-chamber), or (b) Add a DM callout in COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET: "+1 to ALL beholder attack rolls and damage rolls while in the shrine chamber — apply to EVERY to-hit and damage result shown by the roller." Option (b) is non-breaking. Option (a) is permanent and more robust.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (43rd SW1-cycle additions — 2026-06-12T00-20)

**FI-SW1-43-A [FEEL — CRITICAL CARRY — 43rd CONSECUTIVE] UF structurally unreachable in shrine-wedge:**
43rd consecutive SW1 cycle. Unstable Form advantage-on-attacks has never fired once against this party. Root cause confirmed this run: both STDs hit ≤20 HP at R2 entry (STD-A: 20/45 end-R1; STD-B: 16/45 end-R1). LOW HP override takes priority and redirects to AR-reckless or plain multiattack. Even when UF is active AND multiattack is used (STD-B R2, HP 16), the advantage (max of two d20s) produced rolls of 7 and 16 against AC 18 — both still missed at +4. **The UF window requires: STD ≥20 HP AND ≥10 dmg taken prior round AND AR unavailable or held AND melee target reachable. This window is never achieved against a L5 party where Shatter/smite can push both STDs below 20 HP in a single round.** Three options for human review: (1) Lower ≤20 HP threshold to ≤10 HP, creating a 10-20 HP band where UF can fire before LOW HP override kicks in; (2) Change UF trigger threshold from 10+ to 8+ damage, making it easier to activate at lower HP; (3) Rename/remove UF and replace with a simpler bonus — e.g. flat +1 to-hit while bloodied — to reduce cognitive load without losing the flavor.

**FI-SW1-43-C [FEEL — POSITIVE — NEW] OBR self-damage accelerates STD death and adds table feel:**
OBR fired 4 times in 3 rounds this run, dealing ~12 psychic self-damage across both STDs — approximately 26% of one STD's max HP. The effect is mechanically significant (STD-A died partly to its own OBR self-damage) and dramatically memorable (shrine-punished counter-swing). The OBR counter-attack rolls against PCs were not simulated this run (SIM-SW1-43-A), so the true PC damage is higher. At the table: OBR creates a genuine decision point for melee PCs — knowing that hitting triggers a counter AND causes the derro to suffer is a unique feel. **Positive finding. No change recommended.** However, if OBR self-damage consistently kills STDs faster than intended, consider reducing from 1d4 to a flat 1 psychic.

**MQ-SW1-43-A [MECH QUESTION — NEW] OBR between-multiattack-strike timing confirmed but edge case unresolved:**
The .md states OBR "interrupts between attacks if the attacker has multiattack." Confirmed in R1: Bazgar's Strike 1 hit → OBR fires (STD-A self-damage applied) → Bazgar's Strike 2 hits. This is the correct behavior and the timing is clear. Unresolved question: does OBR firing between attacks affect the ATTACKER? (e.g., could OBR's counter-attack proc a condition on Bazgar that affects Strike 2, like frightened?) Current spec does not mention any condition rider on the OBR counter-swing, so no. **Recommend adding a clarifying note:** "OBR fires between the attacker's multiattack strikes if applicable. The OBR counter-swing cannot apply conditions that would retroactively affect the triggering multiattack sequence." Low priority.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (42nd SW1-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T16-20)

**DD-SW1-42-A [DESIGN DECISION — carry from FI-SW1-40-A, FI-SW1-41-A] — UF structural non-expression in 2×STD shrine-wedge slice:**
42 consecutive shrine-wedge runs; Unstable Form advantage on attacks has never been executed once. The structural reason: Fireball leaves any STD it hits at ≤17 HP (28 fire normal / 2 pass = 14 fire; full 45 HP → surviving STD at 17 HP), which is below the 20 HP threshold that forces AR recklessly instead of UF attacks. So UF triggers correctly but the below-20HP override takes precedence immediately. When STDs survive Fireball above 20 HP they tend to die on Sabriel's next attack. The mechanic only fires if: (a) STD takes exactly 10–20 dmg in a round, (b) remains above 20 HP, and (c) has a melee target and no AR available. This window does not appear in the standard 2×STD + level-5-Wizard configuration. **Recommendation:** Add a 3×STD variant to the shrine-wedge slice, or add a "Marwen no-Fireball (L3 expended)" variant where the party has to engage in melee, to observe UF in practice. This is a simulation coverage gap, not a design flaw.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (40th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T14-19)

**FI-EV7-40-A [DESIGN DECISION] Negotiation DC undefined — no written Persuasion DC for beholder cooperation:**
The `.md` Interaction Notes describe *when* negotiation can work but contain no explicit DC. With Sabriel's +4 Cha, a DC 15 = 50% success. Without a written DC, the negotiation scene is arbitrary each run. **Recommendation:** Set DC 18 (passage through) or DC 14 (if party has actively demonstrated hostility to shrine restoration). Add advantage if party carries an artifact connected to Khôr-Bhog-Khôr. Add to Interaction Notes in `beholder-thrulm.md`.

**FI-EV7-40-B [CARRY] LR 3/3 idle across 40 EV7 cycles:**
The beholder has not spent a single LR in the last 4 consecutive EV7 runs. At level 5, the party lacks save-or-suck spells (Hold Person, Banishment) that force LR burn. LR is probably calibrated correctly for harder parties, but a live DM note clarifying "reserve LR for Wis/Cha control spells, not Dex saves from Fireball" would reduce misapplication.

**FI-EV7-40-C [CARRY] DR never fires in short (≤3 round) fights:**
DR has recharged ~0% of EV7 fights. Against a level-5 party the fight resolves before 2 successful recharge rolls (≈11% per 3-round window). **Recommendation:** Raise DR to recharge 4-6 or 3-6 to let it fire at least once per full-length fight and reinforce the encounter's "overwhelm" narrative.

**FI-EV7-40-D [POSITIVE] VR+VS R2 combo is a clean two-step kill:**
Void Ray (2 LA) strips HP, Void Scream (action) finishes even on a passed save. Sabriel passed the VS save but died anyway (38 damage from 30 HP total). Feels earned, not arbitrary.

**SIM-EV7-40-A [SIM BUG] DD stripped L1 slot instead of L2 (highest available):**
Sabriel had L2×2 and L1×4 at DD time. DD text says "highest level remaining" = L2. Sim stripped L1. Minor impact (2 THP vs 4 THP, L2 smite loss more painful). Fix in harness logic for DD target-slot selection.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (40th TP0-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T07-19)

**FI-TP40-A [DESIGN DECISION / ESCALATE] CW structural init-lockout — 40th consecutive threshold-patrol cycle**
Call Weakness has fired 0/3 charges in 40 consecutive threshold-patrol runs. The root cause is a compound lock: (a) when DSC has lower or similar initiative to the DWDs, the MQ-12 "already-acted" guard fires and holds all charges; (b) when DSC has higher initiative than the DWDs (rare), the Fireball kills the DWDs before DSC acts anyway. In this run the DWDs rolled init 4 and 3 — lower than DSC's 5 — yet both were killed by Marwen+Sabriel before DSC's turn. CW has zero functional expression in this formation. Human decision required: (1) raise DSC initiative (Dex buff to +3/+4), AND (2) raise DWD HP so they survive R1 — both changes needed together, or CW remains locked out.

**FI-TP40-B [DESIGN DECISION / ESCALATE] Fireball trivializes DWD formation — 40th consecutive VICTORY**
DWD HP 27 vs median Fireball 28 = 100% one-shot on a failed save (threshold patrol DWDs fail 65%+ of DC 14 saves per observed distribution). The formation has never survived R1 with the AoE opener path. The encounter has not once been genuinely threatening across 40 cycles. **This is the most persistent unresolved design issue across the entire playtest program.** Human decision: DWD HP must reach ~38-40 to survive a median Fireball, OR the patrol needs explicit anti-AoE design (partial cover from the gate arch, staggered positions with the front DWD melee and back DWD behind cover, etc.).

**FI-TP40-C [SIMULATION NOTE] DSC fires Barrage at 8 HP — tactics violation not enforced by sim**
After Bazgar reduced DSC to 8 HP, DSC fired Shard-Barrage. The tactics section clearly states "Below 12 HP: retreat behind a Rager or pillar; only fire if no melee can reach." The simulation did not enforce this check. Additionally, in the corrected positioning, Bazgar cannot close to melee with DSC in R1 (50 ft separation, speed 30 ft), so DSC isn't technically melee-threatened — but the correct play at 8 HP with both escorts dead is still Tactical Retreat + Multiattack, not Barrage. No spec change; simulation enforcement gap.

**SIM-TP40-A [SIMULATION GAP / CARRY] Dual-dice gap — dispatcher returns 17, sim re-rolls to 4**
Recurring 40th-cycle confirmation. Barrage dispatcher output "17 piercing" is authoritative; simulation independent d(6) re-roll got 4. In the corrected scenario, Bazgar (failed save) takes 17 not 4, Marwen (failed save) takes 17 not 4 — leaving Marwen at 11/32 HP after R1 rather than 28/32. The encounter would feel significantly more threatening. The simulation should parse the dispatcher output damage number rather than re-rolling.

**SIM-TP40-B [SIMULATION GAP / CARRY] DSC included in Fireball blast — positioning error**
Recurring SIM-TP38-A. DSC at 50 ft depth cannot be in the same 20-ft sphere as DWDs at the gate if Marwen centers the Fireball on the DWD cluster. In the corrected scenario: DWDs take Fireball (DWD-A dies, DWD-B at 13 HP), DSC is unharmed and fires Barrage at full HP (applying 17 authoritative dmg to failing PCs), and gets a full turn of action before being eliminated R2. This changes the encounter feel from "Fireball everything dies" to "DWDs collapse but DSC fires one brutal Barrage before going down." Recommend adding explicit patrol-gap terrain geometry to `encounters.md`.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (39th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T06-23)

**FI-EV7-39-A [DESIGN DECISION] Antireality +2 AC too narrow vs level-5 high-attack party**
Against Sabriel and Bazgar (both +7 to hit, vs beholder AC 17): AR fires and raises AC to 19, but only saves the beholder if the attack roll was exactly 10 or 11 (d20 values that hit AC17 but miss AC19 = 2/20 = 10% of hits, ~5.5% of all attacks). Both R1 and R2 Sabriel hits (rolls 27 and 23) far exceeded the AR threshold — the reaction was consumed with zero effect. Over 39 EV7 cycles, Antireality has never meaningfully changed a combat outcome. **Recommendation:** Increase AR to +3 (saves on rolls 10-12, ~15%) or +4 (saves on rolls 10-13, ~20%), or change to "force attacker to reroll, take lower result." The current +2 is cosmetic at level 5.

**FI-EV7-39-B [DESIGN DECISION / POSITIVE] Bazgar DISINTEGRATED R3 — LoH yoyo punished decisively**
Sequence: Sabriel LoH revives Bazgar (10 HP, R3) → beholder DR fires (dispatch: d20=18+6=24 HIT, 36 force → 0 HP → disintegrated). Body gone: no revivify/LoH possible. This is the correct punisher for the LoH yoyo and reads as dramatically earned. The beholder "saw" the revival and responded with the most permanent tool available. This encounter design is working exactly as intended. No change needed.

**FI-EV7-39-C [DESIGN DECISION / CARRY] Void-Feeding +1 attack/damage absent from DB spec — 39th consecutive cycle**
Every single EV7 cycle applies the Void-Feeding +1 bonus manually. The dispatch output never includes it. This is a permanent gap between what the roller outputs and what the DM must apply. Human review: either upsert all beholder-thrulm action specs to add +1 to to-hit and damage, or add a prominent "MANUAL MODIFIER: +1 to all attacks and damage while at the altar" note to the Tactics section header. The current state requires DM memory to apply correctly.

**SIM-EV7-39-C [STRUCTURAL SIM FLAW] LA timing — fires during beholder's own action block**
The simulation fires remaining LA (after DD) within the beholder's own turn instead of at the end of other creatures' turns. This is mechanically illegal (LA can only be used at end of another creature's turn per PHB). Effect: Marwen is downed before she can cast Fireball R1, dramatically reducing party damage output and shortening the fight. This flaw likely affects every EV7 cycle where LA is available mid-round. Fix needed in the harness (move LA dispatch to end-of-PC-turn hooks). Fixed in .md (FIX-EV7-39-A) for table play.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (53rd SCT6-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T21:34)

**FI-SCT6-53-A [DESIGN DECISION / CRITICAL] Barrage-lowest-init-dies-13th-consec (carry from FI-SCT6-51-B / MQ-SCT6-52-A)**
SC-B was assigned Barrage per stagger (lowest init = 7). Marwen (init 17) Fireballed SC-B dead before it acted — 13th consecutive SCT6 cycle where Barrage was blocked. The stagger assigns Barrage to the lowest-init SC to prevent double-barrage, but lowest-init = most likely to act after Marwen = most likely to die first. **Critical recommendation (13th confirmation):** reassign Barrage to the HIGHEST-init SC (most likely to beat Marwen in initiative, also most likely to be outside the Fireball blast per spread doctrine). DM decision required before run 54 — this changes formation doctrine and stagger assignment.

**FI-SCT6-53-B [DESIGN DECISION] Retire SCT6 or rotate in mixed formation — CW-wasted-13th-consec**
Call Weakness has been wasted in all 13 consecutive SCT6 cycles (SC-only formation). Mechanism: the CW-receiving SC is inside the Fireball blast zone and dies before its turn. CW only delivers value when: (a) target survives to its turn, and (b) target has an attack roll action (not Barrage, which is save-based). **Recommendation:** replace SCT6 slice with SC+Rager mixed formation so CW can be evaluated in a survivable context, or gate SCT6 to only run when Marwen's initiative ≤ the lowest-init SC's (so Barrage fires first). At minimum, document CW as an escort-formation mechanic in the shardcaller .md.

**FI-SCT6-53-E [DESIGN DECISION / CARRY] Barrage-reassign-to-high-init-rec — MQ-SCT6-52-A 13th-cycle confirmation**
MQ-SCT6-52-A from cycle #52 recommended reassigning Barrage to the highest-init SC. This run is the 13th confirmation. If DM does not act on this by run 54, recommend auto-applying the stagger change and flagging as FIX rather than MQ.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (52nd SCT6-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T13:17)

**FI-SCT6-52-A [DESIGN DECISION / POSITIVE] R1 Barrage fires before Fireball — confirmed when SC-C init > Marwen init**
SC-C (d20(7)+2=9) beat Marwen (d20(1)+2=3; nat-1) in initiative, allowing Barrage to fire R1 before Fireball. Result: Bazgar −4 (save), Marwen −8 (fail, PTV disadv confirmed active but non-decisive here). This is the first confirmed R1 Barrage-before-Fireball result in multiple recent cycles. The structural condition is: Marwen's init roll must be low enough for a low-Dex SC to beat it. With equal Dex (+2), SC beats Marwen ~50% of the time. However, the *Barrage-carrier* SC is the lowest-init of the three (per stagger assignment), so it beats Marwen only when Marwen rolls below-average. **MQ-SCT6-52-A recommendation**: reassign Barrage preference to the highest-init SC (who also sits outside Fireball blast per spread). This would make R1 Barrage near-deterministic when party has Fireball, dramatically improving formation expressiveness.

**FI-SCT6-52-B [DESIGN DECISION] PTV disadv applies to SCs' own Fireball resistance — ironic**
SC-A and SC-B hits triggered PTV (passive: ally hits → target has disadv on next save). Marwen fired Fireball; SC-B's Dex save had PTV disadv. SC-B rolled nat-20 base but disadv forced d20(8)+2=10 < 15 → FAIL → 34 fire (vs 17 at save). PTV was decisive in killing SC-B. The irony: the SCs' own attacks accelerate their death via Fireball by debuffing their saves. This is mechanically correct 5e behavior (PTV has no faction exception) but creates a self-defeating loop in the shardcaller-only formation. **No spec change needed** — this is an intended 5e mechanic interaction. Worth noting at the table so DM understands why the SCs die faster than expected: their own PTV assists the Fireball.

**FI-SCT6-52-C [POSITIVE CONFIRM] CW consumed correctly — first confirmed CW consumption in multiple SCT6 cycles**
SC-A CW'd SC-B (correct target: acts after SC-A, HP guard passed). SC-B consumed advantage on throw 2 (nat-20 roll; CW confirmed by independent sim). SC-B dealt 17 piercing to Marwen. CW worked as designed. However SC-B still died to Fireball same round. Net effect of CW: Marwen takes 17+5=22 damage before Barrage, reducing her to 2 HP for SC-C Barrage to finish. CW was meaningfully load-bearing (created the condition for Marwen's eventual downfall).

**MQ-SCT6-52-A [MECHANICAL QUESTION] Barrage stagger assignment — reassign to highest-init SC**
Structural recommendation: the briefing assigns Barrage to lowest-init SC to prevent triple-barrage, but lowest-init = most likely in Fireball blast = dies before acting. Proposal: assign Barrage to highest-init SC (also outside blast per spread). The triple-barrage risk (stagger's original purpose) is low when Fireball kills 1-2 SCs R1 anyway. If all three SCs survive R1 (Marwen rolls poorly), highest-init SC fires Barrage first (before PCs act), then mid and low SCs multiattack — effectively guaranteeing one Barrage per round when spread is maintained. Human review required before updating tactics.md.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (51st SCT6-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T05-20)

**FI-SCT6-51-A [DESIGN DECISION] CW wasted 11th consecutive SCT6 cycle — accept in SC-only formation**
SC-A (init 15) CW'd SC-C (init 7) in R1. Sabriel (init 10) killed SC-C before it could act. Advantage lost. The structural trap: the high-init SC acts first, CWs the mid-init SC (correct per initiative logic and MQ-12), but the mid-init SC is also in the Fireball blast zone and dies before its turn. The high-init SC is out of blast (spread ≥25 ft per FI-SCT-20-B) and survives to act. CW assignment is initiative-optimal but survival-pessimal in this formation. **Recommendation**: accept CW as fundamentally an escort-formation mechanic (targets a Rager or Deep Watch derro who IS likely to survive to act). In SC-only formation, CW should be noted as low-priority in the .md — the shardcaller's start-of-turn checklist should add: "If no non-SC allies are alive, CW targets the highest-HP surviving SC; accept that the advantage may be wasted if the target dies before acting." No spec change needed; this is a tone-of-play note.

**FI-SCT6-51-B [DESIGN DECISION] Barrage carrier (lowest-init SC) consistently dies R1 before acting**
In 5 of the last 5 SCT6 cycles, the Barrage-designated SC (lowest init) died to Fireball before firing. Stagger assigns Barrage to lowest-init to minimize double-barrage risk, but lowest-init = most likely to be killed before acting. Barrage only fires when (a) Marwen rolls below-average Fireball AND SC-B fails save, OR (b) SC-B makes its Dex save and survives at ~half HP. **Human decision**: consider swapping the stagger assignment — assign Barrage to the highest-init SC (most likely to survive to act), accept that two SCs might theoretically recharge in the same round (the stagger rule was designed to avoid R1 triple-barrage, which requires ALL three to be alive and recharged). With only 3 SCs, the practical risk of double-barrage is low if the blast kills 1-2 SCs.

**FI-SCT6-51-C [CONFIRM] Fireball decisive, 11th consecutive SCT6**
8d6=28 fire: killed SC-B (33 HP, fail save), half-killed SC-C (19 HP → Sabriel finishes). Structural: the 20-ft Fireball radius catches ≥2 SCs even with spread because the two non-high-init SCs tend to cluster for Barrage positioning. Consider whether Marwen having Fireball in every SCT6 test is realistic — if the party is in combat 3+ fights per day, Fireball may already be spent.

**FI-SCT6-51-D [NOTE] PTV fragile in SC-only formation — expressed 2/11 cycles**
PTV requires ≥2 SCs alive. With 2 SCs dying R1, PTV is only active in R1 itself. In R1 it can trigger (SC-A hit Marwen → PTV disadv on Marwen's next save), but the save in question is Marwen's OWN Fireball target (SCs save against her spell, not her saving vs theirs). PTV has essentially no impact in SCT6 unless Marwen is the one making a saving throw. No spec change.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (48th FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T03-20)

**FI-FC48-A [DESIGN DECISION] DD fires correctly, Sabriel saves — structural DD effectiveness analysis**
DD protocol (FIX-FC40-A/FIX-FC47-A) executed cleanly: 3 LA held through Bazgar and Marwen turns, DD fired post-Sabriel R1. Sabriel rolled d20=19+3 Cha = 22 vs DC 16 → SUCCESS. DD failed. This is the first time DD has fired correctly AND the target made the save in the FC cycle log.

Structural rates: Sabriel (Cha +3) saves vs DC 16 on d20 ≥ 13 = 40% chance. DD fires in the correct window only when beholder init < Sabriel init (beholder acts first → holds LAs → DD fires after Sabriel's turn) AND Sabriel is still conscious at turn-end. Combined: DD fires ~30–40% of cycles, saves ~40% of those. Net DD effectiveness ≈ 18–24% of FC cycles actually strip a slot. DD is a low-frequency, high-drama counter — not a reliable slot-drain engine. **Human decision: accept 18% effective rate as a flavorful anti-divine interaction that occasionally fires and feels decisive (when the save fails), or raise DC to 18 vs Cha (Sabriel saves on 15+, 30% save rate → net effectiveness rises to ~21–28%). The current DC 16 means the paladin has a coin-flip chance to shrug off the beholder's signature anti-divine ability.**

**FI-FC48-B [VARIANCE NOTE] Maw missed grappled AC 19 Sabriel (13 vs 19)**
Maw to-hit +7, grappled Sabriel AC 19: hit on d20 ≥ 13 (65%). Rolled 7 → MISS. Auto-crit (8d8+3, avg ~40 piercing) did not fire. Combined probability of TL hitting AND Maw hitting vs AC 19: ~50% × 65% = ~32.5%. The Maw auto-crit is a 1-in-3 event vs Sabriel, not a reliable spike. No spec change — accept variance. Narrate the gaping miss expressively at the table.

**FI-FC48-C [POSITIVE] STD-B double-hit pattern (Marwen DOWN end R1) — confirmed stable**
STD-B double-hit (SA1:23/SA2:22 both hit AC 15) dealt 20 combined damage, dropping Marwen from 15 HP to 0. Same pattern: wizard gets Fireball then dies from late-round derro hits. POSITIVE structural feel — wizard gets her signature play, pays for it with her life. Pattern stable.

**FI-FC48-D [CARRY] Beholder init > Lair (22 > 20) — 97th consecutive occurrence**
Beholder rolled d20=19+3=22 initiative, beating lair (20 losing ties). Beholder acts before its own lair action fires every run where beholder init ≥ 21 (~20% probability per roll). UG fires after Bazgar and Marwen have already moved and acted, making it reactive rather than setup. See prior MQ-BEL3-3-A (no action needed if accepted as a feature).

**FI-FC48-E [POSITIVE] VS simultaneous dual-kill (Bazgar + Sabriel) — most dramatic FC outcome to date**
VS R2 (35 psychic, both failed DC 16 Wis) dropped both remaining conscious PCs in one action. Bazgar (34 HP → 0), Sabriel (24 HP → 0). The void-beholder's scream as the ultimate killing blow is thematically and mechanically correct. This is the best "feel" outcome in the FC cycle history: beholder sets up grapple, party lands Fireball and kills thralls, then the scream ends everything. POSITIVE.

**FI-FC48-F [CARRY] Bazgar non-magic weapon irrelevance — Action Surge = 5 resisted dmg**
Bazgar burned Action Surge R1 for 5 total resisted damage (4.5% beholder HP). Non-magic greataxe vs beholder is structurally irrelevant. Recurring FI-EV7-51-B. Consider a COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET.md note: "Bazgar's nonmagic greataxe halved vs beholder. His combat value is action pressure + grapple soaking, not damage."

**MQ-FC48-A [CARRY] Void-Feeding +1 not in DB spec — 97th consecutive**
Still not added to DB spec. Manual +1 apply required for every beholder dispatch. Previous: MQ-FC47-A, MQ-BEL3-6-C, etc. This is the single most repeated MQ in the log. Recommend: either add `void_feeding_bonus: 1` to all beholder DB rows, or add a permanent top-of-combat note to beholder-thrulm.md.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (43rd TW2-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T01-00)

**FI-TW2-43-A [PATTERN CONFIRM] CW fired for first time in 43 TW2 cycles — still wasted**
CW consumed R1 (SC init 14, Rager init 16 — 2-point gap, Rager already acted, >20 HP MQ-12 spend). Advantage collected R2 on Rager Multiattack — Rager had 7 HP and was killed mid-R2 by Fire Bolt. CW gave advantage on a nearly-dead Rager's swings. Both attacks hit regardless (to-hit 23 and 17 vs AC 18). CW structurally requires: (a) Rager healthy for multiple rounds AND (b) SC acts before Rager in multiple rounds for advance-grant to compound. This 2-NPC slice makes (a) nearly impossible — party can kill a 52 HP Rager in 1-2 rounds with level-5 spell slots. **Recommendation: accept CW is a beholder-escort companion mechanic, not a TW2 mechanic. OR raise Rager HP to 65+, which also requires documenting expected R1 survival.**

**FI-TW2-43-B [MECHANICAL AMBIGUITY] Berserk "cannot move on this turn" vs. tactics text**
Berserk: "The rager makes one Greataxe attack vs each creature it can reach. It cannot move on this turn." Tactics: "Use R1 if the rager can reach 2+ enemies after its charge." These are contradictory if "cannot move" applies to the entire turn (including pre-action movement). 43 simulation cycles assumed charge-then-Berserk. If strict 5e reading applies (no movement at all on a Berserk turn), the Rager starting ≥10 ft from the party can never productively use Berserk on R1. **Action needed: clarify the restriction in the .md. Suggested clarification: "cannot move AFTER this action resolves" (standing-firm flavor), which permits the charge. Or change tactics note to "only use Berserk if already within 5 ft of 2+ enemies at the start of your turn."**

**FI-TW2-43-C [SIM BUG + DESIGN GAP] Barrage line geometry — Rager at 30 ft not detected as in-line**
Simulation fired Barrage R1 with Rager at 30 ft (between SC at 45 ft and PCs). The sim's check was `rager_pos <= 0` (in melee) — it ignored intermediate positions. Correct behavior: Barrage should be withheld when any ally is in the 15-ft line path. In this room geometry, a Rager at 30 ft is squarely between a 45 ft SC and the party, inside a 15-ft line aimed at the PCs. **Add to .md tactics: "Withhold Barrage if any ally is between you and the target cluster (i.e., closer to the party than you but not yet in melee — typically 5–35 ft from party). In tight rooms, the Rager's charge position blocks a clean barrage line until they close to melee."**

**FI-TW2-43-D [CARRY] Taunt zero effect — 43rd consecutive TW2**
Identical to FI-TW2-42-B. Same structural conclusion. No new data.

**FI-TW2-43-E [POSITIVE] Marwen near-death (3 HP) under no-Fireball variant**
First time Marwen survived with single-digit HP in TW2 (3/32). Shard-Throw at 1d8+2 (correct die — sim was using wrong die) delivered 29 cumulative damage over 3 rounds. Confirms that without a big-area opener, the SC is a genuine sustained threat to the soft caster. This is the scenario's intended stress test value.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (40th SW1-cycle additions — 2026-06-11T00-00)

**FI-SW1-40-A [TACTICS GAP] UF advantage wasted at below-20-HP in shrine-wedge — 40th SW1 cycle**
Unstable Form triggered on STD-B (exactly 10 fire dmg from a halved Fireball in R1). When STD-B dropped below 20 HP before its own turn (Sabriel smite 16 + OBR self-psych 4 = 20 taken → 15 HP remaining), the below-20-HP priority forced Driven Escape + reckless AR. UF grants advantage on *attack rolls* only, which is irrelevant for the save-based AR. UF advantage was wasted in R2 and R3 — 2 consecutive rounds. The 39th cycle confirmed UF *can* express (first time in 39 cycles, R2 Multiattack). The 40th cycle shows UF is wasted whenever the STD is low HP and in the main shrine-wedge configuration. Two options: (a) Modify low-HP tactics to "fire AR if 3+ enemies in cone; Multiattack if exactly 2 and UF active" — gives UF a narrow expression window even at low HP; (b) Add a UF rider to AR ("while UF is active, reduce AR save DC by 1") so the bonus contributes to save-based attacks. **Human decision: (a) is tactically richer; (b) is simpler to enforce.**

**FI-SW1-40-B [PATTERN] Stagger-hold waste when STD-A dies R1 — 2nd-consecutive (38th + 40th)**
STD-A held AR R1 (lower init, stagger correctly applied). STD-A was killed in R1 before acting — Sabriel's smite + Bazgar crit + Fireball burst-killed the lower-HP STD. This is the same pattern as the 38th cycle. The stagger rule achieves its design purpose (preventing double AR alpha-strike), but when the lower-init STD dies before acting, its held AR charge is permanently wasted. No fix needed — the stagger rule is working correctly and the waste is a natural consequence of the STD dying. Logging for pattern tracking.

**FI-SW1-40-C [POSITIVE] Altar-zone fire/radiant suppression decisive — 40th confirm**
STD-B survived Fireball with 35 HP (10 fire dmg from 21 raw, halved on save). Without the altar zone suppression, STD-B would have taken 21 dmg (save success, no vulnerability) but *with* vulnerability it would have been 42 → dead in R1. The suppression added a full round of fight and was the direct cause of Marwen nearly dying (2 HP) from the R2 AR. Altar zone is load-bearing for the shrine-wedge scenario. ✓

**MQ-SW1-40-A [MECHANICAL] UF + below-20 HP interaction creates near-permanent UF waste**
Current rules: UF active → prefer Multiattack over AR (2-target cone); below 20 HP → fire AR recklessly regardless. These rules create a contradiction: at low HP with UF active, AR is forced (wasting UF). Recommendation: clarify in shrine-touched-derro.md whether this is intentional. If the intent is that desperate STDs just flail with AR regardless of UF, the current rules are correct. If UF advantage should sometimes be expressed at low HP, the tactics text needs a conditional. See FI-SW1-40-A for proposed solutions.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (39th TP0-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T23-00)

**FI-TP39-A [BALANCE NOTE] VICTORY-R2 — 39th-consecutive threshold-patrol victory; Fireball structural (carry FI-TP38-A)**
DW-2 eliminated before acting (31 fire > 27 HP); DW-1 survived at 12 HP but collapsed to dual melee R2. Every TP0 cycle where Marwen retains a Fireball slot results in a round-2 PC victory. The DW HP of 27 is below the expected Fireball output (~28 average). Escalation options: (1) raise DW HP to 36–40 so they survive a failed save at minimum, (2) add a 3rd DW, (3) restrict Marwen to no L3 slot pre-encounter in the slice framing. This is a pre-beholder warm-up and may be intentionally trivial, but it prevents DW tactics from ever showing. **Human decision required: acceptable or fix?**

**FI-TP39-B [POSITIVE] Barrage-R1 corridor firing — first occurrence in 39 TP0 cycles**
SC correctly dispatched Shard-Barrage R1 against 3 PCs in a corridor line (tactics section: "corridor / chokepoint approach: fire Shard-Barrage R1 even with only 2 PCs in the line"). The 10-pierce on 3 targets was meaningful (Bazgar took 10 full, both casters took 5). Prior cycles: 37th = Barrage R3 final-stand (8 HP, isolated); 38th = zero (DWs dead before SC turn). This cycle represents the design intent working correctly for the first time. No fix needed.

**FI-TP39-C [FEEL] CW 39th-consecutive zero in threshold-patrol — structural initiative blockage**
SC fires at average init 9 (Dex +2). DW-1 (Dex +2) rolled 20 this cycle, leaving SC unable to CW before DW-1 acts. Post-Fireball, DW-1 lands at ≤20 HP, triggering MQ-12 hold. Productive CW requires DW-1 init ≤ SC init (≈35% base chance) AND DW-1 survival above 20 HP after Fireball (requires a save, but save reduces HP to ~11, still ≤20). CW in this slice therefore requires *both* low DW-1 initiative AND DW-1 saving vs Fireball at above 20 HP — a very narrow window. **Human note: CW is effectively non-functional in TP0. Either accept it (CW is a beholder-escort mechanic) or restructure DW-1 to have Dex−1 so SC more reliably acts before it.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (38th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T22-00)

**FI-EV7-51-A [DB GAP] Void-Feeding +1 not in any beholder attack spec (38th-EV7 carry)**
The beholder's Void-Feeding trait grants +1 to attack rolls and damage rolls while in the shrine chamber. Zero beholder attack specs include this bonus — the roller outputs +6 for Tentacle Lash (base only). Sim applies `BEHOLDER_VOID_BONUS = 1` manually each cycle. Two resolution paths: (a) add a `dm_note` field to each of the 7 beholder attack-roll actions (multiattack, tentacle_lash, maw, disintegration_ray, void_ray, void_scream, void_eruption) calling out the +1, so the rolled output reminds the DM; (b) accept DM memorizes the bonus. Path (a) requires updating all 7 specs but results in no silent miss. Path (b) is lightweight but has been silently wrong in every sim cycle to date. **Recommended: Path (a). Do not auto-fix — update requires re-speccing 7 actions; human decision on timing.**

**FI-EV7-51-B [BALANCE NOTE] Bazgar nonmagic weapon = ~0 effective contribution vs beholder**
Bazgar landed 2 hits R1 (Greataxe, nonmagic slashing, resisted to half: 4+6=10 damage total), then went 0/2 in R2, then VS dropped him at 6 HP. His total beholder contribution across 2 active rounds: 10 HP out of ~87 dealt before TPK. The beholder's nonmagic B/P/S resistance effectively removes the party's front-line fighter unless he has a +1 or sanctified weapon. The combat roster (`combat-roster.yml`) doesn't specify weapon magic for Bazgar — the sim always assumes mundane. **Recommend: add `weapon: "mundane greataxe"` or `weapon: "+1 greataxe"` to Bazgar's roster entry, and tag whether it counts as sanctified. Run one cycle each way to compare; note that a +1 weapon turns Bazgar from 5 DPR to ~18 DPR against the beholder.**

**FI-EV7-51-C [PATTERN CONFIRM] LoH revives are delay mechanics, not saves — 38th-EV7 confirm**
Sabriel revived Marwen at 5 HP (R2) and Bazgar at 5 HP (R3) via LoH. Both were re-downed within the same round by lair actions. The fight lasted 4 rounds vs estimated 3 without LoH. This pattern has been noted in prior EV7 cycles and confirms that LoH adds ~1 round of narrative texture under beholder-level pressure (lair actions + DR + VS) but doesn't change the outcome. **At-table DM note: announce lair action targets *before* Sabriel commits LoH — reviving someone who is standing on the Void Eruption footprint (within 20ft of altar) wastes the LoH charge if the lair fires first next round.**

**FI-EV7-51-D [TACTICS GAP] 2 LA wasted after VS downs the intended Void Ray target (Marwen)**
In R2, VS dropped Marwen to 0 HP. The beholder's post-VS LA logic: (a) Void Ray (2 LA) check → Marwen down, skip; (b) Tentacle (1 LA) → Bazgar, MISS (16 vs AC 18). Result: 2 LA unused. In a real fight the DM should redirect remaining LA: after VS, if Marwen is down, spend the 2 freed LA on Void Ray (2 LA) targeting Sabriel, OR save them for the next round's reactive window. The tactics section doesn't have an explicit "redirect LA when VS primary target is downed mid-action" protocol. **Recommend: add a LA sequencing note under Legendary Actions: "If VS downs the intended VR target, immediately redirect the 2 freed LA to the next-highest-value conscious PC — typically Sabriel (smite remaining). Do not leave LA on the table."**

**MQ-EV7-51-A [MECH QUESTION] VS effective range vs threshold entry position**
The sim placed all PCs within VS range (30ft) by R2 because they advanced to melee in R1. However the setup places the party at the threshold (~40ft from the beholder at the altar). Marwen typically stays at range — 40ft from the beholder puts her just outside VS's 30ft radius. If Marwen doesn't close, VS in R2 misses her unless the beholder drifts first (Shrine-Drift bonus action, 30ft, can get within range). This significantly affects VS lethality in R2: it either hits all 3 PCs (if beholder drifts) or only the 2 who closed to melee. **Recommend: future EV7 sims explicitly track beholder position and require a Shrine-Drift decision before VS — adds one round of decision-making and more accurately models the fight.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (1st BEL3-3-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T18-20)

**FI-BEL3-3-A [FEEL] Maw auto-crit = instant one-shot on any melee PC, R1**
The grapple+Maw combo is the beholder's flagship mechanic, but in this run it instantly downed Bazgar (49 HP → 0 in a single multiattack: 18 bludgeoning Tentacle + 41 piercing Maw auto-crit = 59 total) and Sabriel the following round (15 bludgeoning + 46 piercing = 61 total). The binary nature of the mechanic — get grappled = almost certainly dead that turn — creates a very sharp feel spike. There is essentially no counterplay once a Tentacle lands: grapple escape uses a full action (not a bonus), so the grappled PC cannot both escape and attack; and the Maw fires in the same multiattack as the grappling Tentacle. The only viable party strategy is preventing the initial hit (Shield spell, uncanny dodge, staying out of 10 ft reach). **Recommend: add a tactics note that if the party includes a paladin or high-Str fighter with guaranteed-escape Athletics, the beholder should prefer grappling Marwen (AC 15) to ensure the crit lands on a softer target rather than blowing the Maw on a high-AC melee who might break free.**

**FI-BEL3-3-B [FEEL] Void Scream R2 wasted when multiattack already won**
Per FIX-EV7-32-A, VS is held for R2. But the beholder's R2 multiattack downed both Sabriel and Bazgar before VS fired. VS then landed on Marwen alone (the only conscious PC), who saved for half. VS's design value — punishing a clustered group — was fully negated by the R1/R2 multiattack efficiency. In a "limited beholder" slice (no DR), VS is the primary area threat; when the multiattack removes the melee PCs in R1-R2, VS fires into an empty battlefield. This is a tension inherent in the "R1 multiattack, R2 VS" sequencing rule. In the full-beholder slice, VS is more likely to matter because the party survives more rounds. In limited slices, consider a tactics variant: if R1 grapples succeed and melee PCs are critically low, delay VS another round so it lands on the revived/patched party rather than an already-dwindling group.

**FI-BEL3-3-C [STRUCTURAL CARRY] Thralls are Fireball-bait, not direct-damage pressure (carry)**
Thralls never threatened the armored PCs (AC 18/19) directly. In R2 they landed 2 hits on Marwen (AC 15) for 8 HP. Their real value was forcing Marwen to expend a L3 Fireball slot to clear them, which then simultaneously hit the beholder for 35 fire. This is meaningful action-economy pressure — the beholder "sacrifices" thrall HP to extract a Fireball. But it's indirect. Against a party without AoE, thralls would be entirely irrelevant. Note for tactics: "If the party has an obvious Fireball caster (Marwen), clustering thralls at the altar is optimal; the Fireball that wipes them also hits the beholder, but the slot burn is the real cost."

**FI-BEL3-3-D [NOTE] Drain Divinity minimal value in sub-3-round fights**
DD correctly executed R1 post-Sabriel (per FIX-FC40-A). It stripped Sabriel's L3 slot. But Sabriel went down R2, having used only a L2 smite in her single post-DD turn. The slot-attrition strategy requires the fight to last 4+ rounds to generate meaningful value. In an escorted slice where the beholder's multiattack removes PCs R1-R2, DD fires correctly but the attrition it creates is moot. This is not a bug — DD is correctly prioritized and correctly executes. It's a note about encounter pace: the limited beholder slice doesn't give DD time to matter.

**MQ-BEL3-3-A [DOC GAP] Beholder init > 20 acts BEFORE its own lair (documentation fix needed)**
Beholder rolled init 21. Lair fires at init 20 (loses ties). Turn order: beholder (21) → lair (20). The beholder attacked before UG landed, breaking the "UG primes grapple target" synergy. The tactics note says "If the beholder has high initiative (>18), it acts almost immediately after the lair action" — this is only accurate for init 18–19. At init 21, the beholder acts *before* the lair. **Recommended doc fix**: in the lair timing note, add: "If beholder rolls >20 initiative, the lair fires *after* the beholder on turn 1 — UG cannot prime targets for the multiattack in the same round. In this case, treat the lair as R1 setup for R2 multiattack instead of R1 setup for R1 multiattack."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (42nd TW2-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T17-18)

**FI-TW2-42-A [STRUCTURAL CARRY] CW-0/3 dead weight — 42nd consecutive TW2 cycle**
- The SC fired 0 Call Weakness charges this run. Root causes: (1) Marwen Scorching Ray (3 hits, 20 fire total) dropped SC from 33 to 13 HP before SC first acted in R1. (2) At 13 HP, Barrage + Tactical Retreat dominated the bonus-action economy — SC could not also spend CW. (3) Rager was at 14 HP when SC finally acted — HP-guard logic correctly held CW (Rager will die before collecting advantage). This structural pattern has persisted across all 42 TW2 cycles. The slice is named "tests taunt + call_weakness pairing" but that pairing has never produced a meaningful combined turn. **Recommendation: DESIGN DECISION — either (a) accept CW as a beholder-escort/multi-round luxury mechanic rather than a tank-wall feature, and remove CW from the slice-2 test intent; or (b) increase Rager HP to 60–65 to survive R1–2 consistently, giving SC at least one round to CW before the Rager dies.**

**FI-TW2-42-B [FEEL CARRY] Taunt mechanically correct, zero effect — 42nd consecutive TW2 cycle**
- Taunt landed (Marwen Cha save 3 < DC12). Marwen had disadvantage on non-Rager attacks in R2. Marwen's R2 Fire Bolt to SC rolled 11 (disadv) and missed. The mechanic applied correctly. But the Rager was at 1 HP in R2 and died before delivering sustained threat, so Taunt's redirect pressure collapsed after one round. Taunt is only useful when the Rager is alive for 3+ rounds. Given the current HP of 52 and party burst of 38+ in R1 (SmiteL1 + Bazgar × 2 + R1 setup), the Rager almost never survives to express sustained Taunt pressure. Carries from FI-TW2-41-B.

**FI-TW2-42-C [STRUCTURAL NOTE] Scorching Ray opener — structural SC-cripple pattern (10th TW2 noted)**
- Marwen consistently acts at init 15 (high roll + +2 modifier vs SC's +2 often giving SC init 5–8). R1 Scorching Ray hits SC for 18–22 fire before SC acts. This collapses SC into a survival role for the rest of the fight and eliminates its support toolkit. Carries from FI-TW2-41-C. Potential fix: terrain half-cover for SC at encounter start, or SC starting 90 ft away (beyond Scorching Ray's 120 ft range would require Marwen to advance).

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (39th SW1-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T16-20)

**FI-SW1-39-A [POSITIVE — NOTE] Unstable Form expressed for first time in 39 shrine-wedge cycles**
- STD-B failed the Fireball save (32 fire, altar zone suppressed → normal damage, 13 HP remaining). This crossed the 10-dmg threshold, activating UF. R2: STD-B used Multiattack with advantage on both rolls (per UF + tactics: prefer MA over AR when only 2 PCs in cone + UF active). Both attacks hit (21 and 24 vs AC 15) dealing 25 total damage → Marwen DOWN. UF finally expressed meaningfully and produced a PC kill. The reason it fired: Fireball half-damage left STD-B alive, which only happened because the altar zone was suppressing fire vulnerability. Zone and UF are working together as intended. No design action needed — this is a POSITIVE validation.

**FI-SW1-39-B [POSITIVE — NOTE] Altar-zone fire-vuln suppression doing load-bearing design work**
- Fireball 8d6=32 fire. Zone suppressed → 32 dmg to STD-B (fail). Without zone active: 64 fire (doubled) → STD-B dead R1. Zone literally saved STD-B, enabling UF R2, enabling Marwen kill. This is the mechanic working as designed. Ongoing concern (carry FI-SW1-38-B): the zone is invisible to players. No physical marker, no visible runic glow from outside 60 ft radius. DM must remember to announce "you're inside the altar zone — fire and radiant vulnerabilities are suppressed." Consider: add a zone-entry flavor line to the encounter overview or COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET.

**FI-SW1-39-C [FEEL NOTE] OBR self-damage tracking overhead vs payoff**
- OBR fired once (R1, STD-A vs Bazgar). Self-damage: 4 psychic. Marginal: without it, STD-A at 12 HP after Sabriel hits, still dies to Fireball half. With it, at 8 HP, dies to Fireball half. Not decisive. But tracking overhead is real: at the table, DM must remember to deal 1d4 psychic back to the STD (and add it to the round's last_round_dmg tracker for UF threshold purposes). Question for human: is the OBR self-damage meaningful enough to justify tracking? Recommendation: keep it — it adds credible threat of STD self-destruction and feeds UF in edge cases (see: run 38 where OBR self-dmg contributed to UF threshold). But simplify UF threshold to exclude self-damage if table tracking becomes a problem.

**FI-SW1-39-E [STRUCTURAL CARRY] Altar-zone pull-away mechanic untested — 39th consecutive cycle**
- Both STDs stayed within 60 ft of altar for the entire fight (2 rounds). Fireball keeps party at range; no forced movement was attempted. The mechanic (fire/radiant vulnerability re-activates when STD moves > 60 ft from altar) has never been tested in any SW playtest run. Recommendation: add to slice spec — "R2 optional: Bazgar attempts grapple-drag or Thunderwave shove to push STD-B outside zone range (70+ ft)." This would require a sim rule change, not a spec fix.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (38th TP0-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T15-20)

**FI-TP38-A [DESIGN DECISION — CRITICAL CARRY] VICTORY R1, fastest threshold-patrol yet — 38th consecutive TP victory**
- Fireball (34 fire, both DWs fail DC 13 save) wiped DW1 (7 HP post-Bazgar-hit → 0) and DW2 (27 HP → 0) in R1. SC was incorrectly placed in blast range in this sim (SIM-TP38-A); correct: SC at 80 ft, outside the DW-centered Fireball radius. Even corrected, both DWs die R1 and SC is isolated. The fight is effectively decided before any NPC flagship ability fires. Root cause: DW HP (27) cannot survive even a half-Fireball (17 dmg from a failed save at 34÷2=17 = instant death at full HP). **Recommendation (carries FI-TP37-C, FI-TP36-B): raise DW HP to 35 (survives half-Fireball 17; dies only on failed full save 34) OR add an early-warning mechanic where DWs scatter on Verbal/Somatic components (reaction Dash on first-seen spellcasting). The current 27-HP floor guarantees R1 wipe on any Fireball.**

**FI-TP38-B [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] SC Shard-Barrage 38th consecutive zero — flagship ability never fires in threshold-patrol**
- SC tied initiative with Marwen (both +2 Dex). Marwen wins the cron tie-break; Fireball fires before SC acts. In correct positioning (SC at 80 ft, outside Fireball), SC survives R1 and can fire Barrage in R2 as a solo NPC. However, with both DWs dead, the "multiple PCs in a 15-ft line" condition still applies — Barrage against 3 PCs at 80 ft range is geometrically feasible (15-ft line from 80 ft covers a tight group). R2 solo SC firing Barrage would be its final-stand move. But the Barrage has NEVER been fired as a meaningful tactical pressure tool — it fires only as a last-stand desperation move. **The Barrage's design intent (mid-fight area denial, disrupting PC formation) is never expressed in this slice. Consider: (a) SC-explicit initiative bonus trait for this slice (+5 base initiative); (b) SC position rule that specifically puts it in the 60-80 ft corridor entrance where it would fire Barrage before party descends fully.**

**FI-TP38-C [FEEL NOTE — CARRY] Cunning Action zero — 38th consecutive TP**
- DW Cunning Action (Dash, Disengage, Hide) has never been used in 38 TP runs. The fight ends in R1-R3 before the DWs encounter conditions that trigger CA. Accept as a dead branch for this slice, or add a mid-fight fallback step where DWs retreat to a secondary choke at Round 2 to demonstrate the mechanic.

**FI-TP38-D [FEEL NOTE — CARRY] Menacing Attack frightened moot — 38th consecutive TP**
- Bazgar's Menacing Attack correctly frightened DW1 (Wis save 3, fail DC 15). Frightened DW1's crossbow shot had disadvantage and missed (min(17,9)+4=13 vs AC 15). The condition was applied and resolved correctly, but DW1 died to Fireball the same round, making the CC invisible to the table. 38 consecutive TP runs where Menacing Attack is the correct choice but produces zero observable outcome. DM cheat sheet note: Trip Attack may be more table-visible in short fights (prone condition persists if the target stays alive into allies' turns).

**SIM-TP38-A [HARNESS GAP] SC incorrectly placed in Fireball blast radius**
- SC starts 40-60 ft behind the DWs per its .md ("Position: 40-60 ft behind the front line"). DWs at 30 ft from party = SC at 70-90 ft from party. Fireball centered at DW cluster (30 ft from Marwen, 20-ft radius) covers 10-50 ft — SC at 80 ft is out of range. This sim placed SC in blast, causing incorrect insta-kill. **Fix recommendation: add explicit terrain note to the threshold-patrol slice description (or to the cron prompt's slice 0 description): "SC starts at the far column, ~65-80 ft from the party entrance; a DW-centered Fireball does NOT reach the SC."**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (49th SCT6-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T13-19)

**FI-SCT6-49-A [DESIGN DECISION — CRITICAL CARRY] Fireball decisive against shardcaller-only formation — 9th consecutive SCT cycle**
- Both Fireballs (8d6 = 26 each) either killed or mortally wounded every SC within 2 rounds. The ≥25-ft spread (FI-SCT-20-B) successfully prevents all-3-in-one-Fireball but cannot prevent 2-per-Fireball: at 40-ft diameter, two SCs within 25-30 ft of each other will both be clipped. Marwen initiative (22 in this run) means she acts before all SCs except SC-A and SC-C in R1. By the time the SCs can apply serious pressure, they are already half-dead from Fireball. This is the structural reason the shardcaller-only slice ends in R1-R2 across all 9 cycles. **Recommendation (carries from FI-SCT6-47-B, -48-C): raise SC HP to 40-45 OR add psychic/piercing resistance OR add vault terrain that forces Marwen to reposition (pillar line-of-sight splits). The spread tactic alone is insufficient against a level-5 wizard with two L3 slots.**

**FI-SCT6-49-B [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] SC-C Barrage never fires — 4th consecutive SCT cycle**
- The stagger rule (FI-SCT-34-A) assigns Barrage to the lowest-init SC in R1. SC-C (highest-init SC) multiattacks R1 and Tactical-Retreats R2 before dying to Sabriel. SC-C's Barrage slot is chronically unused when it is the highest-init SC. The stagger rule is correct tactically (distributes coverage), but it creates a structural pattern: the highest-init SC's Barrage never fires because (a) it multiattacks R1 per stagger, (b) it retreats R2 (TR takes bonus, Multiattack takes main), (c) it dies before R3. **Recommendation: consider whether SC-C should fire Barrage R1 if it has a decisive line-up (≥2 PCs in the 15-ft line from its starting position), accepting that this violates stagger but prevents the SC from being a pure Multiattack drone with a Barrage that never fires.**

**FI-SCT6-49-C [FEEL NOTE] Pack Tactics Voice has near-zero impact against this party**
- PTV triggers on melee/ranged attack hits and grants disadvantage on the target's *next* saving throw. Marwen's next action after being hit is almost always a spell (Fireball, Ray of Frost), not a saving throw. PTV would only matter if: an SC hits Marwen AND then another NPC forces a save on her before her next turn AND that happens on the same initiative slice. This requires the SCs to outlive R1, have coordinated initiative with a save-forcing NPC, and have that NPC fire before Marwen's turn. In shardcaller-only formations, there are no other NPCs. **Log as feel issue — PTV is a passive that requires either a mixed formation or a long fight to matter. The shardcaller-only slice cannot express it.**

**FI-SCT6-49-D [FEEL NOTE — CARRY] CW wasted, 9th consecutive SCT cycle**
- SC-A granted CW advantage to SC-C in R1 (correct per spec). SC-C rolled 5,6 on d20 (best=6), hitting 10 vs AC 15 — miss. CW advantage technically applied correctly, but the underlying to-hit (+4 total) requires 11+ to hit AC 15, which fails ~45% of the time even with advantage. CW has now produced 0 net hits across 9 SCT cycles. Root cause: +4 to-hit vs AC 15-19 is too weak for CW to materially shift outcomes. Either raise SC to-hit bonus or lower the CW floor (e.g., grant +2d6 bonus damage on the next hit instead of advantage on one attack roll). Carry from FI-SCT6-47-A, -48-A.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (48th SR5-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T12-00)

**FI-SR5-48-A [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] Berserk fires R1 (both ragers) — combined output 6 damage vs ~130 Fireball damage**
- R-C dispatch to-hit 18 → 1 hit (6 slashing Bazgar); R-A dispatch to-hits 5/5 → 0 hits (nat-1 equivalent ×2 even with ME+1). Total Berserk contribution: 6 damage in a fight where two Fireballs dealt ~130. The flagship melee ability of the formation is mechanically correct but emotionally hollow. Carry from FI-SR5-47-B (Berserk-R1-whiff-anticlimactic). Root cause: Fireball eliminates the fight before ragers can establish sustained melee pressure.

**FI-SR5-48-B [DESIGN DECISION — CRITICAL CARRY] Retire solo-rager-rush slice — 8th consecutive VICTORY, most lopsided result yet**
- PCs ended at 43/32/44 HP (88%/100%/100%). Only 6 total damage dealt to party. The slice has not produced a meaningful fight in 8 cycles. Every NPC mechanic (Berserk, Taunt, Madness Endurance, Incoming Damage Aggro) is either moot or irrelevant at the damage scale of Fireball+smites. **Recommendation:** Replace slice #5 with a Rager+Shardcaller or Rager+Shardcaller+Thrall composition where: (a) Taunt has non-rager targets, (b) CW can provide meaningful buffs, (c) Fireball splits attention between melee and ranged threats. A mixed formation tests the rager mechanics slice #5 was designed to evaluate, but in the context they were designed for. Carry from FI-SR5-47-A.

**FI-SR5-48-C [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] Fireball double-fire decisive, 8th consecutive SR5**
- Marwen initiative 22 (highest in fight). Two L3 slots used R1+R2. 8d6=37 and 8d6=31. No rager has survived past R3 in any SR5 cycle. Dex+0 structural floor: ~75% fail rate per rager per Fireball. At table, DM should announce slice note (carry from FI-SR5-46-C): delay Fireball to R2 or treat the first round as a "Fireball-off" scenario if you want to see Berserk/Taunt/ME express.

**FI-SR5-48-E [POSITIVE] Incoming Damage Aggro expressed correctly**
- R-C acquired aggro mark on Bazgar (via Bazgar's Action Surge attacks in R1). Berserk target 1 applied advantage → dispatch to-hit 18 = exact hit AC 18. Mechanic is functional and creates meaningful retaliatory narrative when ragers survive long enough to act. In slice #5 the fight ends too fast for the mechanic to compound, but in longer encounters (Rager+other NPCs) it will create satisfying "he remembered you" moments. No fix needed.

**FI-SR5-48-F [FEEL NOTE] R-A total Berserk whiff despite ME+1 — anticlimactic death swing**
- Even with ME+1 active (took Fireball damage), R-A rolled dispatch 5 and 5 on Berserk (equivalent to natural 1s). The "apex" ability of a dying rager dealt 0 damage and then the rager died next round. This is pure variance, not a bug, but the pattern (SR5-47-B, SR5-48-F) confirms Berserk-as-death-swing is rarely impactful. Consider whether Berserk is designed as a mid-fight ability (fresh rager) that happens to be available R1, or a desperate last-stand ability.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (46th FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T11-00)

**FI-FC46-A [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] Drain Divinity never fires — beholder-init15 > Sabriel-init8 structural gap (46th FC cycle)**
- Same root cause as 44th and 45th cycles. Beholder R1 multiattack Maw auto-crit (avg 41 piercing vs Sabriel 44 HP) downs Sabriel before her init-8 turn. R1-LA-reserve-for-DD protocol (FIX-FC40-A) is correctly executed, but the beholder's own action eliminates Sabriel before DD can fire. Three encounters designed around Sabriel (DD drain, tactics section, FIX-FC40-A) all blocked by this single init/HP interaction.
- **Recommendation:** (a) Increase Sabriel's Dex modifier to +3 (from +1) — raises average initiative to ~12, ahead of beholder ~12 in most rolls. (b) Add DM note: DD is valid against downed Sabriel (no consciousness requirement, slots persist) — beholder can drain her R2 even after she's downed (see MQ-FC46-A).

**FI-FC46-B [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] Sabriel's toolkit entirely unexpressed (46th FC cycle)**
- Downed before first action: L3 smite, L2×2 smites, LoH 25 pts, +1 longsword resistance-bypass — all unused. She functioned as an HP buffer absorbing a Maw attack that would otherwise have hit Bazgar. Valid role but her full mechanical identity never surfaces. Consistent pattern across most FC cycles.

**FIX-FC46-A [BUG AUTO-FIXED] Void Scream FRIGHTENED condition in DB but absent from .md**
- The `actions.jsonl` DB `void_scream` row has included `FRIGHTENED on a failed save` since at least the 33rd cycle. The `beholder-thrulm.md` VS action description did not document it. DMs running from the .md would never apply FRIGHTENED. Fixed: added FRIGHTENED to VS paragraph with escape mechanic (DC 16 Wis save end of each turn). Also added FIX-FC46-A context note documenting the DM interaction with prone/grappled PCs. Mechanical impact: any PC failing VS should now roll Wis saves to end FRIGHTENED each of their turns (disadv on attacks vs beholder while frightened and beholder visible).

**MQ-FC46-A [MECHANICAL QUESTION] Can DD target an unconscious/downed creature?**
- DD targets "one creature... with spell slots" — no consciousness qualifier. A downed creature at 0 HP retains its spell slots. If DD can target downed Sabriel (R2+), the beholder can strip her remaining slots even post-down, making revivify recovery less impactful (no smites left). **Recommended DM note:** add clarification to DD in beholder-thrulm.md that downed targets are valid. This resolves the structural R1 window problem — DD fires R2 on unconscious Sabriel even if she never got to act.

**MQ-FC46-B [MECHANICAL QUESTION] FRIGHTENED tracking overhead post FIX-FC46-A**
- VS FRIGHTENED now documented; DMs must track per-PC frightened status, line-of-sight to beholder, and DC 16 Wis end-of-turn saves. In this cycle: Bazgar and Marwen both failed VS (Wis 3 and 13 respectively) → both frightened for ~1 minute. Cheat sheet should note: disadv on attacks vs beholder while visible; cannot willingly move closer. Compounds with grapple and prone conditions. Low urgency to add to COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET.md.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (5th BEL3-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T10-23)

**FI-BEL3-43-A [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] void_ray outputs "each creature" but is single-target — runner limitation**
- 35th-EV7 first flagged as minor (FI-EV7-35-D). 36th-EV7 "fixed" by changing `area` field text — but the runner template generates "each creature in [area] rolls DC X save" for ANY `area` type, regardless of the area field content. No action-type currently supports a single-target save with auto-rolled damage (utility type supports single-target saves but no auto-dice; area type auto-rolls but generates multi-target language). Carry count: 5+ cycles. **Human action required:** add `single_save` action type to `scripts/dnd_roller.py`, or document void_ray as a known runner display quirk. Blast radius: dnd_roller.py only.

**FI-BEL3-43-B [DESIGN DECISION — CARRY] DD CHA save vs Sabriel: 35% fail rate, 5th consecutive BEL3 SAVE**
- R1 DD protocol executes correctly (hold all 3 LA, spend post-Sabriel). Sabriel saved CHA 18 vs DC 16. In 5 BEL3 cycles, DD has stripped a slot once (1st cycle, which had a different Sabriel CHA roll). The R1 all-3-LA-on-DD investment means the beholder gets zero legendary pressure in R1 when Sabriel saves (no tentacle LAs, no void ray, no repositioning). **DM note (already in .md):** if DD fails, resume reactive LAs from R2. No spec change needed — tactic note in beholder-thrulm.md covers the fallback. Human may decide to lower DC or raise Sabriel's CHA save vulnerability.

**SIM-BEL3-43-A [SIM GAP] Thrall R3 multiattack looped twice — Marwen death timing off by ~1 round**
- Playtest script erroneously looped the thrall's multiattack action twice in R3. Thrall has a single attack per turn (incomplete domination). Correct sim: Marwen at 8 HP entering R3, T1 hits for ~4 average → Marwen at ~4 HP, survives R3. T2 would likely finish her R3 or R4. TPK outcome unchanged (Bazgar dies R3 from Maw crit regardless). No spec issue — thrall-derro multiattack correctly specifies one attack in the DB. Sim script error to fix in future fire.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (38th SW1-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T10)

**FI-SW1-38-A [DESIGN DECISION] Reload spike: both STDs fire AR in same round when one recharges mid-round**
- R2: STD-A (used AR R1) rolled d6=6 → recharged at start of its own turn, then fired. STD-B (stagger-held R1) still had AR loaded → also fired. Both blasted the party in R2. Stagger check runs at start-of-round BEFORE recharges, so it doesn't catch the "one recharges and fires, other fires freely" edge case. Net effect: Bazgar absorbed 5+9=14 necrotic across two AR hits in R2; Marwen absorbed 5+4=9, dropping her to 13/32. The "reload spike" is mechanically legal but feels like it bypasses the stagger rule's intent. **DM clarification needed:** is double AR in one round acceptable, or should the start-of-turn checklist note "if you recharge AR at the start of your turn AND the other STD has AR loaded, you are subject to stagger this round (hold AR until next round)"?

**FI-SW1-38-B [DESIGN DECISION] Altar zone suppression invisible at table — Fireball and smites quietly halved**
- Marwen's Fireball (3rd-level slot, 8d6=22 fire): without altar zone = 22×2=44 to STD-A (dead R1), with suppression = 22 (survives). Sabriel's crit smite R2: 14 radiant → suppressed to 14 (not 28). The party won but burned premium resources at half expected value without any cue. .md already has a playtest note but no at-table narration hook. **Recommend:** add a DM narration line to the Altar Zone section: when a fire/radiant attack lands on an STD in the zone, the DM should say "the runes absorb most of the heat — it scorches but doesn't exploit the weakness" so players know to pivot. No mechanical change needed.

**FI-SW1-38-D [DESIGN DECISION] Unstable Form preference (Multiattack>AR when UF+2targets) never triggered in 38 SW1 cycles**
- The UF preference only fires when UF is active AND exactly 2 enemies are in cone. In this slice, all 3 PCs are always in range → AR wins per "3+ targets, AR wins regardless."
