The preference path has never been exercised in the simulation because the party always acts as a unit. To exercise it: either reduce the party spec to 2 active PCs per turn (e.g., Marwen hangs back ranged), or add a terrain note to the shrine-wedge setup: "Sabriel enters delayed (arrives R2) — only Bazgar and Marwen are in range R1." This would force the UF-preference branch on R2.

**MQ-SW1-38-A [MECHANICAL QUESTION] Reload spike: is double-AR-in-same-round intentional?**
- See FI-SW1-38-A. The stagger rule's documented intent is to prevent "both burning AR R1 and leaving R2+ Resonance-dry." The reload spike doesn't leave R2 dry (only the recharging STD fires; the other just had AR recharge-tested). But the combined effect is two AR blasts in one round. If unintended, fix is one line in start-of-turn checklist: "If you recharge AR AND another shrine-touched already has AR loaded this round, mark your AR as Available but hold until next round." If intentional, add a DM note: "Two AR blasts in the same round is a valid spike scenario."

**MQ-SW1-38-B [MECHANICAL QUESTION] OBR is an instruction (to-roll template), not auto-resolved**
- OBR dispatch output: "Make one Shrine-Axe melee attack against the attacker — +4 to hit, on hit 1d8+1 slashing + 1d8 necrotic." No dice rolled; DM carries out the attack manually. All other STD actions auto-resolve dice. This is likely intentional (reaction targets are dynamic — the system can't know the attacker's AC), but it creates an inconsistency at the table. Low priority. DM cheat sheet should note: "OBR: roll d20+4 vs attacker's AC manually; on hit roll d8+1+d8; self-damage 1d4 regardless."

## DESIGN DECISIONS (45th FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T03)

**FI-SCT6-48-A (2026-06-10 05:22): CW-1/9-spent-8th-consec-SCT-structural-waste**
- SC-A died holding 3 charges; SC-C died holding 3 charges; SC-B spent 1 productively (→SC-C, enabled 2 hits on Marwen). Root cause: stagger+barrage-carrier assignment leaves lowest-init SC with no CW opportunity; when any SC dies, remaining SCs lose CW targets. Recommend: allow CW self-buff when no allies available, OR add DM note that 3-SC formation CW is only productive when formation survives to R2+.

**FI-SCT6-48-B (2026-06-10 05:22): SC-C-Barrage-never-fires-3rd-consec-SCT**
- SC-C had Barrage ready all 3 rounds but never fired: R1 not carrier, R2 SC-B fired first, R3 died before acting. Recommend: clarify in .md — if R1 barrage carrier dies before acting, next-highest-init SC with Barrage may fire (stagger = prevent two SCs firing same round, not replace a dead carrier's unfired shot).

**FI-SCT6-48-D (2026-06-10 05:22): PTV-decisive-chain-POSITIVE-FIRST-across-all-SCT-cycles**
- SC-C 2-hit Marwen R1 → PTV disadv → SC-A Barrage FAIL (roll 5) → SC-B Barrage half kills Marwen. First cycle where PTV created kill chain. No change needed; DM narration opportunity.

**FI-SCT6-48-E (2026-06-10 05:22): SC-B-last-stand-Barrage-dramatic-POSITIVE**
- SC-B at 1 HP fired final Barrage before dying — killed Marwen. Recommend DM note: SC at ≤5 HP with Barrage ready fires it regardless of stagger (won't survive to R2 anyway).

**MQ-FC45-B [DESIGN DECISION] Void Eruption vs downed PCs — RAW hits all creatures in shrine zone (including dying bodies)**
- VE text: "each creature within 20 feet of the shrine" — no consciousness qualifier. RAW, a downed PC (0 HP, dying) within 20 ft of the altar takes VE damage. Each VE damage instance = 1 failed death save. The simulation excluded downed PCs from VE (only hit conscious combatants). This may have allowed Marwen's nat-20 death save to occur — if VE correctly accumulated failures on both Bazgar and Marwen in R3/R4, both would likely die before Sabriel's nat-20 mattered. **This changes TPK speed significantly**: with 2 downed PCs in the shrine zone, each VE pulse is 2 death save failures for free. DM note needed: "VE targets ALL creatures in 20 ft zone including dying PCs. DM option: waive if downed PCs have been carried to threshold (>20 ft from altar) — otherwise legal and potent."

**FI-FC45-B [DESIGN DECISION] Void Eruption lair action as final kill on last standing PC (anticlimactic)**
- Sabriel's final defeat in R3 came from VE (lair action, 7 force) not from a named beholder ability. With DR recharged and VS also recharged, the beholder had dramatic options on its main turn — but the lair action fired first (init 20) and stole the kill. **DM note to add to Lair Actions section:** "If only one PC remains conscious at ≤ 10 HP and the beholder has a main-action ability recharged (VS, DR, or grapple-Maw setup), consider holding the lair action or using Manifest Thralls instead, to preserve the initiative slot for a cinematic kill on the beholder's own turn."

**MQ-FC45-A [MECHANICAL QUESTION] DR targeting stabilized (nat-20 death save, 1 HP) PCs**
- Current DR note (FIX-EV7-30-A) says "target conscious creatures only." A nat-20 death save restores 1 HP — the PC is now conscious. The beholder correctly fired DR at Marwen at 1 HP post-nat-20. The tactics note doesn't explicitly cover this case, leaving DM confusion possible. **Recommend:** Add to beholder-thrulm.md Tactics section: "A PC stabilized at 1 HP via nat-20 death save IS a valid DR target (conscious, has a body, disintegration is meaningful). Do NOT skip DR on them."

## DESIGN DECISIONS (41st TW2-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T09)

**FI-TW2-41-A [DESIGN DECISION] Taunt+CW core combo structurally blocked when Rager rolls high initiative — 41st TW2 confirm**
- Rager init 16 (highest in fight), SC init 7 (lowest). Rager charges to melee before SC's turn. SC-to-Rager gap grows to ~50ft (> 30ft CW range). All 3 CW charges unspent. The Taunt+CW pairing is the design intent for this slice, but it can only execute if SC acts BEFORE the Rager (SC init > Rager init — roughly a coin-flip with Rager Dex +0 vs SC Dex +2). When Rager wins init (the *more threatening* scenario), the combo collapses. **Recommendation:** Add "Slow Starter" trait to Rager (always acts last in R1 initiative) OR add a note to SC tactics explicitly flagging this as the known dead zone. Carries FI-TW2-A (40th cycle) and FI-TW36-A.

**FI-TW2-41-B [FEEL ISSUE] Taunt disadv clause irrelevant — Marwen attacks Rager anyway (41st TW2 confirm)**
- Marwen failed Taunt save (9 vs DC 12). Immediately targeted the Rager with Scorching Ray — which IS the taunting creature, so no disadv applies. The mechanic only bites if Marwen prefers to shoot the SC over the Rager. She never does, because the Rager is in melee destroying Bazgar. 41 consecutive TW2 cycles: Taunt save-fail rate ~50% but disadv clause activates effectively zero times when the Rager is the primary threat. Carries FI-TW39-A.

**FI-TW2-41-C [DESIGN DECISION] Rager R1 death from alpha-strike (41st TW2 confirm) — needs explicit design intent clarification**
- Rager 52 HP died R1: 27 fire + 15 slash (sup die) + 29 radiant smite = 71 total. Minimum kill threshold (ScRay + any smite): ~56 — still a R1 kill. The Rager cannot survive focused L5 burst against 3 concentrated PCs. As "damage sponge," this fails the design role. **DESIGN DECISION for human:** (a) Bump Rager to HP 70–75; (b) Add partial damage resistance (nonmagical B/P/S only, matching beholder logic); or (c) Add explicit note "Rager is expected to die R1 in tank-wall — the SC is the sustained fight." Option (c) requires re-labeling slice intent. Options (a)/(b) require NPC stat change.

**FI-TW2-41-D [FEEL ISSUE] CW 0/3 dead weight — 41st TW2 confirm**
- All 3 CW charges unspent (Rager acted before SC, charged out of CW range). Prior cycle (40th): 1/3 spent but Rager died before collecting the advantage. Root cause: SC Dex +2 provides minimal initiative advantage over Rager Dex +0; when Rager wins init (very common), CW is functionally disabled for the entire fight. Carries FI-TW2-B and FI-TW36-A structural carry.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (40th TW-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T01)

**FI-TW2-A [DESIGN DECISION] Taunt+CW pairing never tested (slice #2 core mechanic unexecuted — 40th TW cycle)**
- The tank-wall slice exists specifically to test Taunt + Call Weakness working in concert. In this run, the Rager rolled nat 1 initiative (init = 1, last in order), was killed in R1 by Sabriel + Bazgar before it ever acted. Taunt was never used. CW was correctly fired by the SC on the Rager (Rager's turn slot was after SC's), but the Rager was killed before collecting the advantage. The slice has now run multiple TW cycles; in cycles 38 and 39, the Rager had higher init and the pairing did fire. But with DEX +0, a nat-1 roll puts the Rager last in every group. **Recommendation:** Add a "Battle Fury" initiative bonus (+3 flat) to the Rager's trait block, or bump DEX to 12 (+1). A melee tank that consistently goes last after a full party never absorbs hits — the whole role premise breaks.

**FI-TW2-B [DESIGN DECISION] CW-on-doomed-Rager: MQ-12 guard covers "already-acted" but not "will-die-before-turn"**
- MQ-12 prevents CW waste on allies who have already taken their turn. No guard exists for "this ally has very low initiative and 3 PCs act before them at full HP." When the Rager has init 1 and Sabriel (init 11), Bazgar (init 7) both act before it while the Rager is at 35 HP (post-Fireball), CW is almost certainly wasted. Consider adding a soft heuristic to SC tactics: "If the Rager's initiative is ≤5 and two or more high-damage PCs have not yet acted, hold CW until the Rager has survived to R2." Not critical — init 1 is an edge case, and R39/R38 TW cycles had SC-init > Rager-init working correctly.

**FI-TW2-C [DESIGN DECISION] Fireball-decisive pattern — 4th+ consecutive multi-slice occurrence**
- Marwen opened with Fireball (init 22) before the SC could act (init 17), halving both NPCs before any NPC mechanic fired. This has appeared in slices 0 (threshold-patrol R36), 1 (shrine-wedge R37), and now TW (R40). The pattern is structural: Marwen DEX +2 gives her init parity with the SC. When she wins the roll, L3 Fireball trivializes any 2-NPC encounter. The slice design for tank-wall does not need to change (encounter 3 is a sub-encounter; full thrulm with beholder is different), but the playtest rotation spec should add a `force_initiative_order` key so Marwen acts second at least once per 3-cycle run of each slice. Otherwise the CW + Taunt synergy is never stress-tested at the table.

**MQ-TW2-A [MECHANICAL QUESTION] Rager DEX +0 — tank role requires reliable early initiative**
- A melee tank's design intent is "absorbs hits, forces redirects, taunts." Consistent nat-1 initiative outcomes are a dice problem, not a design flaw, but DEX +0 means ~5% of runs will have the Rager going last (init 1). This is statistically rare but feels bad. Recommend: DEX 12 (+1) or a flat +2 initiative bonus from Madness Endurance ("adrenaline keeps it in the fight, always surging forward"). This would bring Rager average initiative from 10.5 to 11.5–12.5, ensuring it acts before mid-DEX PCs more often. Human to decide.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (37th SW-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T00)

**FI-SW1-A [DESIGN DECISION] Altar zone pull-away mechanic never activated — encounter setup issue**
- Both STDs remained within 60 ft of the altar throughout the fight. Fireball's 8d6 at normal damage was still decisive (24 fire — both STDs failed DC14 Dex). The suppression mechanic's hook ("pull them away to re-expose vulnerability") requires the STDs to move toward PCs = moving away from altar, OR the PCs to bait them out. In a 2-STD fight in an enclosed alcove, neither occurred. Fix is ENCOUNTER SETUP not NPC design: start STDs between the altar and the party entry point, forcing them to close toward PCs (= moving away from altar). Alternatively, add a 3rd STD or an altar-anchored minion that makes staying near the altar tactically costly for one side.

**FI-SW1-B [DESIGN DECISION] UF + AR interaction — advantage wasted when 3+ enemies override**
- When Unstable Form is active (advantage on attack rolls) and 3+ enemies are in cone, tactics currently mandate Ancient Resonance. UF's advantage is completely irrelevant on a save-based area effect. In this run: STD-B entered R2 with UF active and AR available. Firing AR wasted the UF advantage; UF Multiattack would have delivered more expected damage (2 attacks with advantage vs 3 saves at DC14 that high-Dex PCs tend to pass). DESIGN DECISION: consider adding "unless UF is also active AND all 3+ targets are also within 10 ft (melee reach), in which case UF Multiattack wins regardless of enemy count." This avoids the mechanically wasteful AR+UF combination.

**FI-SW1-C [DESIGN DECISION] Stagger rule adds 3-step overhead with diminishing payoff in short fights**
- The stagger rule requires: (1) compare both STDs' initiatives at round start, (2) pre-mark lower-init as "holding AR," (3) lock hold permanently for the round (even if higher-init fires). In this run, STD-A died end-of-R1, making the R2 stagger moot. Of 37 SW-cycle runs, the stagger hold persists into R2 only if both STDs survive — which happens infrequently when Fireball lands R1. Simplification option: "If both STDs have AR at the START of Round 1 only, the lower-init one holds. From Round 2 onwards, both fire freely." This eliminates per-round re-evaluation while preserving the anti-burst-R1 intent.

**FI-SW1-D [DESIGN DECISION] OBR self-damage contributing to UF is correct but tracking-heavy**
- The .md correctly notes that OBR's 1d4 psychic self-damage counts toward UF's 10+ threshold. In this run, STD-A's OBR self-damage (2 psychic) + Bazgar's 12 slashing = 14 total → UF triggered. The self-damage contribution was real but small (2 pts). Tracking requires a running "total damage received this round" accumulator. Consider adding a note to the combat cheat sheet: "STD total-damage-received accumulator includes OBR self-damage." No NPC change needed.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (47th SCT-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T13)

**FI-SCT6-47-A [DESIGN DECISION] CW fires zero times in SCT formation — 3rd consecutive cycle**
- Call Weakness requires 2+ SCs alive for 2+ rounds. In every SCT cycle tested: SC-C (highest init) fires Barrage R1 → no CW on that turn; SC-A and SC-B (lowest init) die to Fireball before their turns; SC-C in R2 has no alive allies to CW. The mechanic is structurally locked out by Fireball. 9/9 charges unspent this cycle.
- Recommendation: consider allowing CW as a self-buff variant (SC calls out its OWN weak point on the enemy for advantage on its next attack), or accept that CW only fires when Fireball is unavailable. If accepting, add a note to .md tactics: "CW is a sustained-combat mechanic — in Fireball encounters it will go unspent."

**FI-SCT6-47-B [DESIGN DECISION] Fireball trivially decides SCT formation — 47th consecutive SCT cycle**
- A single Fireball (DC 15, SCs Dex +2 → avg total 14 — fails by 1) deals ~28–32 fire to all 3 SCs, dropping one outright (if pre-damaged) and leaving others at 1–5 HP. The formation has no staying power vs. a level-5 Wizard. 
- Recommendation: raise SC HP from 33 to 40–45 OR add psychic resistance (thematic for derro) to give SCs one extra round of pressure application. Alternatively, the SCT slice needs a "no Fireball this encounter" DM note (tagged dungeon room, prior movement expenditure, etc.).

**FI-SCT6-47-D [DESIGN DECISION] Spread ≥25 ft rule is the formation's only anti-Fireball defense, but sim ignores it**
- The SC .md explicitly mandates ≥25 ft spread to avoid single-Fireball wipe. At-table, a 20-ft Fireball radius clips at most 1–2 SCs if spread is enforced. In the sim, all SCs are always in range. This is the single biggest gap between sim results and real-table outcomes for the SCT slice.
- Recommendation: DM must verbally announce spread positions at encounter start. Add a session-prep note to the thrulm encounter overview that SCT formation requires explicit positioning declaration.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (44th FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T11)

**FI-FC4-A [DESIGN DECISION] STD Ancient Resonance is the actual TPK mechanic — beholder signature abilities never fire in final-confrontation slice**
- In this cycle (and per prior FC cycle data), the party was TPK'd entirely by STD-B's Ancient Resonance (R2, 21 necrotic+psychic, all 3 PCs failed DC 14 DEX save). Void Scream and Disintegration Ray both went unused. The beholder spent its LA budget on a failed Drain Divinity (Sabriel CHA save 21 vs DC16 — 5th consecutive pass), used TL2 to grapple Sabriel, then acted at init 7 in R2 — but the fight ended at STD-B's turn (init 20) before the beholder ever acted in R2.
- Root cause: STD-A init 21 and STD-B init 20 both act before the beholder (init 7) in every round. With two AR-capable STDs staggered across R1/R2, they deliver 14+21=35 average psychic/necrotic damage to each PC across the two rounds — more than most L5 PCs can survive after taking any other damage.
- Recommendation (for human): the final-confrontation slice is the climactic fight. Consider ensuring the beholder has at least one dramatic action before the party falls — options include higher beholder initiative (roll with advantage on initiative, or just use a fixed init 18+), or a lair rule that the first STD AR of the encounter doesn't fire until after the beholder's R1 action. This is a fundamental design decision.

**FI-FC4-B [DESIGN DECISION] Fire vulnerability suppression is encounter-shaping (confirmed again)**
- Marwen's Fireball dealt 29 fire damage as normal (not doubled) to both STDs due to altar zone suppression. STD-A survived at 4 HP instead of going down to 0. STD-A's survival meant it got two more attacks on Bazgar in R2 (bringing him to 2 HP before STD-B's AR finished everyone). Without altar zone, STD-A would have been eliminated R1 by Fireball — materially different outcome. Mechanic is working as designed.

**FI-FC4-C [DESIGN DECISION] 2-round TPK gives insufficient dramatic arc for the climactic encounter**
- This is the second consecutive same-day FC cycle to end R2 (prior: 2026-06-08T17-24-00 also R2 TPK). The final-confrontation slice is intended as the encounter where "the death feels earned" per the briefing. A 2-round fight gives the DM no time to narrate the beholder's horror, no opportunity to showcase DR or VS, and no moment where the party makes a last-ditch dramatic choice. Consider whether PC HP or the encounter setup needs adjustment so the fight lasts at least R3–R4 with the beholder acting at least twice.

**FI-FC4-D [DESIGN DECISION] R1 LA reserve for DD leaves beholder with zero reactive impact in R1**
- The beholder held all 3 LA for post-Sabriel DD in R1 (correct per R1-LA-reserve protocol FIX-FC40-A). DD attempted and failed (Sabriel CHA save 21). Result: beholder had 0 reactive LAs during Bazgar's and Marwen's turns, and the DD was wasted. The protocol is correct in isolation, but five consecutive FC cycles show DD failing at ~35% success rate against CHA+3 Sabriel. If DD keeps failing, the protocol costs the beholder 3 reactive LAs for no benefit and no pressure on other PCs. Recommendation: add a post-DD-fail protocol — if DD misses in R1, shift to reactive Tentacle/VR from R2 onward immediately rather than attempting DD again.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (1st BEL3-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T10)

**FI-BEL3-A [DESIGN DECISION] Thrall-derro paper fragility renders action-economy-pressure test void**
- Slice #3 calls for 3× thrall-derro to apply "action-economy pressure" alongside the beholder. In practice: Marwen's R1 Fireball (8d6=35) one-shot ThrallB and ThrallC (22 HP, failed DEX saves), ThrallA limped to 5 HP and died R2 Fireball. Total thrall contribution: 1 hit on Bazgar (4 dmg). They were irrelevant.
- Root cause: 22 HP at DC 14 DEX (thrall DEX +1) means a Fireball has a ~70% chance of killing each thrall outright, and near-100% chance of dealing more than their HP on a fail. Against any party with AoE, thrall-derro are structural zeroes.
- Recommendation: bump thrall HP to 30–35, or add a mechanic (e.g., on AoE targeting, Beholder commands Fractured Will reaction to scatter — thralls move away from blast center, forcing separate targeting). Fundamental design decision; do not auto-fix.

**FI-BEL3-B [DESIGN DECISION] Beholder at 9 HP survivor — "limited" slice nearly ends in beholder death**
- Without Disintegration Ray, the beholder took 58 HP from Sabriel (three Longsword+Smite hits) and 43 HP from Fireballs = 101 total, starting at 110 HP → survived at 9 HP. Survived ONLY because Void Scream recharged on exactly 6/6 (16.7% chance) in R3.
- If VS had not recharged, Sabriel (at 5 HP) would have had another round to attack. Given she had 1 L1 slot remaining, that's another 1d8+3+2d8 radiant ≈ 15 dmg average — enough to kill the beholder.
- Log for human: the "limited" slice is already a winnable fight for the party if dice go even modestly in their favor. Intentional tension or unexpected? Confirm design intent for slice #3.

**FI-BEL3-C [FEEL NOTE] Maw auto-crit is the encounter's most decisive single-hit mechanic — needs table-warning**
- Bazgar went from 45 HP to 0 in one beholder turn: 17 bludgeoning (Tentacle 1) + 37 piercing (Maw auto-crit on grappled = 8d8+4). This is correct per FIX-FC39-A and is working as designed.
- The problem is surprise: at the table, players don't know the Maw crits on grappled targets until it happens. Losing 54 HP in one turn with no warning feels cheap. Recommend: add a narration cue when the beholder grapples ("the tendril tightens and the great maw swings hungrily toward you — this is going to hurt") so players understand the threat and can try to escape before Maw fires.

**FI-BEL3-D [DESIGN DECISION] Drain Divinity vs Sabriel CHA+3 — 35% hit rate carry**
- DD fired correctly per R1 LA hold protocol. Sabriel rolled 15+3=18 vs DC 16 → success. 35% chance of failure at CHA +3; with Paladin Aura of Protection (CHA mod to saves for allies within 10 ft), Sabriel's own effective DD save is DC 16 vs +3 = 35% fail → reliable enough to attempt. But worth confirming: at CHA +4 (likely for a level 5 paladin), fail rate drops to 30%.
- Possible alternative timing: fire DD in R3 after VS has weakened the party — Sabriel at low HP might be more risk-averse and the table atmosphere more fraught. Carry for human review.

**MQ-BEL3-B [MECHANICAL QUESTION] Beholder below-30-HP retreat — rule gap when thralls are dead**
- At end of R2, beholder hit exactly 30 HP (retreat threshold). Entered R3 at 30 HP, hit to 9 HP by Sabriel (R3 init 16) before beholder acted (R3 init 9). At 9 HP, beholder is well below threshold.
- Tactics say: "retreats deeper into lower shaft, using thralls to block pursuit." All thralls are dead. There is no rule for what the beholder does when it wants to retreat but has no thralls to block.
- Recommendation: add a sentence to Tactics: "If below 30 HP and all thralls are dead, the beholder Shrine-Drifts through the nearest solid surface toward the lower shaft each turn, spending LAs on Move to maximize distance, and fires VS/VS-recharge offensively on any PC within 30 ft en route." This prevents the DM freeze at the table.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (39th TW-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T09)

**FI-TW39-A [DESIGN DECISION] Taunt 3× zero vs Wizard Fireball — 39th-cycle carry**
- All 3 Rager Taunt uses fired on Marwen. All three bypassed by design: R1 Marwen cast Fireball (save-based, no attack roll → Taunt disadvantage clause doesn't trigger), R2 Marwen targeted the Rager directly (taunter = valid target, no disadvantage), R3 Marwen passed the save (14 vs DC 12). Net influence: zero.
- Root cause: Taunt disadvantage is conditional on attacking a non-Rager, AND only applies to attack rolls. Save-based spells (Fireball, Shatter, Thunderwave, Web) bypass it entirely regardless of target. Against a Wizard who has Fireball, Taunt is structurally near-zero.
- This is the 39th TW-cycle and the pattern has held every cycle where Marwen has 3rd-level slots. DC 12 is also too low (carry FI-TW38-C); but raising DC to 14 would still not stop Fireball.
- **RECOMMENDATION**: Add a non-attack-roll rider to Taunt — e.g., "on a failed save, the target must also use its movement on its next turn to move toward the Rager if able (Cha save to negate on that turn)" — or change the primary effect from "disadvantage on attack rolls" to "target cannot voluntarily move away from the Rager (DC 12 Str save to negate)" which constrains spellcasters more meaningfully. Human decision.

**FI-TW39-B SC solo endgame anticlimactic — 39th-cycle carry**
- After Rager fell R3, SC lasted one full round (R4) with 23 HP, missed both attacks, then was cleaned up by Bazgar. Identical pattern to FI-TW38-D (0/4 Barrage recharges in that cycle). Shard-Barrage recharge 4-6 would give SC meaningful spike threat in the solo endgame. Carry.

**SIM-TW39-A [harness gap] CW advantage cleared at round start — not a spec bug**
- Simulation script reset CW advantage flag at round start instead of at SC's next turn. In real play, Rager had advantage on Axe 1 in both R2 and R3 (CW granted by SC at end of each prior round). The 35% flip chance per round was not modeled. Outcome (Rager dying R3) likely unchanged, but Bazgar's near-death state (2 HP) might have come earlier. Flag for harness improvement.

**MQ-TW39-A Taunt text should note save-based spell bypass**
- The Taunt DB effect says "disadvantage on attack rolls against any creature other than the rager." Technically correct. At table, DMs may forget that Fireball, Shatter, etc. are saving throws, not attack rolls, and mistakenly apply Taunt disadvantage to the whole roll. Recommend adding a parenthetical to the DB effect text: "(save-based spells bypass this condition — it applies to attack rolls only)." Low priority.

**MQ-TW39-B CW timing note: SC after Rager in init is still worth spending**
- The SC tactics note says "fire CW on the Rager in R1 before it charges (when SC acts first in initiative); R2+ CW on a melee Rager is impossible." This is misleading: when SC goes AFTER Rager (as in this cycle, Rager init 20 > SC init 11), CW granted on SC's R1 still benefits Rager's R2 attack (before expiry at end of SC's R2 turn). It is not wasted. Recommend updating the note: "when SC acts after Rager, CW R1 still benefits Rager's R2 attack — spend it unless Rager is ≤10 HP and unlikely to survive to R2."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (1st SW-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T08)

**FI-SW-D UF wasted vs Sabriel AC 19 — add lowest-AC targeting to Tactics (NEW)**
- STD-Beta's Unstable Form activated (R2, 13 dmg from Shatter) but it targeted Sabriel (AC 19) — both UF-boosted attacks missed. UF's advantage is wasted against high-AC targets. The Tactics section has no explicit directive to prefer lowest-AC PC when UF is active. Recommend adding: "When Unstable Form is active, prefer the lowest-AC PC in melee reach (Bazgar AC 18 or Marwen AC 15) to maximize advantage value." Low-priority quality-of-life fix.

**FI-SW-F [DESIGN DECISION] Stagger hold for 2 consecutive rounds — too conservative?**
- STD-Beta held AR for R1 (stagger: lower init) and R2 (UF-priority: only 2 targets in cone, UF advantage is wasted on save-based AR) then fired AR normally in R3. The banked-AR arc felt satisfying but the lower-init STD effectively did no meaningful area damage for 2 full rounds. DESIGN QUESTION: should the stagger rule be relaxed so it only blocks in the SAME round both STDs recharged, not across multiple rounds? The current rule can create 2+ round suppression chains when UF-priority also applies. Consider: loosen to "only suppress stagger if BOTH STDs will fire AR in the same 6-second round; if higher-init STD was already staggered last round, release the lower-init hold." Leave for human review.

**MQ-SW-A OBR once-per-round ambiguity (NEW)**
- The .md says OBR "interrupts between attacks if the attacker has multiattack." Under D&D5e rules a creature has one reaction per round — OBR can fire at most once per round. The phrase "interrupts between attacks" is accurate (fires after hit 1, before hit 2 resolves) but could be misread as "fires on every individual hit in a multiattack." Recommend adding to OBR text: "OBR fires at most once per round per shrine-touched (one reaction per round). If the triggering attacker has multiattack, OBR fires after the first hit resolves but before subsequent attacks — the reaction is then spent until the shrine-touched's next turn."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (34th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T06)

**FI-EV7-34-A Void Scream R2 decisive — 6th consecutive EV7 confirm**
- VS R2 (31 psychic) downed Bazgar (25 HP after LoH) and reduced Marwen to 1 HP. Sabriel PASSED (roll 16+2=18). VS is the dominant kill mechanic in every EV7 run observed. Six consecutive fires; the fight ends functionally when VS lands. Carries FI-EV7-33-A. DESIGN: consider 5d10 (avg 27.5 vs 6d10 avg 33) or a per-encounter cap (1 use) to let the fight breathe past R2.

**FI-EV7-34-B DD boundary miss (NEW) — Sabriel Cha 16 = DC 16 = PASS**
- Beholder spent all 3 R1 LA on Drain Divinity; Sabriel rolled exactly 16 on her Cha save (= DC 16 = success per RAW). 3 LA wasted. ~30% occurrence rate for Sabriel build (needs 14+ on d20 with Cha +2 vs DC 16). DESIGN QUESTION: should the beholder's R1 LA protocol include a contingency when DD misses — e.g., note that 0 LA remain (DD costs all 3) and shift to reactive play from R2? Or raise DD DC to 17 to tighten the success window?

**FI-EV7-34-D Marwen-last-standing Fireball nearly kills beholder solo**
- With Bazgar and Sabriel both downed, Marwen (1 HP) survived to R4 and landed a Fireball (29 fire, beholder FAIL Dex save) reducing the beholder to 12/110 HP before being downed by Void Ray. Feel note: the "last wizard standing, nearly dead, almost wins" moment is cinematically interesting but structurally odd for a fight intended to be an overwhelming TPK. The fight dragged one round past when it felt over. DESIGN: add guidance on when to wrap narration — once 2 PCs are down the slice can be called resolved without the final round if last PC has <5 HP.

**MQ-EV7-34-A Prone + ranged attack disadvantage (NEW) — fixed in beholder-thrulm.md**
- RAW: ranged attacks (including ranged spell attacks like DR and Void Ray) against a prone target are made with DISADVANTAGE. When UG makes a PC prone, beholder's own DR and VR LA targeting that PC would be at disadvantage — working against the beholder's own action economy. Added "Prone + ranged attack note (FIX-EV7-34-A)" to UG section of beholder-thrulm.md. No DB change required. Human note: confirm at table that DM is aware of this interaction.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (46th SCT-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T05)

**FI-SCT6-46-A SC-A DOWN R1 before Barrage fires — NEW barrage-carrier vulnerability**
- The FI-SCT-34-A assignment puts the lowest-initiative SC in the Barrage carrier role. In
  this cycle, SC-A rolled init 4; Bazgar (init 15) and Sabriel (init 6) focus-fired SC-A
  to 0 HP before SC-A's turn, so R1 Barrage was never delivered.
- This is the first R1-kill of the designated Barrage SC in 46 observed cycles. The
  paradox: the Barrage carrier is the formation's most initiative-fragile combatant,
  making guaranteed R1 AoE pressure probabilistic rather than reliable.
- **Decision required:** Should the highest-init SC carry Barrage R1 (acts before PCs,
  guarantees AoE) with the tradeoff that CW timing is disrupted (CW on mid-init SC is
  the current same-round benefit ✓)? Or is probabilistic R1 Barrage acceptable as a
  balancing mechanism (high-init rolls = party survives R1 area hit; low-init rolls =
  barrage lost but formation trades it for better CW coverage)?
- Recommend: add a DM note to `derro-shardcaller.md` — "If the lowest-init SC drops
  below 10 HP from PC attacks before its R1 turn, the next-highest-init SC with Barrage
  available should fire on its turn even if another SC already Barrageged. The R1 Barrage
  is more valuable than stagger-compliance when the carrier is about to die."
  Actually, the current stagger note only prevents *double* Barrage — the R2/R3 stagger.
  This is specifically about the R1 carrier being killed. Since SC-A never fired, there
  was no "stagger violation" possible. A pre-emptive note about fallback is still useful.

**FI-SCT6-46-B VICTORY R3 comfortable — 47th consecutive SCT-only confirm**
- Bazgar 40/49 · Marwen 17/32 · Sabriel 21/44. No deaths. Marwen at 53% most stressed.
- Carry FI-SCT6-45-B. 3-SC formation consistently resolved in 3–5 rounds without PC
  deaths. Formation serves as a harassment encounter, not a threat. Add 1× Thrall (8 HP
  minion to absorb focus fire) or raise SC HP to 40 to create a real death threat.

**FI-SCT6-46-C Fireball init-gating in SCT — structural pattern**
- Marwen last in initiative (init 4). By time Marwen acts in R1, SC-A is dead →
  2 targets remain → 3-SC Fireball requirement not met → Scorching Ray instead.
- This is a structural feature of: (a) SC spread tactic (no cluster for Fireball
  line), (b) Marwen consistently rolling low init. If Marwen rolls ≥15 init, she acts
  before focus fire depletes targets. DM at-table note: "remind Marwen player that
  Fireball is most useful R1 before the party breaks formation; if Marwen acts late,
  switch to Scorching Ray by default."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (45th SR5-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T04)

**FI-SR5-45-A Taunt mechanically inert in solo-rager-rush — 5th consecutive SR5 confirm**
- In any formation where ONLY ragers are present, Taunt's "disadvantage on attacks vs
  non-rager targets" clause never triggers because all valid attack targets ARE ragers.
  The bonus action fires, a save is rolled, and zero game-mechanical constraint results.
- Recommendation: add a DM-facing note in derro-rager.md Tactics section: "In a
  rager-only formation, Taunt disadv is moot — DM may narrate as flavor but need not
  track the save." A mechanical fix (secondary clause for all-melee scenarios) would
  require a balance decision from the human.

**FI-SR5-45-B Triple Berserk R1 narration repetition — new**
- When all 3 ragers have Berserk available at initiative start and 3 PCs are in melee
  range, all 3 fire Berserk in the same round, printing the same narration text 3 times.
  This dulls impact. Consider adding 2 alternate narration strings to the Berserk spec
  so DMs see variety across a multi-rager turn.

**FI-SR5-45-C Fireball structural counter — carry from FI-SR5-44-E / FI-SR5-L**
- 5th consecutive SR5 cycle where Fireball ×2 is the decisive element. Rager Dex+0
  means nearly all ragers fail DC 14 saves. Recommend adding a slice-level DM note:
  "For richer Berserk/Taunt/ME expression, encourage Marwen to hold Fireball until
  R2 so R1 Berserk fires at full HP and the rager identity is established first."

**MQ-SR5-45-A Incoming Damage Aggro "next attack" ambiguity — clarification needed**
- The .md says "the next attack the rager makes against that enemy has advantage."
  In multiattack (2 swings vs the same target), should advantage apply to both rolls
  or only the first? Strict reading of "next attack" = one roll.
- Recommend: update derro-rager.md to read "The **first** attack roll the rager makes
  against that enemy on its next turn has advantage." This eliminates table ambiguity
  and is a minor nerf (second swing loses advantage after a miss on the first).

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (43rd FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T03)

**FI-FC43-A: VS-recharged-R3-not-fired — 3rd consecutive FC cycle (carry FI-FC42-B)**
Void Scream recharged at R3 start (rolled 6) but the fight resolved via beholder Multiattack + STD shrine-axe before VS could swing. This is the 3rd consecutive slice-4 cycle where VS fails to fire before TPK. P(VS fires in a 3-round fight at Recharge 6) = 1 - (5/6)^3 ≈ 42%. We're falling in the 58% non-fire zone consistently. VS is the beholder's highest-drama area effect; not firing means the encounter ends without its most distinctive moment. **Carry: strong recommend changing VS to Recharge 5-6 (P-fire 3-round ≈ 70%). Human decision.**

**FI-FC43-B: Beholder zero damage — 43rd FC structural note**
Beholder took 0 damage across 3 rounds: Bazgar spent turns downed/prone (no offensive contribution); Marwen fired Fireball at thralls (correct) and died before beholder range; Sabriel missed her only longsword attempt (roll 15 vs AC 17). For a CR13 vs level-5 encounter this is expected. However, combined with grapple+Maw-autocrit as the primary kill engine, it means the encounter's full ability palette (DR, VS, legendary resistance, antireality, DD slot-attrition) is never stress-tested in a 3-round fight. The beholder demonstrates overwhelming force correctly but the DM has no feedback on which *defensive* abilities matter.

**FI-FC43-C: STD-A killing blow — DM narration note**
Sabriel was downed by STD-A shrine-axe (roll 21 vs AC 19, 17 slashing+necrotic) in R3, not by the beholder. In 2 of 3 slice-4 cycles run this week, a non-beholder NPC delivers the killing blow. This is mechanically correct (STDs are CR3 and have real multiattack DPS) but may feel narratively flat at the table. **DM note: when an STD lands the killing blow in the final confrontation, narrate it as the shrine's ambient will striking through its instrument, not a separate agent — "The altar's chorus reaches out through the creature's arms..."**

**FI-FC43-D: LoH yoyo — 10th FC cycle confirm, DD interaction gap**
Sabriel spent all 25 LoH reviving Bazgar twice (R1 +15, R2 +10). Both revivals were neutralized within 1 round (STD double-AR R1 downed Bazgar again; Tentacle+Void Ray reduced Bazgar to 3 HP R2; beholder Tentacle re-downed R3). DD correctly stripped Sabriel's L3 slot R1 (Cha save 4 vs DC16), but Sabriel used LoH (a separate HP pool) rather than spell slots throughout. DD's anti-divine effect was partially wasted. **DESIGN QUESTION: should Drain Divinity also drain LoH uses? As written, DD only targets spell slots. At L5, Sabriel has 25 LoH that DD cannot touch — the anti-divine counter ignores this entire resource pool.**

**FI-FC43-E: DR never recharged (30% probability)**
Disintegration Ray did not recharge in 3 rounds. P(no recharge at Recharge 5-6 in 3 rounds) = (4/6)^3 ≈ 30%. This run landed in that 30%. DR is the encounter's most dramatic "earn the loss" ability (disintegrate → no body); when it doesn't fire, the TPK is clean but lacks the visceral narrative spike. **No fix — the math is correct for CR13. Log as context: 30% of 3-round FC fights will not see DR.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (3rd-BEL3-cycle additions — 2026-06-09T02)

**FI-BEL3-3RD-A: INDETERMINATE — near-win validates slice intent**
All 3 PCs downed with 2 death-save failures by R3; beholder at 9/110 HP retreating. Tense outcome: Marwen's Scorching Ray brought beholder to 9 HP but she was downed same round by beholder's Multiattack. Without disintegration ray, the party got very close. Slice intent ("tests legendary economy, lair actions, action-economy pressure") is validated — the fight ran to natural exhaustion. No fix needed.

**FI-BEL3-3RD-B: Maw auto-crit to-hit ambiguity — NEW, recommend .md note**
Simulation applied Maw auto-crit without requiring a to-hit roll when the target was grappled. By RAW (PHB: crits apply on a hit), the attack still requires a to-hit roll; on a hit, it scores a critical hit. If the to-hit roll is required, R1 Maw vs Bazgar (AC 18, +7 attack) has ~55% hit rate. Missing Maw means Bazgar survives R1 at ~37 HP — fight runs longer. **Recommend adding to beholder-thrulm.md Maw action text: "Roll to hit normally (+6); on a hit against a grappled target, automatically score a critical hit (8d8+3 avg 39 piercing)."** Human decision: keep auto-crit-no-roll (more brutal, clear mechanical identity) or require to-hit (RAW, slightly more swingy).

**FI-BEL3-3RD-C: VS never recharged — 3rd-BEL3 consecutive non-fire (carry FI-BEL3-38-B)**
Void Scream recharge 6 = 16.7% per round. In a 3-round fight: 42% chance it never fires. All 3 BEL3 cycles have seen VS not fire (cycle 1: not attempted; cycle 2: dice failure; cycle 3: dice failure). VS is the beholder's highest-impact area ability but has effectively zero presence in slice 3. Recharge 5-6 (33% per round) raises 3-round fire probability to ~70%. **Carry recommendation: change VS to recharge 5-6.**

**FI-BEL3-3RD-D: LoH yo-yo — 3rd-BEL3 structural confirm (carry FI-EV7-33-C)**
Bazgar downed twice, revived twice at 1 HP. Sabriel's bonus actions fully consumed by LoH rather than combat. Net Bazgar contribution: ~11 damage at cost of 2 Sabriel bonus actions. Root cause: Maw auto-crit from grappled state (avg 39 piercing) exceeds Bazgar's HP in 2 hits; reviving to 1 HP gives one action before next down. Table note: revive to 5+ HP to give Bazgar meaningful agency, or Sabriel holds LoH for emergencies rather than reflexive revives.

**FI-BEL3-3RD-E: Thralls trivially low DPS — 3rd-BEL3 confirm (carry FI-BEL3-38-C)**
Total thrall damage: ~9 HP across 3 rounds. Manifest Thralls reaction attacks all missed. At +1 hit vs AC 18-19, expected damage per thrall per round is ~0.6 HP. Three thralls = ~1.8 expected HP/round. This is below noise level. **Carry: DM should override Manifest Thralls lair to Unstable Ground when party AC ≥ 18 (already noted FIX-BEL3-38-A). Human decision on thrall to-hit stat bump.**

**FI-BEL3-3RD-F: Fireball thrall splash not modeled — sim gap, real table matters**
Sim targeted Fireball only at beholder. At table: Fireball 20-ft radius easily catches altar + clustered thralls. Thralls (22 HP, Dex +1, no proficiency) likely fail DC 14 save and take full 8d6 ≈ 28 avg fire — all 3 wiped R1. This changes the encounter: no thralls from R2 onward, beholder lair pivots immediately to UG/VE, Manifest Thralls becomes void. **DM note: position thralls spread at encounter start to prevent single-Fireball wipe.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (33rd EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T22)

**FI-EV7-33-A: VS-R2-decisive — 5th consecutive EV7 confirm**
Void Scream fires R2 as standard; 35 psychic downs Bazgar (25 HP) and Marwen (3 HP after VR-LA) simultaneously. Beholder at 72/110 when fight ends R3 — Disintegration Ray, Drain Divinity (post-fire), and Antireality never expressed. Consistent across EV7-28 through EV7-33. **Carry FI-EV7-32-C / FI-EVD-7-A.** DESIGN: consider 1×/encounter cap or reduce to 5d10 max so fight survives to R3+. Human decision.

**FI-EV7-33-B: DD fires but is inert — L3 stripped, Sabriel dies before smiting**
DD fires correctly post-Sabriel R1 (Cha save 3, catastrophic fail). L3 slot stripped. Sabriel downs Beholder for 10 HP (atk 2 hit) then is killed R3 with L2 smites untouched. Anti-smite counter works mechanically but doesn't affect fight trajectory — Maw + VS dominate before smites matter. **Carry FI-EV7-32-B, FI-FC41-A, FI-FC42-C.** DESIGN QUESTION (carry): should DD target LoH pool (25 pts vs Sabriel) instead of / in addition to spell slots? LoH is the more impactful revive resource at L5.

**FI-EV7-33-C: LoH-yoyo — 3rd EV7 confirm**
Sabriel spends 25 LoH to revive Bazgar after R1 Maw auto-crit. Bazgar re-downed by VS R2 on same HP total (25). LoH resource fully wasted. Pattern confirmed EV7-31, EV7-32, EV7-33. **Carry FI-EV7-32-F.** Recommend: add encounter/tactics note — "vs beholder solo, Sabriel should conserve LoH until after VS fires; reviving R1 feeds the LoH-yoyo."

**FI-EV7-33-E: Antireality structural zero — 33rd cycle**
No Antireality opportunity. Beholder acts init 17; only Bazgar (init 19) acts before beholder. Bazgar's resisted attacks (4+6=10 HP) not worth spending reaction on even if triggered. **Carry MQ-EV7-30-B.** DESIGN: +2 AC blocks ~10% of L5 attacks (rolls 10-11 only on d20+7 vs AC 17). Recommend redesign to +4 AC or "reaction: gain advantage on next saving throw" or similar higher-value trigger. Human decision.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (45th SCT-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T19)

**FI-SCT6-45-A: CW-waste structural (3/9 productive) — 3rd consecutive SCT cycle carry**
SC-A died R2 with 3/3 CW charges unspent (held per MQ-12 / FI-SCT-34-A; no eligible target before death). SC-C spent 1 charge on a Barrage (wasted — stagger violation, should not have fired). SC-B spent 2 charges productively (CW→SC-A R1, CW→SC-C R2). 3/9 productive charges is identical to the 44th cycle pattern. The 3-SC stagger protocol structurally prevents CW from flowing when any SC dies before mid-round. **Recommendation (carry FI-SCT6-44-A):** Add tactics note — if middle-init SC falls R2, lowest-init SC should close range toward the surviving high-init SC to stay within 30ft CW range rather than retreating. Human decision on whether this weakens kiting feel.

**FI-SCT6-45-B: VICTORY R4 — 5th consecutive SCT cycle. SC-only formation consistently loses.**
Across 43rd/44th/45th cycles and multiple earlier ones, the Compass Edge defeats 3× shardcallers in 3–5 rounds. The party ends bloodied (all three at <40% HP in 45th cycle) but never near TPK. If this slice is meant to be a preamble/scouting encounter, the outcome is correct. If it's meant to threaten the party, it needs either: (a) +1 thrall-derro as a melee screen to protect SCs from early close-in, or (b) reduce to 2× SC + 1× rager (tank-wall formation already tested). **Human decision required.**

**FI-SCT6-45-C: SC-C untouched through R3 — solo endgame anticlimactic**
SC-C survived at full HP (33) through three rounds because party focused SC-A (melee threat) and SC-B (high init, Scorching Ray). SC-C's solo R4 endgame: no Barrage (2× recharge failures d6=4,4), Multiattack missed both throws each round, died to a single hit. No dramatic moment. **Recommendation:** Drop retreat threshold from 12HP to 5HP when SC-C is the last SC standing — let it fight from current position instead of retreating into an empty corridor. The retreat behavior is designed for when allies are alive to benefit from range coverage; solo, it just delays the inevitable.

**MQ-SCT6-45-A: Stagger + defensive-fire creates CW assignment ambiguity**
When SC-A fires Barrage as a defensive action at initiative 11 (melee-threatened → TR → Barrage), the SC-B CW assignment was SC-A per R1 protocol. But SC-A becomes a Barrage-firer mid-round, meaning SC-B CW'd a Barrage-firer (advantage wasted). The assignment cannot be predicted pre-round when defensive fires occur. **Recommendation:** Revise CW rule to: "CW targets the highest-initiative ally who has NOT yet fired Barrage this round, evaluated at bonus-action time." This is dynamic (evaluated at the moment of spending) rather than pre-assigned, eliminating the defensive-fire edge case.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (42nd FC-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T17)

**FI-FC42-A: STD-B multiattack front-loads 23 damage on Marwen before the beholder acts — shrine-touched as R1 primary threat**
STD-B (init 20, stagger holding AR) fired two Shrine-Axe hits on Marwen: 10 + 13 = 23 slashing+necrotic, dropping her from 32→1 HP. The CR2 shrine-touched inflicted more first-round damage than the CR13 beholder (which missed TL1 and missed Maw vs AC19). When both STDs roll init 20 and one fires AR while the other focused-fires a caster, the shrine-touched pair outpaces the boss's R1 contribution. This isn't wrong — STDs are meant to be dangerous — but the table perception risk is real: players will track the beholder as the primary threat and be surprised a minor NPC nearly tabled the wizard before round 1 ended. **DESIGN DECISION:** Add a DM cheat-sheet note that STDs may outpace the beholder in R1 by design; the beholder's R1 role is grapple-setup and LA-conservation for DD, not immediate kill pressure. Also note that lair-loses-ties means all three (STD-A, STD-B, lair action) at init 20 fire before the beholder (init 19), creating a brutal multi-threat opening sequence where the boss hasn't even acted yet. DMs should telegraph this to players who walk in expecting boss-first pacing.

**FI-FC42-D: VS Frightened rider in DB spec not reflected in .md stat block — human decision required (4th consecutive FC cycle)**
The `void_scream` DB dispatch has included: *"On a FAILED save, target is also FRIGHTENED of the beholder for 1 minute (DC 16 Wis save ends at end of each of the target's turns)."* The beholder-thrulm.md Void Scream entry has NO Frightened condition — it lists only 6d10 psychic damage and the WIS save. This discrepancy has appeared in cycles 39, 40, 41, and 42 without resolution. The combat runner's DB is authoritative for dispatch narration, so the runner correctly narrates Frightened on VS failures; the DM reference (.md) does not. **Human decision required:** (a) Add Frightened rider to .md VS entry — balance note: Frightened + can't-approach is punishing vs melee fighters (Bazgar's entire identity is melee); recommend including a parenthetical DM note that the Frightened condition makes the beholder's own movement irrelevant (beholder already hovering at altitude); or (b) Remove Frightened from the DB void_scream spec if this was an authoring accident. Until resolved, runner narrates Frightened on every VS hit. Carry from FI-FC39-B / MQ-FC39-B / MQ-FC40-A-chain.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (37th TW-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T15)

**FI-TW37-A: CW + Berserk — first valid expression in 37 TW cycles (POSITIVE)**
SC got initiative 21 vs Rager 7 (FI-TW36-A lockout broken). CW fired R1 and R2, granting Rager advantage on Berserk R1 and Multiattack R2. Result: nat-20 crit R1 (17 slashing, Bazgar 49→16) and nat-20 crit + hit R2 (14+12=26 slashing, Bazgar -10 DOWN). CW intent is validated — the ability does work when initiative is favorable and produces a set-piece moment (Rager chains crits, front-liner goes down). The 1-in-37 express rate is a feature of the initiative lottery, not a bug. No change needed. Positive data point — log and carry.

**FI-TW37-B: VICTORY R6, 7th consecutive TW — Bazgar-down adds tension but formation still non-threatening**
First TW cycle with a PC falling (Bazgar). Marwen (6 HP) and Sabriel (33 HP) survived comfortably. Fight went 6 rounds due to SC harassing Marwen under Taunt and Barrage recharge R5. CW+crit combo is the most dangerous sequence seen in TW slice but still doesn't threaten TPK. Carry FI-TW36-B: consider adding 1× Thrall-Derro to provide SC protection and a second threat vector vs Sabriel's flank. Human decision.

**FI-TW37-C: Taunt-disadv mechanic confirmed working (MQ-TW36-C RESOLVED)**
Taunt landed on Marwen R2 (save 6 vs DC12). Under Taunt: Fire Bolt at SC suffered disadvantage (R5 miss, R6 miss); Marwen burned 2×L1 Magic Missile slots to bypass Taunt (MM is not an attack roll → no disadv). The resource trade — burn a slot or accept disadv — is exactly the intended Taunt pressure. Already documented in derro-rager.md (note added prior cycle). Confirmed working as designed. MQ-TW36-C CLOSED.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (slice #1 shrine-wedge — 2026-06-08T14)

**DESIGN-SW-A: Altar zone suppresses BOTH damage levers simultaneously**
Both STDs start in altar zone by default → fire AND radiant vulnerability suppressed for full fight. Marwen (Scorching Ray/Fireball) and Sabriel (Divine Smite) both lose their strongest damage modifiers simultaneously. In run: Marwen's rays dealt normal fire (not doubled); Sabriel's smites dealt normal radiant. Combined effect: party's two highest-burst sources both neutered. Recommend: default shrine-wedge slice should start 1-of-2 STDs at 70+ ft from altar (outside zone) so party has at least one valid radiant/fire target; OR add explicit DM signaling in COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET so the table knows to drag STDs out of zone.

**DESIGN-SW-B: AR recharge die untested in ≤3-round fights**
With 2 STDs and stagger rule, each STD fires AR at most once before a typical fight ends. The 5-6 recharge is never stress-tested. Only matters if ≥1 STD survives to R3+, which didn't happen this cycle. Recharge math effectively irrelevant for the 2-STD shrine-wedge slice.

**DESIGN-SW-C: OBR self-damage accelerates STD death vs melee parties**
Each OBR fire costs 1d4 psychic self-damage regardless of outcome. STD-A took 3× OBR packets in R1 (~7 HP self-damage total), accelerating its death. Against melee-heavy parties (Bazgar + Sabriel both in melee), OBR fires 2-3×/round and the STD chips itself down. Monitor across future melee-heavy slices to assess whether STDs feel too fragile.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (33rd TP-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T12)

**FI-TP33-B — CW + DW Hand Axe +2 structural miss vs AC18/19 (33rd-cycle confirm, carry FI-TP32-B, FI-TP-1).**
In 33 simulated cycles of slice #0, Call Weakness → Deep Watch Derro has never produced a decisive hit against Bazgar (AC18) or Sabriel (AC19). With advantage on +2 to-hit: per-swing hit rate is ~40% (threshold ~43%). This cycle: CW fired correctly on DW-B (pre-turn, HP>20), CW advantage active, best of two rolls was 9 — still MISS. The mechanical identity of the DW pair ("trained formation fighters who hit harder together") is completely absent at the table. **DESIGN DECISION:** Either (a) raise Hand Axe to +4 to hit (add proficiency properly — currently Hand Axe shows +2 which is _already_ including proficiency per the stat block; STR is 10/+0, so +2 is correct; the actual issue is STR+0 producing minimal weapon contribution), OR (b) retarget both DWs onto Marwen (AC15) where their hit rate becomes ~75% per swing and the Tactical Drilling formation matters. Option (b) is the no-stats-change fix.

**FI-TP33-C — Shardcaller one-shot by L2 smite in R1, never survives to R2 (33rd-cycle structural confirm).**
Sabriel's L2 Divine Smite on a single hit = 3d8 radiant (avg 13.5) + d8+3 weapon (avg 7.5) = avg 21 damage, max ~40. The Shardcaller has 33HP. A single L2 smite hit puts SC below half HP; a good roll kills outright. In this cycle: 28+10=38 damage on the first two attacks → SC 33→-5. The SC's rotation design assumes: R1 Barrage, R2 recharge check, R2+ CW + multiattack. That rotation never executes because SC dies R1 in every cycle where Sabriel's initiative is ≤ SC's. **DESIGN DECISION:** Raise SC HP from 33 to 45–50 to survive one smite and act in R2. Alternatively, SC tactics could explicitly note: if Sabriel is targeting SC, trigger TR (Tactical Retreat) as bonus action to put distance. But TR is 30ft movement, not avoidance of an already-declared attack.

**FI-TP33-D — Tactical Drilling expresses but delivers no meaningful damage (33rd confirm, carry FI-TP31-C).**
This cycle: TD fired once (DW-A R1), reroll improved 16→20, HIT for 3 slashing. Net result: 3 damage. The second TD opportunity (DW-B R1) couldn't rescue the +2 base to-hit: reroll 8→9, still MISS. Tactical Drilling is the Deep Watch's signature trait but its table presence is invisible — the party never reacts to it. **DESIGN DECISION:** Either the DW hit bonus needs a floor (see FI-TP33-B), or the TD mechanic should escalate (e.g., when both DWs have advantage via TD and both hit the same target, deal +2d6 bonus damage as a coordinated strike). Currently TD is equivalent to "sometimes advantage that doesn't matter."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (32nd EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T11)

**FI-EV7-32-A — Negotiation reached the ultimatum stage for the first time.**
Sabriel's aura concealment passed DC 15 (barely: 15/15). Bazgar's follow-up failed DC 18 (7). The beholder issued: *"place your weapons down or become clay."* Sabriel refused — in-character, dramatic, correct. This is the first EV7 cycle where the negotiation got past the "beholder senses divinity → instant combat" collapse and reached a genuine impasse. The feel was good. **DESIGN DECISION:** The negotiation DCs are currently ad-hoc (not in the .md). Recommend adding a Negotiation Terms subsection to Interaction Notes with formal DC structure and collapse conditions. Suggested: DC 12 to establish non-hostile intent, DC 15 to conceal divine presence, DC 18 for the weapon-disarm ultimatum follow-through, DC 22 for passage-bargain.

**FI-EV7-32-B — DD fired and stripped a smite slot, but the slot was irrelevant (second confirm).**
Sabriel's Cha save was 12 < DC 16; L2 smite stripped. But Sabriel ended up at 8 HP by R3 and never smited anything. The DD anti-smite logic requires the fight to last long enough for Sabriel to close and swing. VS R2 prevents that. Carry question: **should DD also target the LoH pool?** In this run, Sabriel used LoH twice (30 HP healing total). The beholder's rationale (hates divine energy) extends to LoH equally. Would make DD more impactful in the R2–R3 window.

**FI-EV7-32-C — VS R2 as primary TPK engine: 4th EV7 confirm.**
36 psychic, all 3 saves fail, Marwen DOWN. The pattern is consistent: DD strips R1, VS fires R2, party either retreats or dies R2–R3. This is the intended shape of the encounter. No design change needed; the finding is a data point confirming the beholder's ability rotation works as designed.

**FI-EV7-32-D — Multiattack all-miss R1 is structural at threshold range.**
TL reach = 10 ft; party enters from threshold at 40+ ft. The beholder's melee kit (TL + Maw auto-crit) is never reachable in R1 without the party advancing. Three consecutive EV7 cycles confirm 0/3 multiattack hits R1. **DESIGN CONSIDERATION:** Is the melee kit accessible in the full encounter (slice #4, #5)? In final-confrontation slices, the beholder has thralls who push PCs into range. In solo (EV7), the beholder must advance or wait. The grapple+Maw crit has never expressed in the empty-void slice across 32 cycles.

**MQ-EV7-32-A — Retreat outcome: first EV7 RETREAT across 32 cycles.**
Prior EV7 cycles ended TPK (R2–R7). This run ended RETREAT. The beholder's territorial behavior (doesn't pursue past threshold) means a party with one PC alive can always escape. **DESIGN QUESTION:** Should the beholder fire a parting VS when the party retreats, or is the territorial no-pursuit the correct flavor? If retreat is always possible, the "deadly" encounter rating may be inflated — the party can always choose retreat over death. Recommend adding a "parting behavior" note to Tactics: e.g., "if party retreats, beholder fires Void Ray (1 LA) as a parting shot, then returns to altar."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (43rd SRR-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T09)

**FI-SR5-I — Initiative bimodal: 3rd consecutive confirm. Encounter outcome is entirely initiative-determined.**
Marwen init 21 (highest) → Fireball before any rager acts → VICTORY R2. 42nd cycle: same result (VICTORY R6, different seed dynamics). 41st cycle: ragers init 20/17/16 → triple Berserk before PCs act → TPK R5. The solo-rager-rush slice has no mechanical floor. When ragers win initiative, 34 dmg lands on Bazgar before party acts; when Marwen wins, all ragers drop to half HP before any attack. There is no middle-state scenario where both sides trade blows.

Options for the human to consider: (a) remove Berserk from R1 availability (ragers must spend R1 charging; Berserk available R2+); (b) stagger entry so only 1–2 ragers start in range R1; (c) accept the initiative variance as flavor ("these fights are unpredictable").

**FI-SR5-K — Taunt zero-influence: 3rd confirm across 43rd, 42nd, 41st cycles.**
Taunt fires every rager bonus action (3–4 fires per run). Results this cycle: Marwen failed R1 but Fireball already spent; Marwen passed R2; Sabriel passed R1. Even when Marwen fails, she bypasses with Magic Missile (no attack roll). Bazgar is attacking the taunting rager anyway — Taunt has zero effect on him in melee. Sabriel Cha +3 resists reliably.

Taunt is mechanically hollow against this party. Two options: raise DC to 13–14 (catches Sabriel and Marwen more consistently), or extend effect to "disadvantage on any saving throw against targets other than the rager" (covers spells). At current DC 12, Taunt functions roughly as a bonus-action filler that accomplishes nothing. Human decision.

**FI-SR5-L (new) — Fireball structurally bypasses all rager mechanics. Slice note needed.**
Rager design mechanics (Berserk, Taunt, Madness Endurance, Aggro-Mark) all require sustained melee contact to express. Fireball eliminates that by dropping all three ragers to half HP before any reaches the party, then the party finishes them in 1–2 more rounds. The ragers' Dex +0 and 52 HP mean Fireball is essentially guaranteed to halve their HP. No rager mechanic fires in a meaningful way when Fireball leads.

This is an AoE suppression issue, not a spec bug. Recommend adding a DM note to the slice-5 spec (in `thrulm-rotation.json` or the briefing): "For this slice to stress-test rager mechanics, Marwen should use cantrips R1 and hold Fireball until a rager is in melee range, or until her allies are being threatened." Document as a table-usage note, not a balance fix.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (1st BEL3-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T07)

**FI-BEL3-S3-A — Maw auto-crit one-shots Sabriel (44 HP → -12 in R2). Spike feel question.**
TL2 grappled Sabriel (AC19, 25 to-hit), Maw auto-critted for 8d8+4 = 41 piercing. 8d8+4 avg is ~40; ~45% chance to one-shot a 44 HP PC in a single hit. The grapple→maw chain is the beholder's flagship lethal spike and is working as designed. For DM guidance: narrate this as a "the moment the fight turns" beat, not a silent roll. Consider a tactics note: "When the maw auto-crit drops a PC, narrate it explicitly — this is Thrulm's killing blow and should feel earned."

**FI-BEL3-S3-B — DD fires (R1 reserve protocol works) but slot stripped goes unused. Structural tension.**
DD successfully stripped Sabriel's L3 smite slot R1 (Cha 5 < DC16). But Sabriel missed all attacks R1 and died to maw-crit R2 before any smite. The counter fired at the right time (protocol confirmed) but the encounter resolved too fast for the anti-smite counter to matter. Either accept as variance (DD sometimes doesn't pay off because maw kills faster) or reduce maw crit variance (lower auto-crit to 2× normal instead of doubling all dice). Human decision.

**FI-BEL3-S3-C — Thralls contribute near-zero vs AC18/19 party. Role clarification needed.**
3 thralls survived all 3 rounds; total damage 29 over ~27 attacks (33% hit rate, concentrated on Marwen AC15). Thralls can't threaten Bazgar (AC18) or Sabriel (AC19) meaningfully. If their role is "soften Marwen" (the squishiest PC), they work — Marwen took 20 dmg from thralls by end of R2. If their role is "melee backup" or "action-economy flood," they fail — they never threaten armored PCs. Recommend: document intended role in `_overview.md` or the beholder tactics section, so DMs know whether thralls should be directed at casters (soft targets) or simply act as meat shields between the party and the beholder.

**FI-BEL3-S3-D — 2/3 PCs eliminated in one beholder turn (TL-grapple + maw-crit + LA void-ray). Economy snapshot.**
R2 beholder turn: TL2 hits Sabriel (grappled) → Maw auto-crit kills Sabriel → LA Void Ray kills Marwen. This is within the rules and intended (CR13 vs L5 = overwhelming), but noting that the legendary action economy makes the "one-shot two PCs in a turn" pattern repeatable. The 3 LA per round gives the beholder a second attack-equivalent most turns. This is the encounter working as designed: log for human to assess feel at table.

**MQ-BEL3-S3-A (carry MQ-SW35-A, 56th consecutive sandbox cycle) — Local RNG fallback needed.**
Both quantumnumbers and random.org blocked in Anthropic cloud sandbox. Cache pre-seeded with `secrets.randbelow(65536)` before each fire. Recommend: add `DND_ROLLER_LOCAL_FALLBACK=1` env var in `dnd_roller.py` that uses `os.urandom` when external APIs fail, bypassing the RuntimeError. Alternative: document the pre-seeding step in the briefing Setup section.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (37th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-10T14)

### FI-EV7-7-A: beholder-thrulm — Shrine-Drift movement stacking vs fly speed is ambiguous (2026-06-10T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-10 14:00 UTC, slice #7 (empty-void) cycle 37. The beholder has base fly speed 30 ft. Shrine-Drift is a bonus action that says "Move up to 30 ft." If Shrine-Drift is additive to the fly speed (total 60 ft/turn), the beholder can close from the altar (~60 ft from threshold) to the party in a single turn, putting all PCs inside Void Scream's 30-ft radius immediately. If Shrine-Drift *replaces* the normal fly movement (same pool), the beholder reaches only 30 ft and VS clips only advanced party members.
- **Finding:** The .md never clarifies whether Shrine-Drift is an additional 30 ft or a replacement for the fly speed. This gap means sims and DMs may assume different ranges. The difference is dramatic: at 60 ft/turn, the beholder always brings VS into full-party range on R1; at 30 ft/turn, the party threshold position gives 1 round of safety. Positioning guidance in the Tactics section assumes the beholder "starts near the altar" — but the mobility math is unspecified.
- **Recommendation:** Add one sentence to Shrine-Drift: "This movement is in addition to the beholder's base fly speed (total 60 ft of movement available on its turn when Shrine-Drift is used as a bonus action)." OR "The beholder's only movement is Shrine-Drift (30 ft); it does not have a separate fly-speed move action." **Human decision required — affects all tactical range and VS-coverage calculations.**

### FI-EV7-7-B: beholder-thrulm multiattack — Maw dispatcher output shows non-crit damage; auto-crit visibility gap (2026-06-10T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-10 14:00 UTC, cycle 37. R1 multiattack: T2 grappled Sabriel, then Maw hit grappled Sabriel. The `multiattack` dispatcher output listed Maw damage as a single-damage-pool value (printed ~20 piercing) without flagging the auto-crit. DM had to catch the "(if grappled → auto-crit)" note in the output text and manually apply 8d8+3 (~39 avg) instead of 4d8+3 (~20 avg). This is the correct ruling, but the dispatcher output makes it easy to apply the wrong (non-crit) value at a fast-moving table.
- **Finding:** The Maw entry in multiattack output includes the conditional note in prose, but shows only the normal-hit damage figure. DMs running quickly may apply 20 piercing instead of ~39. The crit doubles the damage tier — this gap is meaningful (the grapple+maw-crit pattern is the beholder's highest-damage opening).
- **Recommendation:** Update the Maw damage display in the `multiattack` DB spec to append a crit-column: `→ CRIT if grappled: avg 39 (8d8+3)`. This keeps the normal-hit number visible (for ungrappled targets) and surfaces the crit value inline. Low authoring effort, high table-utility. **No design decision required — implement when convenient.**

### FI-EV7-7-C: beholder-thrulm — LR depletion rate at L5 via Hold Person is faster than expected (2026-06-10T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-10 14:00 UTC, cycle 37. Marwen used Hold Person (DC 14) in R1 and R2. Beholder failed both saves naturally (Wis save: 4, then 7) and burned Legendary Resistance both times. After 2 rounds, beholder has only 1 LR remaining. In R3, a third Hold Person could exhaust the last LR, then a 4th cast would have a real chance of paralyzing the beholder. A paralyzed beholder is a huge shift: all attacks against it have advantage and hit as critical hits.
- **Finding:** The party is *designed* to lose this fight. However, a clever L5 wizard can force LR depletion in ~2-3 rounds via repeated concentration spells (Hold Person, Hold Monster). If Marwen survives to R3 (not guaranteed), there's a genuine path to beholder paralysis. This appears to be an unintended win vector — a L5 wizard should not be able to systematically pressure a CR13 creature into paralysis.
- **Recommendation (human decision required):** Options: (a) Raise beholder LR to 5 (harder to burn out); (b) Add a Void Scream / Drain Divinity priority note that the beholder targets Marwen first if Hold Person was cast (anti-caster focus); (c) Accept this as a high-skill play option (caster hero moment in a TPK encounter). The current 3 LR feels tight for a CR13 creature facing a 3-PC L5 party with any save-based caster. Prior cycles haven't flagged this because Marwen usually dies before depleting LR, but the empty-void slice (no thralls soaking Marwen from the beholder) exposed this more cleanly.

### FI-EV7-7-D: beholder-thrulm — "Drain Divinity as toll" negotiation path is undocumented (2026-06-10T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-10 14:00 UTC, cycle 37. Negotiation phase: the beholder's demand to "leave the mark" targets Sabriel's divine power. The .md lists de-escalation hooks ("a paladin's fall from grace" etc.) but doesn't specify a toll mechanic. There's a natural in-world option: the beholder accepts one spell slot as a voluntary Drain Divinity (passage toll) without requiring oath-breaking. Sabriel loses a smite slot; the beholder gains 4 THP; the party passes. Thematically resonant, mechanically coherent, requires no oath violation.
- **Finding:** The empty-void slice is the primary negotiation test case. Without a concrete toll mechanic in the .md, DMs either default to full-oath-break (which players reject) or pure combat (which bypasses the beholder's intelligence and motivation). The toll path is an underexplored middle ground that rewards creative roleplaying.
- **Recommendation:** Add a DM note under Interaction Notes → De-escalation Hooks: "**Toll passage (alternate):** The beholder may accept a single voluntary Drain Divinity as payment for passage — the divine caster offers one spell slot willingly (the beholder feeds on the divine energy; no oath violation required). Mechanically: target loses their highest available spell slot; beholder gains 2×slot-level temp HP. The party may pass unmolested. This is only offered once — repeat passage requires a fresh toll. If the party refuses or the divine caster has no remaining slots, combat begins."

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (36th TW-cycle additions — 2026-06-08T06)

### FI-TW36-A: CW structural lockout — 5th consecutive tank-wall cycle confirm (2026-06-08T06)

- **Context:** 2026-06-08 06:18 UTC, slice #2 (tank-wall) cycle 36. Seed 494694. Rager init 19 > SC init 17. Rager charges first; SC-to-Rager gap exceeds 30ft CW range. CW 0/3 spent this cycle. Pattern: FI-TW37-A (4th cycle), DESIGN-TW2-B (prior runs). Also the 18:19 cycle had SC-before-Rager (accidental positive case); all other TW cycles show lockout.
- **Finding:** The defining CW + Taunt synergy of the tank-wall pairing never expresses in practice because (a) Rager almost always has higher or equal initiative vs SC, and (b) even when SC acts first, the Rager charges 20ft in R1, pushing the SC-to-Rager gap beyond 30ft CW range by end of R1. The formation's identity (tanking rager + weakness-calling support) is invisible to the players. **Recommendation (human decision required):** reduce SC starting position to ≤25ft from party OR increase CW range to 60ft. Either change lets SC call weakness on the Rager before/after the charge in most initiative outcomes.

### FI-TW36-B: VICTORY R3 — 5th consecutive TW-slice party win, no PC deaths (2026-06-08T06)

- **Context:** 2026-06-08 06:18 UTC, cycle 36. VICTORY R3. Party aggregate HP: Bazgar 31/49 (63%), Marwen 32/32 (100%), Sabriel 35/44 (80%). Rager dead R2 end (focused from Prone — prone condition amplified Bazgar/Sabriel damage). SC dead R3 solo (2 attacks from Bazgar).
- **Finding:** Tank-wall consistently falls R2-R3 to focused party damage. The pairing generates minimal threat. Rager's Berserk fires R2 (0/2 vs AC18/19 — same as FI-TW37-C and DESIGN-TW2-A). SC's Barrage lands once (9 piercing split) and then never recharges before death. **Recommendation (human decision required):** swap 1-Rager+1-SC for 2-Rager+1-SC OR add 1 thrall-derro as meatshield. Alternatively, Rager HP 75+ would give the pairing time to develop.

### FI-TW36-C: Berserk-after-Prone interaction — at-table DM callout needed (2026-06-08T06)

- **Context:** Bazgar's R1 Trip Attack proned the Rager. R2, Rager used Berserk (cannot move on Berserk turn). The sim incorrectly targeted Marwen (back-line, ~30ft) as the third Berserk attack. Corrected: Berserk reach is 5ft — Marwen out of range.
- **Finding:** At-table DMs may forget that Berserk reach is 5ft and allow targeting back-line PCs if the rager was repositioned by a previous turn's stand-up. The tactics .md should explicitly note: "Berserk attacks are limited to creatures within 5ft at the moment of the attack — a standing-up rager reaches only adjacent combatants, not back-line PCs." **Recommend adding a one-line Berserk range note to derro-rager.md tactics.** Low priority; the DB output already shows 3 lines with the cap note.

### MQ-TW36-C: Taunt text ambiguity — spell attack rolls (2026-06-08T06)

- **Context:** Marwen failed her Taunt save R1 (disadvantage on attacks vs non-rager). She then fired Fire Bolt at SC (non-rager target). Sim applied flat roll. RAW: ranged spell attacks ARE attack rolls (PHB p.194 "When you make an attack roll for a spell..."). Taunt text says "disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than the rager" — this should include Marwen's Fire Bolt at SC.
- **Finding:** The at-table DM may not remember that Taunt disadvantage applies to spell attacks targeting non-rager enemies. This is not a balance issue (the Fire Bolt hit anyway with d20+5=17) but could be relevant when the roll is marginal. **Recommend** adding explicit note to Taunt text: "disadvantage applies to all attack rolls including ranged spell attacks." Low priority. Carry from MQ-SRR5-40-A.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (43rd SCT-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T22)

### FI-SCT-43-B: PTV expressed decisively in SC-only formation — first confirmed activation (2026-06-07T22)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 22:19 UTC, slice #6 (shardcaller-team) cycle 43. Seed 494686. VICTORY R3. SC-B hit Marwen twice in R1 with shard-throws (applying Pack Tactics Voice — disadv on next save). SC-C then fired Shard-Barrage in the same round; Marwen rolled Dex save with disadv (rolled 5 vs DC 13). She failed and died.
- **Finding:** Prior log entry "FI-SCT6-C PTV-useless-SC-only-formation" was seed-dependent. When SCs focus-fire the same target before the Barrage fires, PTV delivers real pressure. The toggle/not-stackable rule worked correctly (second hit refreshed, did not stack disadv). The interaction SC-multiattack-on-target → PTV → SC-barrage-on-same-target-with-disadv is a real and meaningful combo in SC-only formations when initiative permits. **No fix needed.** DM note: when three SCs are active, SC-A (high init) should consider focusing shard-throws on the same target SC-C (low init) intends to barrage — maximizes PTV window.

### FI-SCT-43-D: Shard-Barrage recharge 5-6 too punishing in short fights — SC-C locked out R2+R3 (2026-06-07T22)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 22:19 UTC, slice #6 cycle 43. SC-C fired Barrage R1 (correctly, per 3-SC assignment). Recharge roll R2: d6=2 (fail). Recharge roll R3: d6=1 (fail). Two consecutive failures. SC-C spent R2 and R3 as a pure multiattacker with +4 to-hit against AC 18 (Bazgar), missing both rounds.
- **Finding:** Recharge 5-6 ≈ 33% per round. In a 3-round fight (which SC-only formations always produce against a full L5 party), the lowest-init SC fires Barrage R1 and then has only 2 rounds to recharge — 56% chance of never recharging. This makes the post-R1 shardcaller feel like a less interesting multiattacker. **Recommendation:** consider recharge 4-6 (50% per round), giving SC-C a ~75% chance of recharging at least once in R2-R3. **HUMAN DECISION REQUIRED** — changing recharge rate affects balance vs mixed-formation encounters.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (42nd SRR-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T21)

### FI-SR5-G: derro-rager — Berserk fires at correct timing but whiffs all 3 targets; set-piece with zero mechanical trace (2026-06-07T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 21:21 UTC, slice #5 (solo-rager-rush) cycle 42. Seed 494685. VICTORY R6. Rager-2's Berserk recharged in R4 (recharge roll d6=6 after 3 rounds of waiting: 4, 3, 3). Berserk fired correctly (Rager-2 couldn't move; attacks vs all 3 PCs). Rolls: 8 vs AC 18 (Bazgar), 5 vs AC 15 (Marwen), 3 vs AC 19 (Sabriel) — all three attacks missed. The ability produced only narration.
- **Finding:** With +4 to-hit against AC 18/15/19, expected hit probability is ~35%/50%/30% respectively. Combined probability of hitting at least one PC is approximately 73%. This run landed in the unlucky ~27% bracket. The concern is the experience at the table: after 3 rounds of failed recharge, the Berserk finally fires with dramatic narration ("greataxe carves a horizontal arc through everything hostile") and produces nothing. In the 41st-cycle, Berserk fired R1 triple and was decisive (leading to TPK R5). The swing between "zero Berserks" (this run, where Rager-1 died before recharging and Rager-3 never recharged) and "6 Berserks" (41st cycle) is extreme. Both runs used the same slice, same party — only the seed differed.
- **Recommendation:** Confirm 41st-cycle recommendation (FI-SR5-A): give Berserk advantage on its attacks (committed all-or-nothing momentum) OR raise to +6 to-hit on the Berserk turn only. Either change makes a zero-Berserk-hit result improbable while preserving normal multiattack feel. **Human design decision — carries from FI-SR5-A.**

### FI-SR5-H: derro-rager — solo-rager-rush outcome is 100% initiative-seed-driven; 42nd-cycle confirms bidirectionality (2026-06-07T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 21:21 UTC, cycle 42 (VICTORY R6) vs. 2026-06-07 13:21 UTC, cycle 41 (TPK R5). Identical encounter: 3× Rager, Compass Edge level-5. Only the seed differed. Cycle 41: ragers 20/17/16 vs PCs 10/5/3 → triple-Berserk R1 → Bazgar at 15 HP before PCs act → TPK R5. Cycle 42: PCs 22/18/14 vs ragers 12/7 (Rager-3 at 7, acted last) → Fireball R1 vs all ragers → Rager-1 dead R1 → VICTORY R6.
- **Finding:** The encounter has two distinct attractors depending on who acts first in R1: (a) Ragers high-init → triple-Berserk → structural damage before Fireball → hard TPK path. (b) PCs high-init (especially Marwen) → Fireball → halved all rager HP → easy VICTORY path. There is no intermediate path where both sides trade early. The Fireball-Berserk race is the fight's single critical variable. This makes the encounter feel mechanical (coinflip) rather than tactical at the design level, even though the individual rounds inside each path have good texture.
- **Recommendation:** See FI-SR5-A recommendation (stagger rager entry) — this addresses the R1 triple-Berserk path. For the Fireball path: consider adding terrain cover (pillars) that Ragers start behind, preventing a clean Fireball-cone geometry in R1 unless Marwen moves closer. Both changes bring the fight closer to initiative-independent and force round-2 decision-making. **Human design decision.**

### MQ-SR5-B: derro-rager — Taunt stacking from 3 simultaneous ragers creates compound disadvantage on one PC (2026-06-07T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 21:21 UTC. In R1-R3, all three ragers taunted Bazgar simultaneously. Bazgar made 3 separate DC 12 Cha saves per round. Any save that failed applied "disadvantage on attacks against any creature other than the taunting rager." Since all three ragers applied the Taunt and Bazgar failed some, he was technically under simultaneous disadvantage vs R1 (from R2's Taunt) AND vs R2 (from R1's Taunt) AND vs R3 (from R3's Taunt). In practice Bazgar only targeted Rager-1 all rounds anyway, so this never mattered mechanically.
- **Question:** Should Taunt be limited to "one active Taunt at a time per target" (only the most-recently-applied or highest-DC one counts)? Or is the intended behavior that three ragers can chain-Taunt the same target and effectively impose disadvantage on all their attacks versus non-taunting creatures simultaneously?
