The latter creates a situation where a taunted PC can never switch targets without disadvantage penalties to all rager attacks — which may be overpowered in edge cases where a PC needs to attack a different NPC.
- **Recommendation:** Add to Taunt effect: "A creature already Taunted by an allied rager cannot be Taunted again by a different rager while the first Taunt is active (only one Taunt condition can apply at once)." This removes the stacking ambiguity and keeps the mechanic clean. **Low priority — the stacking scenario only arises in 3-rager formation and the net effect was zero in this run.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (SCT6-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T14)

### FI-SCT6-A: derro-shardcaller — ≥25 ft spread guidance insufficient to survive Fireball (2026-06-07T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 14:16 UTC, slice #6 (shardcaller-team). Seed 494678. Party VICTORY R2. Marwen (Wizard L5) cast Fireball (L3, 20-ft radius) at SC-A + SC-B in R1. Both were only ~25 ft apart per spread guidance. Fireball center at midpoint = only 12.5 ft from each SC, well within 20-ft blast radius. Both SCs took 31 full fire damage (saves 7 and 12, both < DC 15) and were left at 2 HP each. SC-B was then downed by Sacred Flame in the same round. SC-A was downed by Magic Missile at the start of R2.
- **Finding:** The spread guidance "≥25 ft apart" prevents a single-target all-in-one-square catastrophe but does not prevent a 20-ft-radius Fireball from catching two SCs. To guarantee no two SCs are ever both inside a Fireball's blast simultaneously, they need >40 ft separation (the Fireball's full diameter is 40 ft, so SCs must be >40 ft apart). In the open vault chamber geometry this may mean SCs are too far apart for Barrage line coverage and CW range (CW is 30 ft). A realistic fix: raise the spread guidance to "≥45 ft when a Wizard is identified in the party (sacrifice line coverage; survival > burst damage R1)" and add a Wizard-detection tactic note.
- **Recommendation:** Raise spread guidance to ≥45 ft OR add an explicit tactic: "If the party includes a visible Wizard or Sorcerer: spread to ≥45 ft (accept CW range loss on far SC, accept Barrage-line coverage loss). Survival past R1 is worth the coordination loss." Human decision required — affects formation geometry and multiple tactic notes.

### FI-SCT6-D: derro-shardcaller — no last-resort Barrage override when SC is at ≤5 HP with charged Barrage (2026-06-07T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 14:16 UTC, slice #6 (shardcaller-team). Seed 494678. SC-A entered R2 at 2 HP with Barrage still charged (never fired in R1 — SC-A was the multiattack SC per FI-SCT-34-A). Per 2-SC endgame rule SC-A was assigned to Multiattack R2, saving Barrage for SC-C. SC-A used Tactical Retreat (bonus) and then Multiattacked (two misses, rolls 5 and 3). Was killed by Magic Missile before acting again. Barrage was never used.
- **Finding:** A SC at ≤5 HP with a charged Barrage faces certain death regardless of action choice. Holding the Barrage for stagger discipline is correct when the formation partner is healthy, but wasting the charge on two misses with a near-dead SC produces zero value. Current tactics have no override for this case: the checklist says "retreat behind pillar" and the 2-SC endgame says "SC-A multiattacks." Neither addresses the "charging to die with an unused Barrage" failure mode.
- **Recommendation:** Add to start-of-turn checklist item 1 (after recharge roll): "Override for dying SC: If Barrage is charged AND SC has ≤5 HP AND this SC has a formation partner at ≥15 HP, fire Barrage THIS turn rather than holding for stagger. Do not let the Barrage die with you." This preserves stagger discipline when both SCs are viable while allowing a dying SC to extract value from its most powerful ability. Low-priority fix — the stagger rule is the correct default 95% of the time.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (41st SRR-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T13)

### FI-SR5-A: derro-rager — R1 triple-Berserk fires before all PCs in solo-rager formation; extreme initiative dependency (2026-06-07T13)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 13:21 UTC, slice #5 (solo-rager-rush) cycle 41. Seed 494677. TPK R5. All 3 ragers rolled high initiative (20, 17, 16) vs party (10, 5, 3). All 3 Berserks available R1. Result: 34 damage to Bazgar (49→15) before any PC could act.
- **Finding:** In a 3-rager formation with Berserk available at combat start (5e RAW), a high-initiative draw produces 3 AoE melee attacks before the party acts. Bazgar entered R2 at 15/49 HP; Rager-2's R2 multiattack killed him. The outcome was more correlated with the d20 initiative roll than with any tactical decision. The effect is amplified by the tight Thrulm corridor geometry where all PCs are within reach after one round of movement.
- **Recommendation A:** Stagger rager encounter entry — one rager enters from a side passage on R2, preventing all-Berserks-R1. Fits the fiction (ambush from multiple directions). **Human design decision.**
- **Recommendation B:** Add tactic note: "Berserk R1 only if 2+ enemies already in reach at start of combat (no movement needed). If the party hasn't closed distance, use Multiattack + advance." Prevents the trivial case where ragers Berserk before PCs can act.
- **Recommendation C:** For scenario testing only — add a "Berserk start unavailable" flag to the encounter-opening setup note, making Berserk start as uncharged in playtests to isolate the recharge mechanic.

### FI-SR5-B: derro-rager — Taunt bonus-action saturates by R2 in 3v3 formation (2026-06-07T13)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 13:21 UTC. By end of R1, Marwen taunted (Rager-1 save 4 fail). R2 Rager-2 taunted Sabriel (save 7 fail). Remaining ragers skipped Taunt R2–R5 (all viable targets taunted).
- **Finding:** In a 3-rager vs 3-PC encounter, Taunt fills all PC slots after 1–2 taunts succeed. Once saturated, the bonus action does nothing for the rest of the fight. Taunts expire EoNT (they don't persist), but PCs don't refresh fast enough to open new taunt slots — by the time one expires, the PC is dead or the rager's own target has already re-engaged. The bonus action feels useless after R1.
- **Recommendation:** Add tactic bullet: "When all living PCs are already taunted by an allied rager this round, skip Taunt and instead position for a Berserk next round (move toward a second PC cluster if one exists, or ready for the next recharge)." This turns the saturated state into purposeful positioning rather than a dead action.

### FI-SR5-C: derro-rager — Berserk recharge rate high in rager-only formation; 6 Berserks / 5 rounds (2026-06-07T13)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 13:21 UTC. Berserk R5 recharge: 3 at R1 start (RAW), +1 R2 (Rager-1 d6=5), +2 R3 (Rager-2 d6=6, Rager-3 d6=5). Total: 6 Berserks fired in 5 rounds across 3 ragers.
- **Finding:** A 5–6 recharge means ~33% chance per turn. With 2–3 ragers surviving past R2, the group generates roughly 1 Berserk per round after R1. Combined with the R1 triple-fire, the result is sustained AoE pressure with minimal breathing room. The encounter felt mechanically predictable — each round one rager Berserks. The .md says this is intentional ("fights until it dies") but in isolation (no shardcaller forcing split-focus, no shrine-touched forcing positioning) the rager feels like a damage engine without tactical texture.
- **Recommendation:** No auto-fix; this is a formation-design issue. The solo-rager-rush slice is designed to stress-test Berserk. If the broader encounter adds shardcallers or shrine-touched, the rager's role becomes clearer. **Low priority for standalone design changes.**

### MQ-SR5-D: thrall-derro — action named "multiattack" but DB type is "single_attack" (2026-06-07T13)

- **Context:** Phase A DB review. `thrall-derro/multiattack` spec: type="single_attack", attacks=[1× Hand Axe Weak]. .md confirms: "makes one melee attack; domination is incomplete."
- **Finding:** The action name "multiattack" is misleading — DMs may expect two attacks. The type correctly reflects one attack. No crash risk (Phase A passes). Potential confusion at the table if a DM expects the standard "two attacks" pattern.
- **Recommendation:** Rename DB action from "multiattack" to "attack" to align name with function. Alternatively, add to narration: "(One attack only — domination is incomplete)." Low priority. **Human decision.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (31st TP-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T16)

### FI-TP31-A: harness — restrained speed-zero enforcement gap (2026-06-07T16)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 16:18 UTC, slice #0 cycle 31. Seed 494680. DW2 was restrained by Web (Dex save 6 vs DC 12) on Marwen's turn. DW2 acted next (init 10), and the sim allowed it to close to melee (20 ft away) and use Hand Axe.
- **Finding:** A restrained creature has speed 0. DW2 could not have moved to melee range; it should have been forced to attack at range (Light Crossbow, single shot, at disadvantage). The NPC spec is correct — the harness did not enforce the speed=0 constraint. Net impact: one extra axe hit on Marwen (3 slashing) vs what a disadvantaged crossbow shot would likely produce. Outcome (VICTORY R2) unchanged.
- **Recommendation:** This is a harness limitation, not a spec bug. Log as an ongoing sim-accuracy gap. At the table, DM must manually enforce restrained speed=0. No automated fix available in this blast radius.

### FI-TP31-B: threshold-patrol — formation trivial vs. L5 party with AoE control; 7th-cycle confirm (2026-06-07T16)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 16:18 UTC. Party outcome: ~72% HP aggregate. Marwen (15/32) was the hardest-hit PC. Sabriel spent 2 smites and 1 spell slot (Web); Marwen spent 1 2nd-level and 1 1st-level slot. The fight resolved in 2 rounds.
- **Finding:** This is now the 7th TP-cycle recorded, and the party wins comfortably every cycle. A single Web (DC 12 Dex) reliably catches 2/3 of the NPCs in a narrow corridor (DW2 and SC both rolled ≤9). Once restrained, the SC's Barrage still fires (correctly, rules-wise) but the DWs are nullified as melee threats. The 2-DW + 1-SC formation cannot survive R1 CC from a prepared L5 party.
- **Recommendation (for human):** If the intent is resource drain, the formation needs either (a) an enemy with Legendary Resistance or Evasion, (b) a 3rd DW so at least one escapes CC, or (c) a hard opener that forces the party to react before they can cast Web (e.g., initiative advantage, ambush, or lair-action equivalent). Alternatively, accept that Threshold Patrol is a "gimme" encounter and the real pressure is the resource spend — the 1 slot + 2 smites will be missed later.

### FI-TP31-C: deep-watch-derro — DW1 never attacks in threshold-patrol; 7th-cycle structural confirm (2026-06-07T16)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 16:18 UTC. DW1 rolled init 8, placing it last among NPCs. Sabriel (init 9) attacked DW1 twice + smited for 31 total damage. DW1 (27 HP) died before taking a single action. Across 7 TP cycles, DW1 has a low average initiative (Dex +2 on d20 is flat) and is frequently killed by the party's top-DPS characters before its turn.
- **Finding:** The Deep Watch identity — formation fighting with Tactical Drilling (advantage when ally adjacent) — has never expressed in threshold-patrol. DW1 is erased before it acts; DW2's Tactical Drilling fires but the advantage arrives from having DW1 still standing next to the target, which is usually already dead.
- **Recommendation:** Give each DW a static +2 initiative bonus (Deep Watch discipline: "always on the clock") or enforce a house rule that the party rolls for surprise at the Threshold. Alternatively, increase DW HP from 27 to 35 so they can absorb one hit and still act. **Human design decision.** Carries from FI-TP-1 (SC CW lockout) — both are symptoms of the NPCs dying before their tactical identity fires.

### FI-TP31-D: derro-shardcaller — CW >20 HP guard fires correctly but advantage lost due to R2 kill; 31st-cycle flag (2026-06-07T16)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 16:18 UTC. SC spent 1/3 CW charges on DW2 (HP 27, >20 threshold per MQ-12 exception). DW2 was killed in R2 by Bazgar before it could use the advantage.
- **Finding:** This is spec-correct behavior — MQ-12 correctly prevents CW on low-HP allies but permits it on full-HP allies even when they've already acted. The gap is that a 27 HP DW2 with CW advantage can still be eliminated in one combat turn by a level-5 Fighter. The >20 HP threshold protects against "ally has one foot in the grave"; it doesn't protect against "party front-loads all damage onto the last standing DW."
- **Recommendation:** This is an edge pattern, not a structural bug. The CW charge was not wasted by the tactics — it was wasted by bad luck (Bazgar's 2 attacks dealt 29 HP in one turn). Logging for awareness. The 30th-cycle FI-TP-1 recommendation to raise SC Dex would partially address this by giving CW a pre-DW-turn window.

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (30th TP-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T08)

### FI-TP-1: derro-shardcaller — SC Call Weakness structurally locked out in threshold-patrol; 6th-cycle confirm (2026-06-07T08)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 08:20 UTC, slice #0 cycle 30. Seed 494672. VICTORY R3. CW spent 0/3. DWD1 init 12, DWD2 init 11, SC init 9 in this run; pattern holds across all 6 recorded TP cycles.
- **Finding:** In threshold-patrol, both DWDs consistently roll higher initiative than SC (+2 each, same modifier). By the time SC acts, both DWDs have already taken their turns. MQ-12 correctly prevents CW on already-acted allies. DWDs die R1-R2 (party front-loads Sabriel+Bazgar damage), so no surviving DWD exists to pre-buffer CW on for the following round. Result: CW fires 0 times across 6 TP playtests. SC's primary tactical differentiator is a dead letter in this encounter.
- **Recommendation A:** Raise SC Dex to 14 (+2→+3 Dex mod) to improve initiative reliability over DWDs — SC needs to beat at least one DWD ~65% of the time for CW to have meaningful activation rate. **Human design decision.**
- **Recommendation B:** Add explicit tactic bullet: "If both allies have already acted this round, CW is banked for next round's first-acting ally (pre-buffer)." This doesn't change the stat but makes the intended use case clear to the DM, who can make an end-of-round decision.
- **Recommendation C:** Restructure threshold-patrol so SC acts first (higher position, orders DWDs forward) — initiative order justified in fiction.

### FI-TP-2: derro-shardcaller — Shard-Throw sustained hit-rate too low against Compass Edge party (2026-06-07T08)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 08:20 UTC. SC Shard-Throw: +4 to hit, 1d4+2 piercing. Against this party: Marwen AC15 → 30% per throw; Bazgar AC18 → 15%; Sabriel AC19 → 10%.
- **Finding:** In R2, SC made 2 throws at Marwen (lowest AC) and missed both (14 and 11 vs AC15). Expected miss-miss at AC15: ~49%. Across TP cycles, SC sustained damage from throws is effectively zero — all SC pressure comes from the single Barrage shot. After Barrage, SC is irrelevant until death.
- **Recommendation:** Raise Shard-Throw to +5 to hit (34% vs Marwen, 20% vs Bazgar) or +6 (44%/30%). This makes the back-line SC feel like a continuing threat rather than a one-shot ability with legs. Alternatively, leave at +4 and accept Barrage-is-the-payload design philosophy — SC is designed to fire Barrage and die. **Human design decision.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (30th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T15)

### FI-EV7-30-A: beholder-thrulm — Void Scream bimodal-TPK pattern, 30th-cycle confirm (2026-06-07T15)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 15:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 30. Seed 494679. TPK R7. VS fired R2 with 46 psychic (high roll, avg 33). Marwen and Sabriel both failed Wis saves; both downed in the same action. Bazgar soloed R3–R7.
- **Finding:** Third independent EV7-cycle with VS deciding the fight mid-combat. The damage variance of 6d10 (6–60 range) makes the encounter highly RNG-dependent. A single VS action at or above average eliminates 2/3 PCs in one shot. If VS rolls low or fails to fire, the fight extends meaningfully. This continues a pattern documented in FI-EVD-7-A, FI-FC39-A, FI-BEL3-38-A.
- **Recommendation A:** Recharge 6 — enforce as a once-per-encounter cap. The bimodal behavior disappears; VS remains a credible threat but a deterministic one. **Human decision.**
- **Recommendation B:** Reduce damage to 5d10 (avg 27.5). Still downs Marwen (32 HP) on an above-average roll, but Sabriel (44 HP) survives consistently unless she's already been damaged. Creates a more differentiated experience (wizard dies, paladin keeps fighting). **Human decision.**

### FI-EV7-30-C: beholder-thrulm — Drain Divinity not firing due to VS initiative collision (2026-06-07T15)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 15:20 UTC. DD never used in 30th cycle because Sabriel (the DD target) was downed by Void Scream in R2 before DD could be applied as a post-Sabriel LA. In R1, the beholder used 2 LA for Void Ray and 1 LA for Tentacle; DD not prioritized. DD is designed to be the beholder's anti-divine counter, but the sequence "multiattack R1 → VS R2 → Sabriel down" leaves no window for DD to express.
- **Finding:** DD (LA cost 3) and VS (main action) compete for the same fight window. If VS fires before Sabriel is meaningfully damaged, DD is either wasted (all 3 LA gone, no Sabriel turn before VS fires) or never needed (Sabriel is down). DD showed impact in cycles where VS was absent (28th cycle: DD×4 strips both L3 slots).
- **Recommendation:** Add a tactical branch to the beholder's Tactics section: "If Void Scream has NOT been used this combat AND Sabriel has L2+ smite slots remaining, prioritize **Drain Divinity** as post-Sabriel LA (costs 3) in R1 before VS fires. The smite-stripping is more reliable value than a Void Ray in R1 and sets up VS as the R2 closer with fewer resources in play." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

### MQ-EV7-30-A: beholder-thrulm — Disintegration Ray rules ambiguity on downed targets (2026-06-07T15)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 15:20 UTC. DR fired on Marwen who was already at 0 HP (death save phase). DR spec: "If this damage reduces the target to 0 HP, target is disintegrated." Marwen was already at 0. Sim treated as disintegrated; RAW ambiguous.
- **Finding:** PHB p.197 states damage to a creature at 0 HP causes death save failures (1 failure per damage event, 2 for crits). The disintegration clause requires being *reduced* to 0 HP, implying the target was previously above 0. Against a downed target, DR is a mechanically inferior action (wastes the disintegration rider; no practical difference from any other hit). Additionally, using DR on a downed creature is a poor tactical choice when a conscious enemy remains.
- **Recommendation:** Add to Tactics and to beholder-thrulm.md: "**Disintegration Ray** — target conscious creatures only. The disintegration effect does not trigger against already-downed targets (they are not *reduced* to 0 HP; they are already there). If all PCs are downed except one, hold DR for the standing target. Against downed targets, Multiattack or Void Ray has better action-economy." **Authoring fix — .md + tactic note.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (29th EV7-cycle additions — 2026-06-07T07)

### FI-EVD-7-D: beholder-thrulm — Antireality reaction too narrow; near-zero table impact (2026-06-07T07)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 07:21 UTC, slice #7 cycle 29. Seed 494671. INDETERMINATE R5. Antireality triggered 4 times (Bazgar hits). Bazgar attack rolls: 22, 27, 23, 21 — all ≥4 above AC 17. Zero blocks.
- **Finding:** Antireality grants +2 AC after seeing the roll. At level 5, a Fighter with +7 to hit vs AC 17 hits on a 10+ (55% of attacks). The +2 AC reaction only converts hits to misses when the d20+bonus equals exactly 17–18, a ~11% window of all hits. Against a Battlemaster with Precision Attack maneuvers, the window shrinks further. The reaction is functionally irrelevant against well-built melee PCs and provides only situational value against lower-attack-bonus characters. It consumes the beholder's only reaction slot for minimal return.
- **Recommendation A:** Increase to +4 AC (blocks rolls of 13–16, ~22% of hits — still weaker than Shield but meaningfully more relevant). **Human design decision.**
- **Recommendation B:** Change effect to "advantage on one saving throw made before the start of the beholder's next turn" — thematically fits "reality warps," is relevant more broadly (saves vs. spells), and doesn't depend on the narrow hit-window math. **Human design decision.**

### FI-EVD-7-A: beholder-thrulm — Unstable Ground lair-spam in no-thrall configuration (2026-06-07T07)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 07:21 UTC. Beholder solo (no thralls). Used Unstable Ground all 5 rounds (same as 28th-cycle). Bazgar failed 3/4 Dex saves; spent half movement standing up each round; averaged ~5 DPR (resistance + prone-limited actions). Manifest Thralls is dead lair-action without thralls present.
- **Finding:** Without live thralls, the beholder's lair action set collapses to UG (single-target disruption) and VE (AoE 20 ft). The sim always picks UG by default. Void Eruption (2d10 force, 20 ft radius, no loop) is strictly better when ≥2 PCs are within 20 ft of the shrine (which the tactics recommend for shrine-altar proximity). The beholder's lair-action tactics note does mention "use Void Eruption when thralls have been wiped" — but this scenario had no thralls to begin with.
- **Recommendation:** Add explicit note to lair actions: "If no thralls are alive at encounter start, treat Void Eruption as the default lair action for rounds 2+. Reserve Unstable Ground for isolating high-threat melee PCs (fighters, paladins) who are advancing on the altar." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

### MQ-EVD-7-A: beholder-thrulm — Maw grapple-crit tactical priority unspecified (2026-06-07T07)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 07:21 UTC. DB spec: Maw `rider_on_hit`: "If the target is grappled by the beholder, this attack is a critical hit instead." In R4–R5, Bazgar was grappled but sim targeted Marwen with the Maw (targeting logic error). The auto-crit combo never executed.
- **Finding:** Maw-on-grappled averages: 4d8+3 doubled = 8d8+3 ≈ 39 piercing on crit. Against Marwen (HP 32), this is an insta-down on a hit. Against Bazgar (HP 49), near-down. The combo is the beholder's single highest-damage option, exceeding even Disintegration Ray average (45) on a crit. It requires 1 tentacle hit prior turn, which is the correct multiattack sequence. The tactics section does not explicitly call this out.
- **Recommendation:** Add to Tactics > Against Melee: "**Maw priority**: When any target is grappled, redirect the Maw to that target for the auto-crit. With +7 to hit and ~39 average damage on the crit (4d8+3 × 2), this is the highest expected-value action in the multiattack sequence." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

---

## DESIGN DECISIONS (review in morning)

### FI-TW-1: tank-wall — CW range vs. positioning; core mechanic never fires (2026-06-07T02)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 02:19 UTC, slice #2 cycle 36. Seed 494666. VICTORY R3. SC acted last (init 4); Rager charged to 0 ft before SC's first turn, leaving a 55-ft SC-to-Rager gap — beyond CW's 30-ft range. CW charges used: 0/3.
- **Finding:** The tank-wall slice is supposed to test the CW + Rager taunt-pairing, but whenever SC has lower initiative than the Rager, the Rager charges out of CW range before SC can act. This is a structural positioning problem: the standard SC start (55 ft back) combined with a Rager that charges 30 ft in R1 means the CW window only exists if SC acts before the Rager. This is initiative-RNG-dependent and fails ~50% of the time.
- **Recommendation:** Specify in tank-wall scenario: SC starts 20–25 ft from party front (≤25 ft from Rager at all times). Alternatively extend CW range to 60 ft. **Human design decision.**

### FI-TW-2: tank-wall — Rager HP 52 insufficient for tank role vs. 3-PC level 5 party (2026-06-07T02)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 02:19 UTC. Rager absorbed 49 damage in R1 (11+5+19+14), died at start of R2 before getting a second turn. The "wall" dissolved before the SC's first offensive action mattered.
- **Finding:** At CR 2, the Rager is intentionally fragile, but the tank-wall slice concept requires it to absorb at least 2 rounds to let the CW + taunt mechanics play out. Current HP (52) is insufficient against 3 level-5 PCs (aggregate ~30-40 DPR per round).
- **Recommendation:** Tank-wall slice should use 2× derro-rager, or raise Rager HP to 75–80 for this slice configuration. **Human design decision.**

### FI-TW-3: tank-wall — Taunt target priority undefined; Rager picks wrong target (2026-06-07T02)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 02:19 UTC. Rager taunted Bazgar — who was already targeting the Rager. Mechanically zero effect.
- **Finding:** The .md tactics say Taunt "forces a target to either hit it or suffer disadvantage" but don't specify priority (highest HP? back-line caster? non-melee PC?). Correct target here was Marwen — disadvantage on Scorching Ray aimed at SC would have forced a real decision.
- **Recommendation:** Add to derro-rager.md Taunt tactics: "Prefer the PC NOT already engaging you in melee — the back-line caster or healer is highest value (forces them to redirect or fire at disadvantage vs. the SC)." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

### FI-SW32-A: shrine-wedge — 2×STD formation too lightweight (32nd cycle; 5/5 SW victories) (2026-06-07T01)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 01:20 UTC, slice #1 cycle 32. Seed 494665. VICTORY R3. Marwen fell but fight was never in doubt once STD-A dropped R2.
- **Finding:** 5 shrine-wedge cycles run (28/29/30/31/32): 5 victories. Outcomes: R2, R4, R2, R5, R3. Party wins consistently. The key shrine-wedge mechanics (AR rotation, stagger, UF, OBR) are all firing but the slice is cleared before they can combine meaningfully. R3 is the first cycle where UF did meaningful work (2 hits with advantage). The 2×STD formation needs more staying power to create the intended "resonance rotation under pressure" feel.
- **Recommendation:** Add 1 thrall-derro buffer to the formation, OR raise STD HP from 45 to 55–60. **Human design decision.**

### FI-SW32-C: shrine-wedge — Stagger rule needs explicit low-HP exemption ordering note (2026-06-07T01)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 01:20 UTC, slice #1 cycle 32. STD-A at 8 HP at SOT R2 was still subject to stagger hold in the harness (stagger check ran before HP check). The .md has the exemption but doesn't specify evaluation order.
- **Finding:** A DM running the stagger rule at SOT might assign the hold first, then realize the exemption too late — effectively denying the low-HP STD an AR it should fire recklessly. The stagger bullet says "⚠️ Stagger is evaluated at the START of the round" without specifying that HP exemption is evaluated first.
- **Recommendation:** Add to stagger bullet: "⚠️ Low-HP exemption is evaluated BEFORE assigning the stagger hold — check if either STD is below 20 HP first; exempt those from the hold regardless of initiative." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

### MQ-SW32-B: shrine-wedge — OBR self-kill sequence ruling needed (2026-06-07T01)

- **Context:** 2026-06-07 01:20 UTC, slice #1 cycle 32. STD-B reduced to 0 HP by Sabriel's smite hit. OBR triggered — counter-attack landed 12 dmg on Sabriel. Self-damage (3 psychic) then killed STD-B definitively (it was already at 0). Ruling taken: counter-attack resolves first, then self-damage.
- **Finding:** The .md says OBR fires "when hit by a visible attacker within melee reach." If the hit reduces the shrine-touched to 0 HP, does OBR fire? Ruling taken here is YES — the reaction is triggered by taking damage (not by remaining alive), so the counter-swing resolves as the NPC falls. Self-damage is then incidental. This creates the satisfying "last gasp" beat but needs an explicit ruling note.
- **Recommendation:** Add to OBR description: "OBR fires even if the triggering hit reduces the shrine-touched to 0 HP — the counter-attack resolves before it falls, and the 1d4 self-damage is incidental at that point." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

### MQ-SW32-C: shrine-wedge — Low-HP STD action priority needs explicit .md bullet (2026-06-07T01)

- **Context:** Below-20-HP priority bullets in the .md reference Driven Escape and the stagger exemption separately but don't unify them into a clear action sequence.
- **Finding:** The correct below-20-HP action sequence is: (1) Bonus action = Driven Escape toward altar; (2) Action = AR recklessly if available + 2+ targets in cone, else Multiattack. The stagger exemption (fires AR recklessly) and the Driven Escape bullet (bonus action) are both correct but a DM reading them separately may default to Multiattack when AR is available.
- **Recommendation:** Add explicit clarification to the below-20-HP tactics bullet: "Below 20 HP: Driven Escape (bonus action, move toward altar); action = AR recklessly if AR is available and 2+ targets are in cone, else Multiattack. The stagger hold does NOT apply." **Authoring fix — .md only.**

### FI-SRR5-39-A: solo-rager-rush — Berserk never fires vs. AoE-capable parties (2026-06-06T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 21:00 UTC, slice #5 cycle 39. Seed 494661. VICTORY R3. This is the 2nd SRR cycle (previous: cycle 16, also Berserk-never-fired).
- **Finding:** With Fireball landing for 33/33/16 on the three ragers R1, all ragers enter R2 at ≤36 HP. They die before any recharge check can succeed (5-6 = 17% per round). Even in a longer fight (cycle 16 = 5 rounds), Berserk fired only once and as a death-thrash. In both SRR cycles, Berserk has functioned as a "death-thrash" tool — meaningful only when the rager is already near-death.
- **Recommendation:** Lower recharge to 4-6 (33%), OR add a tactics note explicitly stating "Berserk starts the encounter AVAILABLE (5e RAW for rechargeable abilities — they start available at combat start). Use R1 if two PCs are in melee range." This at minimum guarantees one Berserk in the fight's opening turn. **Human decision needed.**

### FI-SRR5-39-B: solo-rager-rush / Taunt DC 12 — structural weakness confirmed (2nd SRR cycle) (2026-06-06T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 21:00 UTC, slice #5 cycle 39. Carry-forward from FI-SRR16-A (cycle 16, same finding).
- **Finding:** Taunt at DC 12 CHA save is almost never landing. Bazgar (CHA -1) saved 2/3 attempts this cycle. Sabriel (CHA +2) saved all three of her attempts. In both SRR cycles, Taunt has had negligible mechanical impact. The three ragers independently chose the same Taunt target (Bazgar) R1 — this was a redundancy bug now fixed in .md, but even with corrected targeting, the DC is too low to consistently debuff any PC.
- **Recommendation:** Raise Taunt save to DC 13 or change the save type to Wisdom (melee disorientation is more sensibly a Wisdom check). **Human decision needed.**

### FI-SRR5-39-A / MQ-SRR5-39-A: solo-rager-rush — Berserk "always-available at fight start" RAW clarification needed (2026-06-06T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 21:00 UTC, slice #5 cycle 39.
- **Finding:** In D&D 5e RAW, abilities with the "Recharge X-Y" designation start the encounter AVAILABLE and recharge on X-Y after being used. The playtest sim treated them as "unavailable until recharged," which is incorrect. The .md tactics note ("use only when within 5 ft of two or more enemies") doesn't mention that Berserk is immediately available at combat start. This is a .md clarity gap — not a spec bug.
- **Recommendation:** Add to tactics: "Berserk is available at the start of combat (5e Recharge RAW). If two PCs are in melee range at the start of R1, use Berserk immediately before they can reposition." This would let the DM reliably express the ability at least once per encounter.

### FI-SW31-A / FI-SW31-C: shrine-wedge — 2×STD consistently too lightweight; OBR not auto-rolled (2026-06-06T17)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 17:20 UTC, slice #1 cycle 31. Seed 494657. VICTORY R5. Three shrine-wedge cycles: R4 (29th), R2 (30th), R5 (31st) — all PC victories. OBR dispatched `rolls: {}` (31st-cycle confirm).
- **Finding A (FI-SW31-A):** The 2×STD formation is reliably beatable in 2–5 rounds. Unstable Form has never been expressed in any of the three cycles (STDs die before their UF turn). Ancient Resonance has fired at most once per fight, with low-variance outcomes (this cycle: 2d10=2, worst possible). The shrine-wedge slice does not showcase either of the STD's defining mechanics (UF advantage, AR rotation). The encounter needs more tempo.
- **Finding B (FI-SW31-C):** `oath_breaking_retaliation` returns `rolls: {}` — the counter-swing (+4 to-hit, 1d8+1 slashing + 1d8 necrotic) is not auto-rolled by the combat runner. DM must hand-roll mid-attacker's turn, which is cognitively expensive when OBR fires between a multiattacker's strikes.
- **Recommendation A:** Add 1× thrall-derro as a buffer OR increase STD HP to 55–60 to give the fight more tempo and allow UF payoff.
- **Recommendation B:** Restructure OBR DB spec to include a structured `attack` sub-field, enabling the roller to auto-roll the counter-swing. Change `reaction_kind: "buff"` to include explicit attack dice so the runner dispatches the roll. **Human decision needed on both.**

### MQ-SW31-B/C: shrine-wedge — UF same-round vs last-round timing; stagger release condition (2026-06-06T17)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 17:20 UTC, slice #1 cycle 31.
- **MQ-SW31-B:** The .md says UF activates if the STD "took 10+ damage in a single turn last round." When Fireball hits STD-B before its own initiative slot in the SAME round, does that count as "last round"? At the table, most DMs would activate UF immediately (treating "this round, before my turn" as equivalent to "last round" for purposes of the check). The .md should clarify: "damage taken earlier in the current initiative cycle (before this turn) counts for UF purposes."
- **MQ-SW31-C:** If the higher-initiative STD legitimately cannot or chooses not to fire AR (e.g., UF suppresses it in favor of Multiattack), does the stagger hold on the lower-initiative STD release? Current .md implies no (hold is locked at round-start), which is correct, but creates a scenario where neither STD fires AR even with both having it available. Worth one explicit sentence: "Stagger hold is locked at round-start and does not release mid-round regardless of what the higher-init STD does."

### DD-SCT-34-A: shardcaller-team — Which SC fires Barrage in 3-SC formation? (2026-06-06T14)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 14:21 UTC, slice #6 cycle 34. Seed 494654. VICTORY R3. SC-A (init 19, highest) fired Barrage R1 per stagger rule.
- **Finding:** The 2-SC endgame rule explicitly says "the SC that acts FIRST in initiative should multiattack and CW the other SC; the second SC fires Barrage." No equivalent rule exists for 3-SC formation. Current stagger note says only "at most ONE Shard-Barrage may fire per round" without specifying who. In 34th cycle, SC-A (highest init, 19) fired Barrage and gave up its bonus-action window for CW entirely. If instead SC-C (lowest init, 13 tied) had fired Barrage, SC-A and SC-B could have multiattacked and CW'd each other before the party acted — preserving the CW opportunity the 2-SC endgame rule tries to protect.
- **Recommendation:** Add to stagger note: "In 3-SC formation, designate the **lowest-initiative SC** as the Barrage SC. The two higher-init SCs multiattack and Call Weakness each other before the party acts. This matches the 2-SC endgame principle (first-acting SC multiattacks; later-acting SC barrages)."

### DD-SCT-34-B: shardcaller-team — SC-C Barrage-never-fires structural (2026-06-06T14)

- **Context:** 33rd + 34th cycle confirm. SC-C (lowest-init SC) holds Barrage through the entire fight in both runs but never fires. The "3+ enemies in a line" open-vault trigger was never met. By R2, TR monopolizes SC-C's bonus action and the fight ends before Barrage can recharge.
- **Finding:** In a 3-SC open-vault formation where SC-A fires Barrage R1, SC-C is structurally locked out of ever firing Barrage. If SC-C were instead designated as the Barrage SC (lowest init fires Barrage per DD-SCT-34-A), it would fire R1 against the advancing party (likely catching 2+ PCs in a line), providing its area-coverage function before being killed.
- **Recommendation:** Tied to DD-SCT-34-A. Reassigning Barrage to the lowest-init SC solves both the CW-freeze problem (higher-init SCs free to CW) and the Barrage-never-fires problem (designated SC fires early, before melee closes).

### DD-BEL3-32-A: beholder-escorts-limited — Maw "critical hit when grappled" rider vs. .md "save" text (2026-06-06T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 11:21 UTC, slice #3 cycle 32. Seed 494651. TPK R3.
- **Finding:** The `.md` says: "If the target is a creature grappled by the beholder, the target has disadvantage on the saving throw." There is no saving throw in the Maw attack (pure melee weapon attack). The DB spec chose to interpret this as `rider_on_hit: "If the target is grappled by the beholder, this attack is a critical hit instead."` — doubling all dice (4d8 → 8d8, avg ~22 → ~40 damage). In this run, the Maw crit killed Bazgar (30 piercing in round 1, from 11 HP) and Marwen (55 piercing in round 2, from 2 HP). The grapple→crit-Maw combo is the primary kill mechanism in this slice, not legendary actions or disintegration ray.
- **Options:** (1) Keep crit — most lethal, pairs devastatingly with guaranteed grapple. (2) Remove rider — .md text was erroneous copypaste, Maw is just 4d8+3. (3) Change to "grappled target has advantage on the beholder's next save-based ability" — thematic, pairs with void_scream/void_eruption, less swingy. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### DD-BEL3-32-B: beholder-escorts-limited — Tentacle Lash grapple: "DC 16 STR save" vs. automatic on hit (2026-06-06T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 11:21 UTC, slice #3 cycle 32. Seed 494651. TPK R3.
- **Finding:** The `.md` says: "the target is grappled (escape DC 16)" — automatic grapple on a hit, escape is a check. The DB spec has `apply_condition_on_hit: {condition: "grappled", save_dc: 16, save_ability: "str"}`, which the roller formats as "[ASK PLAYER: on a HIT, target rolls a DC 16 STR save — on fail, apply grappled]". At the table, DMs reading this output would call for a STR save (roughly 50% success rate at level 5), making grapple conditional. The simulation applied auto-grapple (per .md), but the printed output disagrees. These two sources create a table discrepancy: simulations show guaranteed grapple; actual table play would get ~50% grapple rate.
- **Recommendation:** Decide which is intended, then align the two sources. Auto-grapple (per .md): remove `save_dc`/`save_ability` from spec, use `apply_condition_on_hit: {condition: "grappled"}`. Save-to-avoid (more player agency): update `.md` stat block to say "the target must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be grappled." **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### DD-BEL3-32-C: beholder-escorts-limited — Void Scream adds Frightened condition not present in .md (2026-06-06T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 11:21 UTC, slice #3 cycle 32. Void Scream did not recharge (0/3 attempts), so this was not triggered in this run. Finding based on spec inspection during Phase A/pre-B review.
- **Finding:** The `.md` Void Scream entry describes only psychic damage (6d10, DC 16 Wis save, half on success, disadvantage within 10 ft of shrine). The DB spec `save.notes` field adds: "On a FAILED save, target is also FRIGHTENED of the beholder for 1 minute (DC 16 Wis save ends at end of each of the target's turns)." Frightened imposes disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks while the beholder is in line of sight, and prevents voluntary movement toward it. This substantially increases Void Scream's power (it becomes a combined damage+soft-CC ability rather than pure damage). The two sources directly contradict.
- **Recommendation:** If Frightened is intentional, add it to the `.md` stat block. If it was an error, remove it from the DB spec's `save.notes`. Either way the sources need to agree. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### DD-BEL3-32-D: beholder-escorts-limited — Thralls die to single Fireball R1; action economy pressure never materializes (2026-06-06T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 11:21 UTC, slice #3 cycle 32. Seed 494651. Fifth confirmed instance across slice #3 cycles.
- **Finding:** Three 22-HP thralls die to a single L3 Fireball on a failed save (22 fire ≥ 22 HP). Even a partial hit (2 fail, 1 save) eliminates 2 of 3 thralls outright. Slice #3 is designed to test "legendary economy, lair actions, action-economy pressure" — but by the time any thrall acts a second time, all three are dead. Total thrall contribution across 3 rounds: 10 damage (2 hits in R1), before being wiped. The Compel Thrall bonus action becomes dead air from R2 onward. The Manifest Thralls lair action has no targets from R2. The "escort" pressure the slice is meant to test evaporates in the party's very first action.
- **Recommendation A (preferred):** Increase thrall HP to 28–30. At 28 HP, Fireball (22 avg fail) leaves a thrall at 6 HP — wounded but alive, still actionable, continues applying melee pressure for 2–3 rounds before dying to chip damage.
- **Recommendation B:** Add +2 Con to thralls (HP to ~26, +Dex save +1) — gives Fireball a realistic chance of leaving at least one thrall at half HP.
- **Recommendation C:** Stagger starting positions: 2 thralls at 25–30 ft, 1 thrall at 55+ ft (held in reserve, approaches via Compel Thrall). Fireball can hit at most 2; the third arrives as a follow-up threat after the party has committed their opener. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### FI-TW2-A: tank-wall — Call Weakness range-freeze when Rager wins initiative (2026-06-06T10)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 10:22 UTC, slice #2 cycle 31. Seed 494650. VICTORY R3.
- **Finding:** CW range is 30 ft. Once the Rager charges to melee (~40 ft from SC's back-line position), the Rager is 50-55 ft from the SC — well out of CW range. In this run, SC and Rager tied initiative (both 11); SC won the tie by Dex and CW'd the Rager successfully. But on any run where Rager wins initiative (or PCs act before SC force-advancing the Rager into the line), CW is unavailable for the entire fight. Over repeated cycles, the CW-on-Rager synergy — the core tactical fantasy of this formation — is initiative-lottery dependent. This is the first confirmed tank-wall cycle; at ~50% initiative odds the synergy is structurally unreliable.
- **Recommendation:** Either extend CW range to 45 ft, OR redesign CW as a triggered reaction ("when an ally you can see within 45 ft declares a charge/Dash, they gain advantage on their next attack roll this turn") — this fires independently of initiative order. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### FI-TW2-B: tank-wall — Rager Berserk never fires due to 40 ft start + 52 HP (2026-06-06T10)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 10:22 UTC, slice #2 cycle 31. Seed 494650. VICTORY R3.
- **Finding:** Starting at 40 ft (>30 ft Rager speed), the Rager must Dash in R1 — forfeiting its attack. It enters R2 at full HP (52) but by then Sabriel's R1 smite (20 dmg) has already hit. Bazgar's R2 Action Surge (4 attacks, ~28 dmg) brings the Rager from 22 HP to 0 before its R2 turn. Berserk — the Rager's flagship area attack requiring 2+ enemies in reach — never fires. This is a structural kill-path issue: the L5 fighter's Action Surge is reliably faster than the Rager's damage output can force a retreat. Berserk is theoretically interesting (stand-still AoE swing) but requires the Rager to survive 2 rounds of focused fire.
- **Recommendation A:** Reduce tank-wall starting distance to 20-25 ft. Rager reaches melee R1 without Dashing, fires Multiattack R1, enters R2 with HP to spare.
- **Recommendation B:** Increase Rager HP to 65 (from 52). CR stays at 2 (HP ceiling for CR 2 is ~70). Berserk reliably fires R2. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### FI-TW2-C: tank-wall — Shard-Throw long-range disadvantage absent from DB spec (2026-06-06T10)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 10:22 UTC, slice #2 cycle 31. Seed 494650. VICTORY R3.
- **Finding:** Shardcaller fired Shard-Throw at Marwen (60 ft — beyond 30 ft short range). Per 5e RAW, ranged weapon attacks beyond short range impose disadvantage. The DB output shows flat to-hit rolls (no disadvantage). In this run the throws missed naturally (to-hits 11, 12 vs AC 15), so no incorrect hits, but the bug exists. Previous shardcaller cycles (TP27, SC33) were at 30 ft or less — this is the first confirmed long-range engagement.
- **Recommendation:** Short term: add a line to SC checklist: "Beyond 30 ft (long range): Shard-Throw has disadvantage — DM rolls d20+4 twice and takes the lower." Long term: the DB spec needs a `long_range_disadvantage` flag or `short_range_ft` field. **Mechanical question/tracker item — borderline blast radius (DB spec change).**

### FI-TW2-D: tank-wall — Barrage positioning axis prevents area threat while Rager alive (2026-06-06T10)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 10:22 UTC, slice #2 cycle 31. Seed 494650. VICTORY R3.
- **Finding:** Shard Barrage is a 15-ft LINE from the SC's position. Once the Rager is in front-line melee (blocking the arch 5 ft from PCs), the SC at 55-60 ft behind cannot fire the Barrage through the party (too far — line originates at SC) and cannot fire it forward along the corridor axis (Rager is in the line, would take friendly damage). Barrage only became available as a late-fight panic shot after the Rager fell (SC at 4 HP, R3 recharge). DM guidance note in SC tactics recommends firing Barrage R1 down the corridor before the Rager reaches melee — this is correct but only documented for the corridor threshold-patrol scenario. For tank-wall, the .md should specify: "Set up SC directly behind Rager in corridor; fire Barrage R1 down the axis at approaching PCs before the Rager closes. Once Rager is in melee, hold Barrage until Rager falls or reposition." **Authoring/tactics note — within blast radius but flagging for human review due to balance implications.**

### FI-TW2-E: tank-wall — CW range structural dead zone confirmed 2nd cycle (2026-06-06T18)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 18:20 UTC, slice #2 2nd tank-wall cycle. Seed 494658. VICTORY R2.
- **Finding:** Confirms and extends FI-TW2-A. With SC at 65ft (setup error: should be 40ft per .md) and Rager charging to melee, CW's 30ft range is 35ft short. CW held all 3/3 charges — 0/3 spent the entire fight. Even at the .md-correct 40ft starting position, the SC→Rager distance after the Rager charges is still ~55ft (40ft SC start + 15ft Rager start = 55ft gap). CW range of 30ft cannot bridge a 55ft post-charge gap in any valid starting setup where the SC is "40-60ft from enemies." The dead zone is not an edge case — it is structural to any formation that follows both positioning rules simultaneously.
- **Recommendation (updated from FI-TW2-A):** Increase CW range to 60ft (matching Shard-Throw range). This allows the SC to buff the Rager from its back-line position at any point during the fight, not just during the brief R1 window before the Rager charges. **Human design decision.**

### FI-TW2-F: tank-wall — playtest slice goal unachieved in both cycles (2026-06-06T18)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 18:20 UTC. 2nd confirmed tank-wall cycle. Both cycle 31 (seed 494650) and cycle 2 (seed 494658) reviewed.
- **Finding:** The slice description states "Tests taunt + call_weakness pairing." In both cycles: Taunt either failed or was inert; CW was range-locked out. The playtest slice cannot test what it claims to test given current formation geometry. The CW+Taunt interaction — "SC buffs Rager's attack against a taunted PC" — requires simultaneous conditions that the current positioning rules prevent from co-occurring: Rager must be within 30ft of SC (CW range), and within melee of a PC, and the PC must be within 30ft of the Rager (Taunt range). In a 60ft corridor with standard positioning, the Rager closes to melee before the SC can CW it, or the SC is too far back to CW once the Rager is in melee.
- **Recommendation:** Before running another tank-wall cycle, update the slice starting positions: Rager 10ft, SC 30ft. This keeps SC in CW range (30ft from Rager) and allows Barrage axis before Rager closes. At these distances, the CW+Taunt synergy can actually be tested. Alternatively, document in the slice rotation that the current geometry is unworkable for the stated test goal. **Human decision.**

### MQ-TW2-A: IDA + Madness Endurance stacking — confirm intended (2026-06-06T18)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 18:20 UTC, slice #2 2nd cycle. R2, Rager's turn.
- **Question:** Rager took damage from both Bazgar (crit, R1) and Sabriel (smite, R1). Per its .md: Incoming Damage Aggro gives advantage on next attack vs whoever hit it; Madness Endurance gives +1 to attack rolls after taking damage. In the sim, both applied simultaneously to R2 Swing 1 (advantage + flat +1 bonus). Is this intended? The two traits have different trigger conditions and different riders — stacking them seems correct by 5e rules (independent passive traits), but the combined effect (advantage + +1 to-hit) on a freshly-damaged Rager creates a noticeably punishing R2 opening. Confirm in .md: "IDA and ME are independent traits that may apply simultaneously."

### MQ-TW2-B: Taunt "end of target's next turn" uptime vs initiative position (2026-06-06T18)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 18:20 UTC. Taunt failed both rolls in this run, so this is hypothetical.
- **Question:** If Taunt lands on Rager's turn (init 18) against Bazgar (init 1), the effect lasts "until end of Bazgar's next turn." Bazgar acts later in the same round (init 1), clearing the taunt at end of his own R1 turn. The Rager would taunt Bazgar R1, then Bazgar acts R1 with the taunt active (can't attack non-rager without disadv), then the taunt expires. This gives approximately 0 additional rounds of coverage. In the other case (Bazgar init > Rager init), Bazgar acts before the Rager and is never taunted until after his R1 attacks. Taunt effectively gives 0–1 rounds of coverage depending on initiative. The .md should clarify: "Taunt expires at end of the target's NEXT turn after the save — if the target acts in the same round as the Rager (after the Rager in initiative), that is the target's 'next turn' and the taunt expires then."

### FI-SW30-A: shrine-wedge — slice too lightweight, showcase mechanics invisible 30th cycle (2026-06-06T09)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 09:18 UTC, slice #1 cycle 30. Seed 494649. VICTORY R2.
- **Finding:** 2× shrine-touched-derro (45 HP each) die in Round 2 against the level-5 party. Over 30 cycles, the fight consistently ends before Ancient Resonance can rotate or Unstable Form can express. This cycle: STD-A recharged AR in R2 SOT and had UF active, but died before its turn (Bazgar landed 10 dmg, then OBR self-damage 4 put it at 0). STD-B had UF active and AR still available, but Sabriel's smite (21 dmg) killed it before it could act. The slice is designed to test the resonance recharge loop and the UF-vs-AR priority decision — but neither mechanic gets screen time.
- **Recommendation:** Add 1× thrall-derro to the slice as a speed-bump (absorbs 1–2 R1 PC actions, keeping STDs alive to R3+). Alternative: boost STD HP from 45 to 55–60 HP. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### FI-SW30-B: shrine-wedge — altar zone suppresses both fire + radiant, DM announcement needed (2026-06-06T09)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 09:18 UTC, slice #1 cycle 30. Seed 494649. VICTORY R2.
- **Finding:** Fireball dealt 12/24 (save/fail) instead of 24/48. Sabriel's Divine Smite dealt 9 radiant instead of up to 18. Both the party's primary damage vectors are neutered by the altar zone. The zone rule is working correctly per the spec. However, in practice the DM needs to announce "altar zone active — fire and radiant vulnerability suppressed" at combat start, or the party will assume their opener performed below average when it actually hit the zone wall. 30 consecutive cycles of this suppression without a dedicated DM callout note.
- **Recommendation:** Add one line to `COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET.md` under Shrine-Touched Derro: "*Altar zone (60 ft from altar): fire & radiant vulnerability suppressed — announce at start of combat.*" **Minor authoring note; within blast radius.**

### FI-SW30-C: shrine-wedge — Unstable Form never expressed in 30 cycles (2026-06-06T09)

- **Context:** 2026-06-06 09:18 UTC, slice #1 cycle 30. Seed 494649. VICTORY R2.
- **Finding:** UF requires taking ≥10 damage in a single turn, then grants advantage on all attacks until end of next turn. In 30 cycles, UF has been triggered but never used in a meaningful way because STDs die between the damage event and their next turn. The mechanic only expresses if an STD survives a round of heavy PC fire AND is still alive when its next turn comes. In a 2-round fight, this is structurally impossible.
- **This is tied to FI-SW30-A.** Fixing the slice speed (adding a speed-bump NPC or more HP) would automatically give UF room to express. No separate fix needed. **Tracking note only.**

### FI-TP27-A: threshold-patrol — SC Barrage R2+ re-fire decision unspecified (2026-06-05T22)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 22:19 UTC, slice #0 cycle 27. Seed 494638. VICTORY R4.
- **Finding:** SC recharged Shard-Barrage on R2 (d6=5). The tactics section says "default to Barrage R1 rather than waiting for a 3-PC cluster" and "In a narrow corridor (≤30 ft wide), PCs advancing in formation are almost always in a 15-ft line." However, there is no explicit instruction for what to do when Barrage recharges in R2 or later while PCs are still advancing in the corridor. The sim chose multiattack instead. With 2 PCs in the corridor at ~30 ft range in R2, a second Barrage would have been tactically correct and more damaging.
- **Recommendation:** Add a line to derro-shardcaller.md Tactics: "If Barrage recharges in R2+ and 2+ PCs are still within the 15-ft line (corridor fight still active), re-fire — the corridor threat does not expire in R1." **Within blast radius; minor tactics documentation.**

### FI-TP27-C: threshold-patrol — CW zero-conversion 27th-cycle structural confirm (2026-06-05T22)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 22:19 UTC, slice #0 cycle 27. Seed 494638. VICTORY R4.
- **Finding:** SC fired CW on DW-1 correctly per MQ-12 (DW-1 HP 27 > 20, already acted, advantage deferred to R2). In R2, DW-1 attacked Bazgar with CW advantage — rolled d20s of 1 and 6 (+2 = 3 and 8, both miss vs AC 18). 27 consecutive threshold-patrol cycles with zero HP-converting CW activations (exception: cycle 23 where SC had high init and CW fired before DW-1 acted). The structural issues are compounding: (a) SC acts after DW-1 and DW-2 due to initiative tie-breaking, so CW advantage is always deferred; (b) DW-1/DW-2 have only +2 to-hit vs AC 18 (25% base rate); (c) fights are short enough (R2-4) that deferred CW rarely survives.
- **Recommendation:** Human review needed. Options: (1) increase patrol spacing so SC acts before one DW (SC Dex +2 vs DW Dex +2 — both at +2, so init tie is coinflip); (2) reduce DW's starting position to 20–25 ft so SC-DW range is within 30 ft and CW can be cast before both DWs act when SC wins a tie; (3) reduce CW HP guard from ≤20 to ≤10 for this formation; (4) accept CW as irrelevant in slice 0 and rely on it for slices 1–4 with mixed formations. **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### FI-EV7-26-A: empty-void — Lair UG + beholder VS R1 timing synergy (2026-06-05T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 21:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 26. Seed 494637. Corrected TPK R3.
- **Finding:** Beholder initiative was 22; lair fires at init 20. Lair targeted Marwen with Unstable Ground (prone, save failed 11 vs DC16). Two ticks later, beholder fired Void Scream — Marwen saved with disadvantage from shrine proximity (NOT from prone; prone doesn't affect spell saves). The 2-tick gap means the beholder almost always acts immediately after its own lair setup when it has high initiative. This feels like the beholder is "gaming" the turn order even though both events are mechanically separate.
- **Note:** This is feel, not a bug. At low beholder initiative (≤12), PCs act between lair and beholder, creating counterplay. The synergy is only present when beholder rolls high init. Consider adding a tactics note: "If beholder has init > 15, lair actions naturally set up beholder attacks (no action needed); if beholder has init ≤ 12, lair actions are delayed setup with PC counterplay possible."
- **Recommendation:** Documentation note only. No spec change. **Minor authoring note; within blast radius.**

### FI-EV7-26-B: empty-void — Bazgar resistance wall, 26th-cycle confirm (2026-06-05T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 21:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 26. Seed 494637. Corrected TPK R3.
- **Finding:** Bazgar used Action Surge in R1, landed 2 hits, dealt 9 total after resistance (nonmagic B/P/S halved). Effective DPR vs beholder: ~4.5/hit, ~9/round (Action Surge). Over 4 rounds without surge: ~9 DPR vs 110 HP beholder. Fighter is contributing <10% of beholder's HP per round and cannot meaningfully contribute without magical or sanctified weapons.
- **Compounding factor:** The Compass Edge roster (`world/party/the-compass-edge/combat-roster.yml`) doesn't specify magical weapons. If this is intentional (party shouldn't be able to damage the beholder easily), the design is working. If it's an oversight, the party needs either (a) a +1 or silvered weapon for Bazgar, or (b) Sabriel to be the primary melee damage vector (radiant smites bypass resistance).
- **Recommendation:** Human decision needed. Add a note to the combat roster YAML or the encounter design doc about whether the party has magical weapons. If not, add a tactics note to the beholder .md: "Bazgar's nonmagic weapons are halved — Sabriel (radiant smites) is the melee threat; prioritize Sabriel for tentacle grapple." **Human design decision — out of blast radius.**

### FI-EV7-26-C: empty-void — VS average auto-kills Marwen (50% swing) (2026-06-05T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 21:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 26. Seed 494637.
- **Finding:** 6d10 VS average = 33 psychic. Marwen's HP = 32. On average fail, VS kills Marwen outright (33 > 32). Dispatch rolled 20 this cycle (below average), leaving Marwen at 12 HP — alive and the fight continued. The high variance (6d10 range 6–60) means VS produces a 50/50 "caster survives" outcome each time it fires. Above-average rolls (>32) = caster dead. Below-average (≤32) = caster alive and Sabriel can LoH.
- **Impact:** The "does Marwen die to VS" question dominates the empty-void arc. There's no counterplay — it's a straight Wis save against a DC that PCs at level 5 can only pass ~35% of the time (WIS +1 = DC16, need 15+). The high variance in damage compounds the already high-stakes save.
- **Recommendation:** Consider whether VS should be capped at 32 (just under Marwen's HP), or whether the instant-kill is intended as the dramatic "wizard down" beat. If intended: no change. If it's creating frustrating all-or-nothing spikes: reduce to 5d10 (avg 27.5, below Marwen's HP). **Human design decision.**

### FI-EV7-26-D: empty-void — DD temp HP negligible, 26th-cycle confirm (2026-06-05T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 21:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 26. Seed 494637.
- **Finding:** Drain Divinity (3 LA cost) drained Sabriel's L3 and granted beholder 6 temp HP. At 87+ HP beholder, the temp HP is mathematical noise. 26th-cycle confirm of this pattern (see FI-EV7-25-D, FI-EV7-24-E). The DD mechanic is correctly valued for slot attrition — but the temp HP line in the narration and spec may confuse DMs into thinking the beholder gains something meaningful. In practice, the strategic value is entirely "Sabriel loses a 4d8 smite."
- **Recommendation:** Add a DM note to the beholder.md Drain Divinity description: "The temp HP is incidental; the real strategic value of DD is denying Sabriel's L2/L3 smite slots. Use DD early (R1–R2) when the slot pool is full; after L2+ slots are exhausted, DD's value drops sharply." This is a DM coaching note, not a spec change. **Within blast radius; minor .md edit.**

### MQ-EV7-26-A: empty-void — dual-dice gap (VS dispatch=20 vs sim=41) (2026-06-05T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 21:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 26. Corrected TPK R3.
- **Finding:** VS dispatch output stated 20 psychic; simulation independently rolled 41 via `random.randint`. Sim used 41 → false TPK R2. Corrected: Marwen survives VS at 12 HP, fight continues. Same class gap as SIM-TW30-B, SIM-SCT-24-A, SIM-SW28-A (all dual-dice gap entries in prior cycles).
- **Recommendation:** The fix is a harness change: parse the damage total from the dispatch output string and use it for HP tracking, rather than re-rolling. Or add a `damage_rolled` field to the dispatch JSON schema. This is a medium-effort improvement with high accuracy payoff. **Out of blast radius — harness change for human to decide.**

### MQ-EV7-26-B: empty-void — VS shrine-proximity disadvantage applies to which PCs? (2026-06-05T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 21:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 26.
- **Finding:** The beholder .md states "Creatures within 10 ft of the shrine have disadvantage on this save." Sim applied this to all 3 PCs (assuming all within 10 ft). But the vault is described as "vast" (main vault with columns, altar at center, entrance from shaft). PCs at the threshold/entrance are likely 40–60 ft from the altar, well outside 10 ft. Only PCs who have advanced to the shrine would qualify.
- **Impact:** With disadvantage, Marwen's save (WIS+1 vs DC16, needs 15+) succeeds ~11% vs ~25% without disadvantage. The shrine-proximity clause is a significant modifier that may not apply to most combatants at round 1 unless they've already closed to the altar.
- **Recommendation:** Add a combat starting position note to beholder.md or _overview.md: "PCs at the vault entrance are ~40–50 ft from the shrine; they do NOT start with shrine-proximity disadvantage on VS unless they've advanced. PCs who approach the altar (within 10 ft) for melee gain the disadvantage." **Minor authoring note; within blast radius.**

### FI-SW29-A: shrine-wedge — AR stagger + cone requirement creates 0-AR R1 + double-AR R2 nova pattern (NEW) (2026-06-05T13)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 13:21 UTC, slice #1 cycle 29. Seed 494629. VICTORY R4. Both STDs survived R1 intact with AR ready.
- **Finding:** In R1, STD1 had only Sabriel in its 15-ft cone — AR requires 2+, so STD1 could not fire. STD2 was stagger-held. Neither STD fired AR in R1. Both entered R2 with AR available (STD1 recharged R2 start). R2: STD1 AR (Sabriel/Bazgar both failed: 17 damage each), then STD2 AR (both saved: 2 damage each). The combined double-AR nova in R2 dropped Sabriel from 37 → 20 HP and Bazgar from 49 → 30 HP in a single round after a quiet R1. On a bad-roll run, if STD2's save target had also been 15+ necrotic (average), both PCs would have taken 34 total across the two ARs. This is the maximum-pressure variant of the stagger mechanism.
- **Distinction from prior cycles:** In cycles 22–28, at least one STD typically died in R1 (from Action Surge / Fireball) or fired AR R1 (when 2+ targets in cone immediately). This cycle's quiet R1 + double-AR R2 is a distinct dangerous configuration that hasn't been captured before.
- **Recommendation:** Consider whether AR should have a single-target fallback (e.g., fire at reduced damage, 1d10 necrotic, no psychic rider) when cone is <2. This would prevent the "dead pressure round" where STD1 has AR ready but cannot fire due to spread. Alternatively, add a note that this 0-AR-R1 pattern is a known balance lever — the party can spread to prevent AR firing, creating an intentional tactical game. **Human design decision.**

### FI-SW29-B: shrine-wedge — Altar zone fire/radiant vulnerability suppression (29th-cycle confirm) (2026-06-05T13)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 13:21 UTC, slice #1 cycle 29. Seed 494629. VICTORY R4.
- **Finding:** Sabriel's L1 Divine Smite dealt 9 radiant (normal); outside the altar zone vs an STD, this would be 18 (doubled). Marwen's Scorching Rays dealt normal fire throughout. The suppression meaningfully shaped Marwen's spell choice (SR over Fireball) and reduced Sabriel's offensive output by ~50% per smite.
- **Problem:** The DB outputs no reminder that vulnerability is suppressed. DM must track this manually and proactively announce it. A table DM who forgets will accidentally double fire/radiant damage, creating wildly inconsistent outcomes.
- **Recommendation:** Add a line to `COMBAT-CHEAT-SHEET.md` as a pre-combat reminder: "Altar zone (60 ft): STD fire+radiant vulnerability SUPPRESSED — normal damage for Fireball/SR/smite." 29th cycle — this is chronic. The cheat sheet is the right place for it. **Minor documentation fix; within blast radius.**

### FI-SW29-C: shrine-wedge — Stagger-hold persists when higher-init STD cannot fire AR (NEW) (2026-06-05T13)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 13:21 UTC, slice #1 cycle 29. Seed 494629. VICTORY R4.
- **Finding:** The stagger rule states "check both STDs at start of round; if both have AR recharged, mark lower-initiative STD as HOLD-AR." In R1, STD1 had AR but could not fire (cone < 2 targets). STD2 remained held. The hold is evaluated on availability, not on whether the higher-init STD actually fires. This is implied but not stated explicitly in the .md.
- **Impact:** When the higher-init STD has AR but the cone geometry prevents firing, the lower-init STD is penalized for the higher-init's positioning failure. Combined with the "both recharged" configuration entering R2, this creates the maximum-nova scenario (double-AR in same round).
- **Recommendation (minor text fix):** Add one sentence to stagger rule: "The stagger hold persists regardless of whether the higher-initiative STD actually fires AR on its turn — it is evaluated at the start of the round based on AR availability, not on execution." No balance change. **Within blast radius — can fix inline.**

### MQ-SW29-A: shrine-wedge — Stagger hold when higher-init STD cannot fire AR (2026-06-05T13)

- **Context:** Same as FI-SW29-C. Is "stagger hold persists even if higher-init cannot fire" the intended ruling?
- **Current .md:** "if two shrine-touched both have Resonance available in the same round, only the one acting FIRST in initiative fires." The phrase "fires" is ambiguous — does it mean "may fire" or "fires and therefore blocks"?
- **Finding:** If "fires" means "fires regardless of cone," then the stagger hold is absolute (STD1 fires into 1 target or holds, STD2 holds). If "fires" means "fires when possible," then the hold should release when STD1 can't fire.
- **Recommendation:** Confirm intent. If stagger is availability-based (recommended — simpler), add the clarifying sentence from FI-SW29-C. If it's execution-based, add: "If the higher-initiative STD cannot fire AR this turn (no valid cone target), the lower-initiative STD's hold releases and it may fire AR at its normal opportunity."

### FI-TP26-A: threshold-patrol — Fireball trivializes DWD formation (26th-cycle confirm) (2026-06-05T12)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 12:17 UTC, slice #0 cycle 26. Seed 494628. VICTORY R3. Marwen init 19 (highest), opens with Fireball.
- **Finding:** Both DWDs have HP 27 and AC 15 with Dex +4. A DC 14 Fireball deals avg ~19.5 on a failed save. DWD1 took 29 (fail), DWD2 took 22 (fail) — both wiped or crippled in one action. In 26 cycles, the threshold-patrol encounter has never survived past R2 when Marwen opens with Fireball. The encounter is designed as a warm-up/resource-drain encounter, not a lethal one — so the rapid clear may be intentional. However, if the design goal is "DWDs feel like a genuine threat and drain 1–2 resources," the current formation (DWDs clustered at the chokepoint) makes them trivial AoE fodder.
- **Recommendation:** Decision needed — is the fast-clear outcome correct for this encounter's narrative role (first contact, wrong question), or should DWDs have a spread formation (30+ ft apart) that forces the party to choose between AoE on one and single-target on the other? Spread would also better reflect Deep Watch tactical doctrine per the .md. **Human design decision.**

### FI-TP26-B: threshold-patrol — CW structurally wasted in this slice (26th-cycle structural confirm) (2026-06-05T12)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 12:17 UTC, slice #0 cycle 26. Seed 494628. SC has CW 3/day. DWD1 died before SC acted (R1 Fireball). Per MQ-66, SC should redirect CW to DWD2, but DWD2 has only 5 HP — CW advantage on a 5-HP creature is near-worthless (one hit kills it regardless). When Fireball wipes both DWDs, SC has no eligible CW target at all. In 26 cycles, CW has fired usefully in threshold-patrol 0 times.
- **Finding:** CW is structurally blocked in this slice because: (1) Fireball wipes or cripples DWDs before SC acts; (2) even when SC acts before Marwen, Barrage R1 must take the action, holding CW for a next-highest ally that's already dead by R2. CW's design shines in formations where melee frontline allies survive long enough to benefit (tank-wall, final-confrontation). In threshold-patrol, CW is wasted overhead every cycle. This is a formation-level feel issue — the SC's most interesting ability never fires.
- **Recommendation:** Accept as structural (CW is deliberately weak in this 3-NPC patrol; it shines in later slices) OR give the SC a fall-through tactic: "If CW has no eligible ally, use CW on yourself to grant advantage on next Multiattack." Self-targeting CW is a minor rules extension but would visibly activate the ability. **Human design decision.**

### FI-TP26-C: threshold-patrol — Tactical Retreat never fires (structural, low priority) (2026-06-05T12)

- **Context:** In 26 cycles, TR has fired correctly in solo-endgame (FI-TP24-D) but never meaningfully changed the encounter outcome. With SC at init 10 and Bazgar at init 8, TR fires (correctly per MQ-73) only when Bazgar is already within 15ft at the *start* of SC's turn. In short 2-3 round clears, SC dies before Bazgar can close to 15ft.
- **Finding:** Low-severity feel note. TR is mechanically correct; it just rarely activates in short patrol encounters. No fix needed unless the encounter is redesigned to run longer.
- **Recommendation:** No action needed — confirmed low-priority per 26 cycles of evidence.

### FI-EV7-25-C: empty-void — no-thrall lair rotation is deterministic and feels mechanical (2026-06-05T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 11:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 25. Seed 494627. VICTORY R3(sim)/R4(corrected). Beholder solo — no thralls.
- **Finding:** With `manifest_thralls` unavailable, the lair action pool shrinks to 2 options (VE and UG). The rotation rule (max 2 consecutive same action) then produces an automatic VE→VE→UG→VE→VE→UG pattern. The DM has no real decision to make — the lair choice is deterministic every round. In 3-round fights this means 2× VE + 1× UG, which is reasonable but mechanically hollow.
- **Recommendation:** Consider adding a 3rd lair option for solo/no-thrall beholder configurations. Option A: "void lure" — one PC in 60 ft must succeed DC 16 Wis save or be pulled 20 ft toward the altar (repositioning hazard, forces melee engagement). Option B: "echo of thralls" — manifests 1 illusory thrall that acts as difficult terrain and draws OAs but has 1 HP. Either would restore decision-making depth to the lair phase in this slice. **Human review needed before implementation.**

### FI-EV7-25-A: empty-void — surprise-DR-miss swings entire fight narrative (25th-cycle confirm) (2026-06-05T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 11:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 25. Surprise round DR: d20=2+6=8 vs AC 15 → MISS (70% normal hit rate → 30% miss). When DR misses, Marwen survives to cast Fireball ×2. When DR hits, Marwen is dust before acting. The variance delta on this single roll shapes the entire fight.
- **Finding:** The 25th cycle (Marwen survived) shows a dramatically different fight from the 1st cycle (Marwen disintegrated). Both are mechanically valid, but the "negotiation → surprise DR" transition design creates an extreme high-variance opening. A 30% chance the fight's hardest hitter (Marwen) survives the first action; a 70% chance she's gone before the first round.
- **Recommendation:** Consider whether the negotiation-to-combat transition should give the party a "perception of danger" beat before the surprise round fires (e.g., "the beholder orients, you have one action" — no initiative, but party gets one reactive decision). This would reduce the pure-luck feel of the surprise DR miss/hit variance while keeping the dramatic hard-trigger mechanic. **Human design decision — do not auto-fix.**

### MQ-EV7-25-A: empty-void — DD vs LoH-TL priority: clarification added but needs confirmation (2026-06-05T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 11:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 25. Seed 494627. Sabriel at 14 HP (< 30 HP threshold) used LoH on Marwen in R1. Per FI-125 (LoH counter): TL at end of Sabriel's turn. Per legendary priority order: DD fires first on first PC's end-of-turn. Conflict.
- **Finding:** The two tactic sections are in conflict when both conditions apply simultaneously. FIX-EV7-25-B was added to the .md to cross-reference and give TL priority under this condition. Net effect: 1 LA TL on Sabriel → 2 LA VR on next PC's turn, vs 3 LA DD on Sabriel. The TL approach yields ~7 damage + pool pressure; the DD approach yields +4–6 tmp HP + slot drain. Both have merit.
- **Recommendation:** Human to confirm FIX-EV7-25-B interpretation: "LoH-counter TL fires before DD when Sabriel < 30 HP and just used LoH." If correct, the .md is now consistent. If DD should still fire first, revert FIX-EV7-25-B and update FI-125 to clarify "override applies only if Sabriel used LoH 2+ consecutive turns."

### MQ-EV7-25-B: empty-void — DR recharge wording "5–6" causes DM misread (2026-06-05T11)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 11:20 UTC, slice #7 cycle 25. SIM-EV7-25-D: sim recharge check for DR treated d6=6 as "still USED" due to a > vs >= comparison bug. The beholder's start-of-turn checklist says "recovers on 5–6" but doesn't explicitly say "5 and 6 both recover."
- **Finding:** DMs may read "recovers on 5–6" as "only on a 6" (confusing it with VS which recovers on 6 only). This is a real ambiguity in the checklist wording.
- **Recommendation (auto-fixable coaching note):** Add to the checklist item: "DR: recovers on 5 or 6 (both recover, not just 6); VS: recovers on 6 only." This makes the two abilities' different recharge probabilities explicit side-by-side. **Low-risk auto-fix — implementing now.**

### FI-SCT-24-A: shardcaller-team — CW-stagger-deadlock structural confirm (cycle 24) (2026-06-05T10)

- **Context:** 2026-06-05 10:19 UTC, slice #6 cycle 24. Seed 494626. VICTORY R3. All 3 SCs entered with Barrage available.
- **Finding:** When all 3 SCs have Barrage available in R1 and the stagger rule applies (only 1 Barrage per round), there is no eligible CW target. The CW rule says "don't CW an ally who plans to Barrage." With all 3 holding Barrage, every ally "plans to Barrage" — the CW targeting loop finds no candidate. In practice the stagger assigns ONLY the first-acting SC to fire Barrage, making the other two multiattackers — but that decision is implicit, not encoded. CW 0/9 is confirmed for cycle 24 (0% use rate across 24 shardcaller-team cycles in open vault). The tactics .md does say "the two non-barrage SCs SHOULD CW each other" but this only fires if the stagger decision is resolved before CW targeting.
- **Recommendation:** Add an explicit stagger-priority rule: "R1 (all three have Barrage): the SC with the LOWEST initiative fires Barrage; the two with higher initiative multiattack and CW each other." This resolves the deadlock and gives CW something to do in R1 of the shardcaller-team slice. No spec/DB change needed — .md tactics edit only.

### FI-SCT-24-B: shardcaller-team — Shard-Barrage range ambiguity: caster-origin vs placeable line (2026-06-05T10)

- **Context:** DB spec `"area": "15-ft line"` with no range field. Tactics .md uses "15-ft line" in a way that implies the SC fires it into a formation of PCs advancing toward them (R1 in corridor: PCs 30+ ft away). If the line originates from the caster and extends 15 ft, PCs at 30+ ft are out of range. If the line is placeable at range (like many 5e line spells with a point of origin), it would work as a ranged area attack.
- **Mechanical conflict identified:** Tactical Retreat fires when threats close to 15 ft (start of SC's own turn). If Barrage is caster-origin (15 ft), TR and Barrage are mutually exclusive every round — TR fires when PCs reach 15 ft, pushing SC back 30 ft, at which point PCs are outside Barrage range. The ability would effectively never fire in normal play.
- **Recommendation:** Add one line to the shard_barrage DB spec: `"range": "30 ft"` (or 30/60 to match Shard-Throw short/long range) to indicate the line is placed at a point within 30 ft. This resolves the TR+Barrage conflict and matches the corridor usage described in tactics. **Do not auto-fix — needs human confirmation before DB edit.**

### FI-SCT-24-C: shardcaller-team — PTV zero-effect in open-vault 24th cycle; clarification needed (2026-06-05T10)

- **Context:** PTV (Pack Tactics Voice passive) primed once in cycle 24: SC-C Shard-Throw hit Marwen R2 → Marwen has disadv on next save. SC-C then fired Barrage R3 targeting Bazgar + Sabriel in melee (not Marwen). PTV consumed without effect.
- **Finding:** PTV requires the same PC to be hit by an SC (priming) then subject to a save from an SC (consuming). In open-vault fights where SCs switch targets freely, this chain rarely aligns. PTV has activated with game-visible effect only once in 24 cycles (corridor-22nd). Two clarifications would improve table enforcement: (1) "the *next* saving throw" — confirm this means vs ANY source (not just SC abilities), which would make PTV more useful since party member spells, terrain hazards, etc. could consume it; (2) confirm whether the SC's own hit triggers PTV ("creature is not its own ally" 5e ambiguity, flagged in MQ-TP23-A and still open).
- **Recommendation:** Add a one-line clarification to the PTV checklist: "Disadv applies to the next saving throw the target makes, from ANY source, before end of its next turn."

### FI-TW29-A: tank-wall — CW fires AND collects for confirmed first time in this formation (2026-06-04T21)

- **Context:** 2026-06-04 21:20 UTC, slice #2 29th cycle. Seed 494613. VICTORY R2. Init order: Rager(7) > Bazgar(6) > SC(5) > Marwen(4) > Sabriel(3).
- **Finding (POSITIVE):** SC granted CW to Rager at SC's R1 turn when Rager had 44 HP (MQ-12 guard: >20 HP, fires). CW advantage collected in R2 swing 1 (advantaged hit 23 vs AC 18 for 12 damage — Rager's best hit of the fight). This is the first confirmed cycle where CW both fires AND is collected in the tank-wall slice. Prior 28 cycles: 1 cycle fired but was wasted (Berserk expected, CW granted after Berserk already used), remainder blocked by range or ≤20 HP guard. The mechanic works; it requires the SC to have lower initiative than the Rager so the CW carries over to R2.
- **Implication for earlier FI-TW28-A finding:** The structural init-block is real but not absolute. When the Rager has >20 HP at time of SC's turn (Fireball hasn't landed yet), MQ-12 allows CW on an already-acted ally and collects next round. The effective window is narrow: if the Rager takes 30+ damage before SC acts in R1 (possible if Bazgar hits and Rager started at 52), MQ-12 fires and CW is held. In this run, the Rager survived with 44 HP through Bazgar's turn.
